*http.Request) {\\n392: \\tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\\n393: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\\n394: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Failed to parse request\\\\n\\\"))\\n395: \\t\\treturn\\n396: \\t}\\n397: \\tif req.Form.Get(\\\"clear\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n398: \\t\\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\\n399: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Cache cleared\\\\n\\\"))\\n400: \\t\\treturn\\n401: \\t}\\n402: \\tif req.Form.Get(\\\"sizes\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n403: \\t\\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\\n404: \\t\\traw := make(map[string]int, len(snapshot))\\n405: \\t\\ttotalSize := 0\\n406: \\t\\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\\n407: \\t\\t\\tif resource == nil {\\n408: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n409: \\t\\t\\t}\\n410: \\t\\t\\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\\n411: \\t\\t\\tsz := len(resource.Resource.GetValue())\\n412: \\t\\t\\traw[resourceType] += sz\\n413: \\t\\t\\ttotalSize += sz\\n414: \\t\\t}\\n415: \\t\\tres := make(map[string]string, len(raw))\\n416: \\t\\tfor k, v := range raw {\\n417: \\t\\t\\tres[k] = util.ByteCount(v)\\n418: \\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\tres[\\\"total\\\"] = util.ByteCount(totalSize)\\n420: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, res, req)\\n421: \\t\\treturn\\n422: \\t}\\n423: \\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\\n424: \\tresources := make(map[string][]string, len(snapshot)) // Key is typeUrl and value is resource names.\\n425: \\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\\n426: \\t\\tif resource == nil {\\n427: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n428: \\t\\t}\\n429: \\t\\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\\n430: \\t\\tresources[resourceType] = append(resources[resourceType], resource.Name)\\n431: \\t}\\n432: \\twriteJSON(w, resources, req)\\n433: }\\n434: \\n435: // kubernetesConfig wraps a config.Config with a custom marshaling method that matches a Kubernetes\\n436: // object structure.\\n437: type kubernetesConfig struct {\\n438: \\tconfig.Config\\n439: }\\n440: \\n441: func (k kubernetesConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\\n442: \\tcfg, err := crd.ConvertConfig(k.Config)\\n443: \\tif err != nil {\\n444: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n445: \\t}\\n446: \\treturn json.Marshal(cfg)\\n447: }\\n448: \\n449: // Config debugging.\\n450: func (s *DiscoveryServer) configz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n451: \\tconfigs := make([]kubernetesConfig, 0)\\n452: \\tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.ConfigStore == nil {\\n453: \\t\\treturn\\n454: \\t}\\n455: \\ts.Env.ConfigStore.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\\n456: \\t\\tcfg := s.Env.ConfigStore.List(schema.GroupVersionKind(), \\\"\\\")\\n457: \\t\\tfor _, c := range cfg {\\n458: \\t\\t\\tconfigs = append(configs, kubernetesConfig{c})\\n459: \\t\\t}\\n460: \\t\\treturn false\\n461: \\t})\\n462: \\twriteJSON(w, configs, req)\\n463: }\\n464: \\n465: // SidecarScope debugging\\n466: func (s *DiscoveryServer) sidecarz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n467: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n468: \\tif con == nil {\\n469: \\t\\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\\n470: \\t\\treturn\\n471: \\t}\\n472: \\twriteJSON(w, con.proxy.SidecarScope, req)\\n473: }\\n474: \\n475: // Resource debugging.\\n476: func (s *DiscoveryServer) resourcez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n477: \\tschemas := make([]config.GroupVersionKind, 0)\\n478: \\n479: \\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.ConfigStore != nil {\\n480: \\t\\ts.Env.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\\n481: \\t\\t\\tschemas = append(schemas, schema.GroupVersionKind())\\n482: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n483: \\t\\t})\\n484: \\t}\\n485: \\n486: \\twriteJSON(w, schemas, req)\\n487: }\\n488: \\n489: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\\n490: type AuthorizationDebug struct {\\n491: \\tAuthorizationPolicies *model.AuthorizationPolicies `json:\\\"authorization_policies\\\"`\\n492: }\\n493: \\n494: // authorizationz dumps the internal authorization policies.\\n495: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authorizationz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n496: \\tinfo := AuthorizationDebug{\\n497: \\t\\tAuthorizationPolicies: s.globalPushContext().AuthzPolicies,\\n498: \\t}\\n499: \\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\\n500: }\\n501: \\n502: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\\n503: type TelemetryDebug struct {\\n504: \\tTelemetries *model.Telemetries `json:\\\"telemetries\\\"`\\n505: }\\n506: \\n507: func (s *DiscoveryServer) telemetryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n508: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n509: \\tif proxyID != \\\"\\\" && con == nil {\\n510: \\t\\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\\n511: \\t\\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\\n512: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n513: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\\\n\\\"))\\n514: \\t\\treturn\\n515: \\t}\\n516: \\tif con == nil {\\n517: \\t\\tinfo := TelemetryDebug{\\n518: \\t\\t\\tTelemetries: s.globalPushContext().Telemetry,\\n519: \\t\\t}\\n520: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\\n521: \\t\\treturn\\n522: \\t}\\n523: \\twriteJSON(w, s.globalPushContext().Telemetry.Debug(con.proxy), req)\\n524: }\\n525: \\n526: // connectionsHandler implements interface for displaying current connections.\\n527: // It is mapped to /debug/connections.\\n528: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionsHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n529: \\tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\\n530: \\tconnections := s.SortedClients()\\n531: \\tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\\n532: \\n533: \\tfor _, c := range connections {\\n534: \\t\\tadsClient := AdsClient{\\n535: \\t\\t\\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\\n536: \\t\\t\\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\\n537: \\t\\t\\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\\n538: \\t\\t}\\n539: \\t\\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\\n540: \\t}\\n541: \\twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\\n542: }\\n543: \\n544: // adsz implements a status and debug interface for ADS.\\n545: // It is mapped to /debug/adsz\\n546: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n547: \\tif s.handlePushRequest(w, req) {\\n548: \\t\\treturn\\n549: \\t}\\n550: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n551: \\tif proxyID != \\\"\\\" && con == nil {\\n552: \\t\\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\\n553: \\t\\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\\n554: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n555: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\\\n\\\"))\\n556: \\t\\treturn\\n557: \\t}\\n558: \\tvar connections []*Connection\\n559: \\tif con != nil {\\n560: \\t\\tconnections = []*Connection{con}\\n561: \\t} else {\\n562: \\t\\tconnections = s.SortedClients()\\n563: \\t}\\n564: \\n565: \\tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\\n566: \\tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\\n567: \\tfor _, c := range connections {\\n568: \\t\\tadsClient := AdsClient{\\n569: \\t\\t\\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\\n570: \\t\\t\\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\\n571: \\t\\t\\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\\n572: \\t\\t\\tLabels:       c.proxy.Labels,\\n573: \\t\\t\\tMetadata:     c.proxy.Metadata,\\n574: \\t\\t\\tLocality:     c.proxy.Locality,\\n575: \\t\\t\\tWatches:      map[string][]string{},\\n576: \\t\\t}\\n577: \\t\\tc.proxy.RLock()\\n578: \\t\\tfor k, wr := range c.proxy.WatchedResources {\\n579: \\t\\t\\tadsClient.Watches[k] = wr.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()\\n580: \\t\\t}\\n581: \\t\\tc.proxy.RUnlock()\\n582: \\t\\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\\n583: \\t}\\n584: \\twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\\n585: }\\n586: \\n587: func (s *DiscoveryServer) typedConfigDumpHandler(typ string) http.HandlerFunc {\\n588: \\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n589: \\t\\tq := r.URL.Query()\\n590: \\t\\tq.Set(\\\"types\\\", typ)\\n591: \\t\\tr.URL.RawQuery = q.Encode()\\n592: \\t\\ts.ConfigDump(w, r)\\n593: \\t}\\n594: }\\n595: \\n596: // ConfigDump returns information in the form of the Envoy admin API config dump for the specified proxy\\n597: // The dump will only contain dynamic listeners/clusters/routes and can be used to compare what an Envoy instance\\n598: // should look like according to Pilot vs what it currently does look like.\\n599: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigDump(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n600: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n601: \\tif con == nil {\\n602: \\t\\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\\n603: \\t\\treturn\\n604: \\t}\\n605: \\tif ts := s.getResourceTypes(req); len(ts) != 0 {\\n606: \\t\\tresources := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(con, nil, ts)\\n607: \\t\\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{}\\n608: \\t\\tfor _, resource := range resources {\\n609: \\t\\t\\tfor _, rr := range resource {\\n610: \\t\\t\\t\\tconfigDump.Configs = append(configDump.Configs, rr.Resource)\\n611: \\t\\t\\t}\\n612: \\t\\t}\\n613: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, configDump, req)\\n614: \\t\\treturn\\n615: \\t}\\n616: \\n617: \\tif con.proxy.IsZTunnel() {\\n618: \\t\\tresources := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(con, nil, []string{v3.AddressType})\\n619: \\t\\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{}\\n620: \\t\\tfor _, resource := range resources {\\n621: \\t\\t\\tfor _, rr := range resource {\\n622: \\t\\t\\t\\tconfigDump.Configs = append(configDump.Configs, rr.Resource)\\n623: \\t\\t\\t}\\n624: \\t\\t}\\n625: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, configDump, req)\\n626: \\t\\treturn\\n627: \\t}\\n628: \\n629: \\tincludeEds := req.URL.Query().Has(\\\"include_eds\\\")\\n630: \\tdump, err := s.connectionConfigDump(con, includeEds)\\n631: \\tif err != nil {\\n632: \\t\\thandleHTTPError(w, err)\\n633: \\t\\treturn\\n634: \\t}\\n635: \\twriteJSON(w, dump, req)\\n636: }\\n637: \\n638: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getResourceTypes(req *http.Request) []string {\\n639: \\tif shortTypes := req.URL.Query().Get(\\\"types\\\"); shortTypes != \\\"\\\" {\\n640: \\t\\tts := strings.Split(shortTypes, \\\",\\\")\\n641: \\n642: \\t\\tresourceTypes := sets.New[string]()\\n643: \\t\\tfor _, t := range ts {\\n644: \\t\\t\\trType := v3.GetResourceType(t)\\n645: \\t\\t\\tresourceTypes.Insert(rType)\\n646: \\t\\t\\t// special case for AddressType, include WorkloadType as well\\n647: \\t\\t\\t// because they shared same short type name `WDS`\\n648: \\t\\t\\tif rType == v3.AddressType {\\n649: \\t\\t\\t\\tresourceTypes.Insert(v3.WorkloadType)\\n650: \\t\\t\\t}\\n651: \\t\\t}\\n652: \\n653: \\t\\treturn resourceTypes.UnsortedList()\\n654: \\t}\\n655: \\treturn nil\\n656: }\\n657: \\n658: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn *Connection, req *model.PushRequest, ts []string) map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource {\\n659: \\tdumps := make(map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource)\\n660: \\tif req == nil {\\n661: \\t\\treq = &model.PushRequest{Push: conn.proxy.LastPushContext, Start: time.Now(), Full: true, Forced: true}\\n662: \\t}\\n663: \\n664: \\tfor _, resourceType := range ts {\\n665: \\t\\tw := conn.proxy.GetWatchedResource(resourceType)\\n666: \\t\\tif w == nil {\\n667: \\t\\t\\t// Not watched, skip\\n668: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n669: \\t\\t}\\n670: \\t\\tgen := s.findGenerator(resourceType, conn)\\n671: \\t\\tif gen == nil {\\n672: \\t\\t\\t// No generator found, skip\\n673: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n674: \\t\\t}\\n675: \\t\\tif resource, _, err := gen.Generate(conn.proxy, w, req); err == nil {\\n676: \\t\\t\\tfor _, rr := range resource {\\n677: \\t\\t\\t\\tswitch resourceType {\\n678: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase v3.SecretType:\\n679: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Secrets must be redacted\\n680: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tsecret := &tls.Secret{}\\n681: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif err := rr.Resource.UnmarshalTo(secret); err != nil {\\n682: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"failed to unmarshal secret: %v\\\", err)\\n683: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n684: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n685: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif secret.GetTlsCertificate() != nil {\\n686: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// When utilizing XDS caching, the resource object is shared, so modifying this would modify the cached item\\n687: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Make a clone\\n688: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\trr = protomarshal.Clone(rr)\\n689: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tsecret.GetTlsCertificate().PrivateKey = &core.DataSource{\\n690: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSpecifier: &core.DataSource_InlineBytes{\\n691: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tInlineBytes: []byte(\\\"[redacted]\\\"),\\n692: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n693: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n694: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n695: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\trr.Resource = protoconv.MessageToAny(secret)\\n696: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\\n697: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase v3.ExtensionConfigurationType:\\n698: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttce := &core.TypedExtensionConfig{}\\n699: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif err := rr.GetResource().UnmarshalTo(tce); err != nil {\\n700: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"failed to unmarshal extension: %v\\\", err)\\n701: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n702: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n703: \\n704: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tswitch tce.TypedConfig.TypeUrl {\\n705: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcase xds.WasmHTTPFilterType:\\n706: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tw := &wasm.Wasm{}\\n707: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif err := tce.TypedConfig.UnmarshalTo(w); err != nil {\\n708: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"failed to unmarshal wasm filter: %v\\\", err)\\n709: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n710: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n711: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Redact Wasm secret env variable.\\n712: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvmenvs := w.GetConfig().GetVmConfig().EnvironmentVariables\\n713: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif vmenvs != nil {\\n714: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif _, found := vmenvs.KeyValues[model.WasmSecretEnv]; found {\\n715: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvmenvs.KeyValues[model.WasmSecretEnv] = \\\"<Redacted>\\\"\\n716: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n717: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n718: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], &discoveryv3.Resource{\\n719: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tName:     w.Config.Name,\\n720: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(w),\\n721: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n722: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n723: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\\n724: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n725: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n726: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\\n727: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n728: \\t\\t\\t}\\n729: \\t\\t} else {\\n730: \\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"generate failed for request resource type (%v): %v\\\", resourceType, err)\\n731: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n732: \\t\\t}\\n733: \\t}\\n734: \\n735: \\treturn dumps\\n736: }\\n737: \\n738: // connectionConfigDump converts the connection internal state into an Envoy Admin API config dump proto\\n739: // It is used in debugging to create a consistent object for comparison between Envoy and Pilot outputs\\n740: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionConfigDump(conn *Connection, includeEds bool) (*admin.ConfigDump, error) {\\n741: \\treq := &model.PushRequest{Push: conn.proxy.LastPushContext, Start: time.Now(), Full: true, Forced: true}\\n742: \\tversion := req.Push.PushVersion\\n743: \\n744: \\tdump := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn, req, []string{\\n745: \\t\\tv3.ClusterType,\\n746: \\t\\tv3.ListenerType,\\n747: \\t\\tv3.RouteType,\\n748: \\t\\tv3.SecretType,\\n749: \\t\\tv3.EndpointType,\\n750: \\t\\tv3.ExtensionConfigurationType,\\n751: \\t})\\n752: \\n753: \\tdynamicActiveClusters := make([]*admin.ClustersConfigDump_DynamicCluster, 0)\\n754: \\tfor _, cluster := range dump[v3.ClusterType] {\\n755: \\t\\tdynamicActiveClusters = append(dynamicActiveClusters, &admin.ClustersConfigDump_DynamicCluster{\\n756: \\t\\t\\tCluster: cluster.Resource,\\n757: \\t\\t})\\n758: \\t}\\n759: \\tclustersAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.ClustersConfigDump{\\n760: \\t\\tVersionInfo:           version,\\n761: \\t\\tDynamicActiveClusters: dynamicActiveClusters,\\n762: \\t})\\n763: \\tif err != nil {\\n764: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n765: \\t}\\n766: \\n767: \\tdynamicActiveListeners := make([]*admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListener, 0)\\n768: \\tfor _, listener := range dump[v3.ListenerType] {\\n769: \\t\\tdynamicActiveListeners = append(dynamicActiveListeners, &admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListener{\\n770: \\t\\t\\tName: listener.Name,\\n771: \\t\\t\\tActiveState: &admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListenerState{\\n772: \\t\\t\\t\\tListener:    listener.Resource,\\n773: \\t\\t\\t\\tVersionInfo: version,\\n774: \\t\\t\\t},\\n775: \\t\\t})\\n776: \\t}\\n777: \\tlistenersAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.ListenersConfigDump{\\n778: \\t\\tVersionInfo:      version,\\n779: \\t\\tDynamicListeners: dynamicActiveListeners,\\n780: \\t})\\n781: \\tif err != nil {\\n782: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n783: \\t}\\n784: \\n785: \\tdynamicRouteConfig := make([]*admin.RoutesConfigDump_DynamicRouteConfig, 0)\\n786: \\tfor _, route := range dump[v3.RouteType] {\\n787: \\t\\tdynamicRouteConfig = append(dynamicRouteConfig, &admin.RoutesConfigDump_DynamicRouteConfig{\\n788: \\t\\t\\tVersionInfo: version,\\n789: \\t\\t\\tRouteConfig: route.Resource,\\n790: \\t\\t})\\n791: \\t}\\n792: \\troutesAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.RoutesConfigDump{\\n793: \\t\\tDynamicRouteConfigs: dynamicRouteConfig,\\n794: \\t})\\n795: \\tif err != nil {\\n796: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n797: \\t}\\n798: \\n799: \\tdynamicSecretsConfig := make([]*admin.SecretsConfigDump_DynamicSecret, 0)\\n800: \\tfor _, secret := range dump[v3.SecretType] {\\n801: \\t\\tdynamicSecretsConfig = append(dynamicSecretsConfig, &admin.SecretsConfigDump_DynamicSecret{\\n802: \\t\\t\\tVersionInfo: version,\\n803: \\t\\t\\tSecret:      secret.Resource,\\n804: \\t\\t})\\n805: \\t}\\n806: \\tsecretsAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.SecretsConfigDump{\\n807: \\t\\tDynamicActiveSecrets: dynamicSecretsConfig,\\n808: \\t})\\n809: \\tif err != nil {\\n810: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n811: \\t}\\n812: \\n813: \\textensionsConfig := make([]*admin.EcdsConfigDump_EcdsFilterConfig, 0)\\n814: \\tfor _, ext := range dump[v3.ExtensionConfigurationType] {\\n815: \\t\\textensionsConfig = append(extensionsConfig, &admin.EcdsConfigDump_EcdsFilterConfig{\\n816: \\t\\t\\tVersionInfo: version,\\n817: \\t\\t\\tEcdsFilter:  ext.Resource,\\n818: \\t\\t})\\n819: \\t}\\n820: \\textensionsAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.EcdsConfigDump{\\n821: \\t\\tEcdsFilters: extensionsConfig,\\n822: \\t})\\n823: \\tif err != nil {\\n824: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n825: \\t}\\n826: \\n827: \\tvar endpointsAny *anypb.Any\\n828: \\t// EDS is disabled by default for compatibility with Envoy config_dump interface\\n829: \\tif includeEds {\\n830: \\t\\tendpointConfig := make([]*admin.EndpointsConfigDump_DynamicEndpointConfig, 0)\\n831: \\t\\tfor _, endpoint := range dump[v3.EndpointType] {\\n832: \\t\\t\\tendpointConfig = append(endpointConfig, &admin.EndpointsConfigDump_DynamicEndpointConfig{\\n833: \\t\\t\\t\\tVersionInfo:    version,\\n834: \\t\\t\\t\\tEndpointConfig: endpoint.Resource,\\n835: \\t\\t\\t})\\n836: \\t\\t}\\n837: \\t\\tendpointsAny, err = protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.EndpointsConfigDump{\\n838: \\t\\t\\tDynamicEndpointConfigs: endpointConfig,\\n839: \\t\\t})\\n840: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n841: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n842: \\t\\t}\\n843: \\t}\\n844: \\n845: \\tbootstrapAny := protoconv.MessageToAny(&admin.BootstrapConfigDump{})\\n846: \\tscopedRoutesAny := protoconv.MessageToAny(&admin.ScopedRoutesConfigDump{})\\n847: \\t// The config dump must have all configs with connections specified in\\n848: \\t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v2/admin/v2alpha/config_dump.proto\\n849: \\tconfigs := []*anypb.Any{\\n850: \\t\\tbootstrapAny,\\n851: \\t\\tclustersAny,\\n852: \\t}\\n853: \\tif includeEds {\\n854: \\t\\tconfigs = append(configs, endpointsAny)\\n855: \\t}\\n856: \\tconfigs = append(configs,\\n857: \\t\\tlistenersAny,\\n858: \\t\\tscopedRoutesAny,\\n859: \\t\\troutesAny,\\n860: \\t\\tsecretsAny,\\n861: \\t\\textensionsAny,\\n862: \\t)\\n863: \\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{\\n864: \\t\\tConfigs: configs,\\n865: \\t}\\n866: \\treturn configDump, nil\\n867: }\\n868: \\n869: // injectTemplateHandler dumps the injection template\\n870: // Replaces dumping the template at startup.\\n871: func (s *DiscoveryServer) injectTemplateHandler(webhook func() map[string]string) func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request) {\\n872: \\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n873: \\t\\t// TODO: we should split the inject template into smaller modules (separate one for dump core, etc),\\n874: \\t\\t// and allow pods to select which patches will be selected. When this happen, this should return\\n875: \\t\\t// all inject templates or take a param to select one.\\n876: \\t\\tif webhook == nil {\\n877: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n878: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n879: \\t\\t}\\n880: \\n881: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, webhook(), req)\\n882: \\t}\\n883: }\\n884: \\n885: // meshHandler dumps the mesh config\\n886: func (s *DiscoveryServer) meshHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n887: \\twriteJSON(w, s.Env.Mesh(), req)\\n888: }\\n889: \\n890: // pushStatusHandler dumps the last PushContext\\n891: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushStatusHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n892: \\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\\n893: \\tdefer model.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\\n894: \\tif model.LastPushStatus == nil {\\n895: \\t\\treturn\\n896: \\t}\\n897: \\tout, err := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()\\n898: \\tif err != nil {\\n899: \\t\\thandleHTTPError(w, err)\\n900: \\t\\treturn\\n901: \\t}\\n902: \\tw.Header().Add(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n903: \\n904: \\t_, _ = w.Write(out)\\n905: }\\n906: \\n907: // PushContextDebug holds debug information for push context.\\n908: type PushContextDebug struct {\\n909: \\tAuthorizationPolicies *model.AuthorizationPolicies\\n910: \\tNetworkGateways       []model.NetworkGateway\\n911: \\tUnresolvedGateways    []model.NetworkGateway\\n912: }\\n913: \\n914: // pushContextHandler dumps the current PushContext\\n915: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushContextHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n916: \\tpush := PushContextDebug{}\\n917: \\tpc := s.globalPushContext()\\n918: \\tif pc == nil {\\n919: \\t\\treturn\\n920: \\t}\\n921: \\tpush.AuthorizationPolicies = pc.AuthzPolicies\\n922: \\tif pc.NetworkManager() != nil {\\n923: \\t\\tpush.NetworkGateways = pc.NetworkManager().AllGateways()\\n924: \\t\\tpush.UnresolvedGateways = pc.NetworkManager().Unresolved.AllGateways()\\n925: \\t}\\n926: \\n927: \\twriteJSON(w, push, req)\\n928: }\\n929: \\n930: // DebugEndpoints lists all the supported debug endpoints.\\n931: func (s *DiscoveryServer) DebugEndpoints() []string {\\n932: \\treturn slices.Sort(maps.Keys(s.debugHandlers))\\n933: }\\n934: \\n935: // Debug lists all the supported debug endpoints.\\n936: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Debug(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n937: \\ttype debugEndpoint struct {\\n938: \\t\\tName string\\n939: \\t\\tHref string\\n940: \\t\\tHelp string\\n941: \\t}\\n942: \\tvar deps []debugEndpoint\\n943: \\tfor k, v := range s.debugHandlers {\\n944: \\t\\tdeps = append(deps, debugEndpoint{\\n945: \\t\\t\\tName: k,\\n946: \\t\\t\\tHref: k,\\n947: \\t\\t\\tHelp: v,\\n948: \\t\\t})\\n949: \\t}\\n950: \\n951: \\tsort.Slice(deps, func(i, j int) bool {\\n952: \\t\\treturn deps[i].Name < deps[j].Name\\n953: \\t})\\n954: \\n955: \\tif err := indexTmpl.Execute(w, deps); err != nil {\\n956: \\t\\tistiolog.Errorf(\\\"Error in rendering index template %v\\\", err)\\n957: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n958: \\t}\\n959: }\\n960: \\n961: // list all the supported debug commands in json.\\n962: func (s *DiscoveryServer) list(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n963: \\tvar cmdNames []string\\n964: \\tfor k := range s.debugHandlers {\\n965: \\t\\tkey := strings.Replace(k, \\\"/debug/\\\", \\\"\\\", -1)\\n966: \\t\\t// exclude current list command\\n967: \\t\\tif key == \\\"list\\\" {\\n968: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n969: \\t\\t}\\n970: \\t\\t// can not support pprof commands\\n971: \\t\\tif strings.Contains(key, \\\"pprof\\\") {\\n972: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n973: \\t\\t}\\n974: \\t\\tcmdNames = append(cmdNames, key)\\n975: \\t}\\n976: \\tsort.Strings(cmdNames)\\n977: \\twriteJSON(w, cmdNames, req)\\n978: }\\n979: \\n980: func (s *DiscoveryServer) forceDisconnect(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n981: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n982: \\tif con == nil {\\n983: \\t\\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\\n984: \\t\\treturn\\n985: \\t}\\n986: \\tcon.Stop()\\n987: \\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"OK\\\"))\\n988: }\\n989: \\n990: func cloneProxy(proxy *model.Proxy) *model.Proxy {\\n991: \\tif proxy == nil {\\n992: \\t\\treturn nil\\n993: \\t}\\n994: \\n995: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n996: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n997: \\t// nolint: govet\\n998: \\tcopied := *proxy\\n999: \\tout := &copied\\n1000: \\tout.RWMutex = sync.RWMutex{}\\n1001: \\t// clone WatchedResources which can be mutated when processing request\\n1002: \\tout.WatchedResources = make(map[string]*model.WatchedResource, len(proxy.WatchedResources))\\n1003: \\tfor k, v := range proxy.WatchedResources {\\n1004: \\t\\t// nolint: govet\\n1005: \\t\\tv := *v\\n1006: \\t\\tv.ResourceNames = v.ResourceNames.Copy()\\n1007: \\t\\tout.WatchedResources[k] = &v\\n1008: \\t}\\n1009: \\treturn out\\n1010: }\\n1011: \\n1012: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getProxyConnection(proxyID string) *Connection {\\n1013: \\tfor _, con := range s.Clients() {\\n1014: \\t\\tif strings.Contains(con.ID(), proxyID) {\\n1015: \\t\\t\\tout := *con\\n1016: \\t\\t\\tout.proxy = cloneProxy(con.proxy)\\n1017: \\t\\t\\treturn &out\\n1018: \\t\\t}\\n1019: \\t}\\n1020: \\n1021: \\treturn nil\\n1022: }\\n1023: \\n1024: func (s *DiscoveryServer) instancesz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1025: \\tinstances := map[string][]model.ServiceTarget{}\\n1026: \\tfor _, con := range s.Clients() {\\n1027: \\t\\tcon.proxy.RLock()\\n1028: \\t\\tif con.proxy != nil {\\n1029: \\t\\t\\tinstances[con.proxy.ID] = con.proxy.ServiceTargets\\n1030: \\t\\t}\\n1031: \\t\\tcon.proxy.RUnlock()\\n1032: \\t}\\n1033: \\twriteJSON(w, instances, req)\\n1034: }\\n1035: \\n1036: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ambientz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1037: \\taddresses, _ := s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(nil)\\n1038: \\tres := struct {\\n1039: \\t\\tWorkloads []jsonMarshalProto `json:\\\"workloads\\\"`\\n1040: \\t\\tServices  []jsonMarshalProto `json:\\\"services\\\"`\\n1041: \\t\\tPolicies  []jsonMarshalProto `json:\\\"policies\\\"`\\n1042: \\t}{}\\n1043: \\t// WDS stores IPs as raw byte form. We want to view them as strings, so convert.\\n1044: \\t// This doesn't quite work ideally, since json marshal will write as base64, but its better than nothing\\n1045: \\trewriteAddress := func(b []byte) []byte {\\n1046: \\t\\tip, ok := netip.AddrFromSlice(b)\\n1047: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n1048: \\t\\t\\treturn b\\n1049: \\t\\t}\\n1050: \\t\\treturn []byte(ip.String())\\n1051: \\t}\\n1052: \\trewriteNetworkAddress := func(b *workloadapi.NetworkAddress) *workloadapi.NetworkAddress {\\n1053: \\t\\tnb := protomarshal.Clone(b)\\n1054: \\t\\tnb.Address = rewriteAddress(nb.Address)\\n1055: \\t\\treturn nb\\n1056: \\t}\\n1057: \\trewriteGatewayAddress := func(b *workloadapi.GatewayAddress) *workloadapi.GatewayAddress {\\n1058: \\t\\tif b == nil {\\n1059: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n1060: \\t\\t}\\n1061: \\t\\tnb := protomarshal.Clone(b)\\n1062: \\t\\tswitch t := nb.Destination.(type) {\\n1063: \\t\\tcase *workloadapi.GatewayAddress_Address:\\n1064: \\t\\t\\tt.Address = rewriteNetworkAddress(t.Address)\\n1065: \\t\\t}\\n1066: \\t\\treturn nb\\n1067: \\t}\\n1068: \\tfor _, original := range addresses {\\n1069: \\t\\taddr := protomarshal.Clone(original.Address)\\n1070: \\t\\tswitch addr := addr.Type.(type) {\\n1071: \\t\\tcase *workloadapi.Address_Workload:\\n1072: \\t\\t\\tw := addr.Workload\\n1073: \\t\\t\\tw.Addresses = slices.Map(w.Addresses, rewriteAddress)\\n1074: \\t\\t\\tw.Waypoint = rewriteGatewayAddress(w.Waypoint)\\n1075: \\t\\t\\tw.NetworkGateway = rewriteGatewayAddress(w.NetworkGateway)\\n1076: \\t\\t\\tres.Workloads = append(res.Workloads, jsonMarshalProto{w})\\n1077: \\t\\tcase *workloadapi.Address_Service:\\n1078: \\t\\t\\ts := addr.Service\\n1079: \\t\\t\\ts.Addresses = slices.Map(s.Addresses, rewriteNetworkAddress)\\n1080: \\t\\t\\ts.Waypoint = rewriteGatewayAddress(s.Waypoint)\\n1081: \\t\\t\\tres.Services = append(res.Services, jsonMarshalProto{s})\\n1082: \\t\\t}\\n1083: \\t}\\n1084: \\tfor _, policy := range s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(nil) {\\n1085: \\t\\tres.Policies = append(res.Policies, jsonMarshalProto{policy.Authorization})\\n1086: \\t}\\n1087: \\twriteJSON(w, res, req)\\n1088: }\\n1089: \\n1090: func (s *DiscoveryServer) krtz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1091: \\twriteJSON(w, s.krtDebugger, req)\\n1092: }\\n1093: \\n1094: func (s *DiscoveryServer) networkz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1095: \\tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.NetworkManager == nil {\\n1096: \\t\\treturn\\n1097: \\t}\\n1098: \\twriteJSON(w, s.Env.NetworkManager.AllGateways(), req)\\n1099: }\\n1100: \\n1101: func (s *DiscoveryServer) mcsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1102: \\tsvcs := sortMCSServices(s.Env.MCSServices())\\n1103: \\twriteJSON(w, svcs, req)\\n1104: }\\n1105: \\n1106: func sortMCSServices(svcs []model.MCSServiceInfo) []model.MCSServiceInfo {\\n1107: \\tsort.Slice(svcs, func(i, j int) bool {\\n1108: \\t\\tif strings.Compare(svcs[i].Cluster.String(), svcs[j].Cluster.String()) < 0 {\\n1109: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n1110: \\t\\t}\\n1111: \\t\\tif strings.Compare(svcs[i].Namespace, svcs[j].Namespace) < 0 {\\n1112: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n1113: \\t\\t}\\n1114: \\t\\treturn strings.Compare(svcs[i].Name, svcs[j].Name) < 0\\n1115: \\t})\\n1116: \\treturn svcs\\n1117: }\\n1118: \\n1119: func (s *DiscoveryServer) clusterz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1120: \\tif s.ListRemoteClusters == nil {\\n1121: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\\n1122: \\t\\treturn\\n1123: \\t}\\n1124: \\twriteJSON(w, s.ListRemoteClusters(), req)\\n1125: }\\n1126: \\n1127: // handlePushRequest handles a ?push=true query param and triggers a push.\\n1128: // A boolean response is returned to indicate if the caller should continue\\n1129: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handlePushRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) bool {\\n1130: \\tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\\n1131: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\\n1132: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Failed to parse request\\\\n\\\"))\\n1133: \\t\\treturn true\\n1134: \\t}\\n1135: \\tif req.Form.Get(\\\"push\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n1136: \\t\\tAdsPushAll(s)\\n1137: \\t\\t_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, \\\"Pushed to %d servers\\\\n\\\", s.adsClientCount())\\n1138: \\t\\treturn true\\n1139: \\t}\\n1140: \\treturn false\\n1141: }\\n1142: \\n1143: // getDebugConnection fetches the Connection requested by proxyID\\n1144: // For non-system namespaces, restricts access to proxies in the caller's namespace only\\n1145: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getDebugConnection(req *http.Request) (string, *Connection) {\\n1146: \\tif proxyID := req.URL.Query().Get(\\\"proxyID\\\"); proxyID != \\\"\\\" {\\n1147: \\t\\tcon := s.getProxyConnection(proxyID)\\n1148: \\t\\t// Verify namespace if caller is not from system namespace (when auth is enabled)\\n1149: \\t\\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\\n1150: \\t\\t\\tcallerNamespace, _ := req.Context().Value(CallerNamespaceKey{}).(string)\\n1151: \\t\\t\\tif con != nil && callerNamespace != \\\"\\\" {\\n1152: \\t\\t\\t\\tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\\n1153: \\t\\t\\t\\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \\\"\\\" {\\n1154: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\\n1155: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1156: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Non-system namespaces can only access proxies in their own namespace\\n1157: \\t\\t\\t\\tif callerNamespace != systemNamespace && con.proxy.ConfigNamespace != callerNamespace {\\n1158: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn proxyID, nil // Return nil connection to deny access\\n1159: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1160: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1161: \\t\\t}\\n1162: \\t\\treturn proxyID, con\\n1163: \\t}\\n1164: \\treturn \\\"\\\", nil\\n1165: }\\n1166: \\n1167: func (s *DiscoveryServer) errorHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, proxyID string, con *Connection) {\\n1168: \\tif proxyID == \\\"\\\" {\\n1169: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\\n1170: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"You must provide a proxyID in the query string\\\\n\\\"))\\n1171: \\t\\treturn\\n1172: \\t}\\n1173: \\tif con == nil {\\n1174: \\t\\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\\n1175: \\t\\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\\n1176: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n1177: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\\\n\\\"))\\n1178: \\t\\treturn\\n1179: \\t}\\n1180: }\\n1181: \\n1182: // jsonMarshalProto wraps a proto.Message so it can be marshaled with the standard encoding/json library\\n1183: type jsonMarshalProto struct {\\n1184: \\tproto.Message\\n1185: }\\n1186: \\n1187: func (p jsonMarshalProto) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\\n1188: \\treturn protomarshal.Marshal(p.Message)\\n1189: }\\n1190: \\n1191: // writeJSON writes a json payload, handling content type, marshaling, and errors\\n1192: func writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, obj any, req *http.Request) {\\n1193: \\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/json\\\")\\n1194: \\tvar b []byte\\n1195: \\tvar err error\\n1196: \\tif req.URL.Query().Has(\\\"pretty\\\") {\\n1197: \\t\\tb, err = config.ToPrettyJSON(obj)\\n1198: \\t} else {\\n1199: \\t\\tb, err = config.ToJSON(obj)\\n1200: \\t}\\n1201: \\tif err != nil {\\n1202: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n1203: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(err.Error()))\\n1204: \\t\\treturn\\n1205: \\t}\\n1206: \\t_, err = w.Write(b)\\n1207: \\tif err != nil {\\n1208: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n1209: \\t}\\n1210: }\\n1211: \\n1212: // handleHTTPError writes an error message to the response\\n1213: func handleHTTPError(w http.ResponseWriter, err error) {\\n1214: \\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\\n1215: \\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(err.Error()))\\n1216: }\\n1217: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/debug.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"context\"\n19: \t\"encoding/json\"\n20: \t\"fmt\"\n21: \t\"html/template\"\n22: \t\"net\"\n23: \t\"net/http\"\n24: \t\"net/http/pprof\"\n25: \t\"net/netip\"\n26: \t\"runtime\"\n27: \t\"sort\"\n28: \t\"strings\"\n29: \t\"sync\"\n30: \t\"time\"\n31: \n32: \tadmin \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/admin/v3\"\n33: \tcore \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\"\n34: \twasm \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/extensions/filters/http/wasm/v3\"\n35: \ttls \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/v3\"\n36: \tdiscoveryv3 \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n37: \t\"google.golang.org/protobuf/proto\"\n38: \tanypb \"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/anypb\"\n39: \n40: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/kube/crd\"\n41: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n42: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n43: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n44: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\"\n45: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n46: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config\"\n47: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/constants\"\n48: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/resource\"\n49: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/xds\"\n50: \tistiolog \"istio.io/istio/pkg/log\"\n51: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/maps\"\n52: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/security\"\n53: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\"\n54: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/spiffe\"\n55: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/protomarshal\"\n56: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n57: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/workloadapi\"\n58: )\n59: \n60: // CallerNamespaceKey is used to store caller namespace in request context\n61: type CallerNamespaceKey struct{}\n62: \n63: var indexTmpl = template.Must(template.New(\"index\").Parse(`<html>\n64: <head>\n65: <title>Pilot Debug Console</title>\n66: </head>\n67: <style>\n68: #endpoints {\n69:   font-family: \"Trebuchet MS\", Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;\n70:   border-collapse: collapse;\n71: }\n72: \n73: #endpoints td, #endpoints th {\n74:   border: 1px solid #ddd;\n75:   padding: 8px;\n76: }\n77: \n78: #endpoints tr:nth-child(even){background-color: #f2f2f2;}\n79: \n80: #endpoints tr:hover {background-color: #ddd;}\n81: \n82: #endpoints th {\n83:   padding-top: 12px;\n84:   padding-bottom: 12px;\n85:   text-align: left;\n86:   background-color: black;\n87:   color: white;\n88: }\n89: </style>\n90: <body>\n91: <br/>\n92: <p style = \"font-family:Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif;\">\n93: Note: Use <b>pretty</b> in query string (like <b>debug/configz?pretty</b>) to format the output.\n94: </p>\n95: <table id=\"endpoints\">\n96: <tr><th>Endpoint</th><th>Description</th></tr>\n97: {{range .}}\n98: \t<tr>\n99: \t<td><a href='{{.Href}}'>{{.Name}}</a></td><td>{{.Help}}</td>\n100: \t</tr>\n101: {{end}}\n102: </table>\n103: <br/>\n104: </body>\n105: </html>\n106: `))\n107: \n108: // AdsClient defines the data that is displayed on \"/adsz\" endpoint.\n109: type AdsClient struct {\n110: \tConnectionID string              `json:\"connectionId\"`\n111: \tConnectedAt  time.Time           `json:\"connectedAt\"`\n112: \tPeerAddress  string              `json:\"address\"`\n113: \tLabels       map[string]string   `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n114: \tMetadata     *model.NodeMetadata `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n115: \tLocality     *core.Locality      `json:\"locality,omitempty\"`\n116: \tWatches      map[string][]string `json:\"watches,omitempty\"`\n117: }\n118: \n119: // AdsClients is collection of AdsClient connected to this Istiod.\n120: type AdsClients struct {\n121: \tTotal     int         `json:\"totalClients\"`\n122: \tConnected []AdsClient `json:\"clients,omitempty\"`\n123: }\n124: \n125: // SyncStatus is the synchronization status between Pilot and a given Envoy\n126: type SyncStatus struct {\n127: \tClusterID            string                    `json:\"cluster_id,omitempty\"`\n128: \tProxyID              string                    `json:\"proxy,omitempty\"`\n129: \tProxyType            model.NodeType            `json:\"proxy_type,omitempty\"`\n130: \tProxyVersion         string                    `json:\"proxy_version,omitempty\"`\n131: \tIstioVersion         string                    `json:\"istio_version,omitempty\"`\n132: \tClusterSent          string                    `json:\"cluster_sent,omitempty\"`\n133: \tClusterAcked         string                    `json:\"cluster_acked,omitempty\"`\n134: \tListenerSent         string                    `json:\"listener_sent,omitempty\"`\n135: \tListenerAcked        string                    `json:\"listener_acked,omitempty\"`\n136: \tRouteSent            string                    `json:\"route_sent,omitempty\"`\n137: \tRouteAcked           string                    `json:\"route_acked,omitempty\"`\n138: \tEndpointSent         string                    `json:\"endpoint_sent,omitempty\"`\n139: \tEndpointAcked        string                    `json:\"endpoint_acked,omitempty\"`\n140: \tExtensionConfigSent  string                    `json:\"extensionconfig_sent,omitempty\"`\n141: \tExtensionConfigAcked string                    `json:\"extensionconfig_acked,omitempty\"`\n142: \tResources            map[string]ResourceStatus `json:\"resources,omitempty\"`\n143: }\n144: \n145: type ResourceStatus struct {\n146: \tSent      string    `json:\"sent,omitempty\"`\n147: \tAcked     string    `json:\"acked,omitempty\"`\n148: \tSentTime  time.Time `json:\"sentTime,omitempty\"`\n149: \tLastError string    `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n150: }\n151: \n152: // SyncedVersions shows what resourceVersion of a given resource has been acked by Envoy.\n153: type SyncedVersions struct {\n154: \tProxyID         string `json:\"proxy,omitempty\"`\n155: \tClusterVersion  string `json:\"cluster_acked,omitempty\"`\n156: \tListenerVersion string `json:\"listener_acked,omitempty\"`\n157: \tRouteVersion    string `json:\"route_acked,omitempty\"`\n158: \tEndpointVersion string `json:\"endpoint_acked,omitempty\"`\n159: }\n160: \n161: // InitDebug initializes the debug handlers and adds a debug in-memory registry.\n162: func (s *DiscoveryServer) InitDebug(\n163: \tmux *http.ServeMux,\n164: \tenableProfiling bool,\n165: \tfetchWebhook func() map[string]string,\n166: ) *http.ServeMux {\n167: \tinternalMux := http.NewServeMux()\n168: \ts.AddDebugHandlers(mux, internalMux, enableProfiling, fetchWebhook)\n169: \treturn internalMux\n170: }\n171: \n172: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AddDebugHandlers(mux, internalMux *http.ServeMux, enableProfiling bool, webhook func() map[string]string) {\n173: \t// Debug handlers on HTTP ports are added for backward compatibility.\n174: \t// They will be exposed on XDS-over-TLS in future releases.\n175: \tif !features.EnableDebugOnHTTP {\n176: \t\treturn\n177: \t}\n178: \n179: \tif enableProfiling {\n180: \t\truntime.SetMutexProfileFraction(features.MutexProfileFraction)\n181: \t\truntime.SetBlockProfileRate(features.MutexProfileFraction)\n182: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/\", \"Displays pprof index\", pprof.Index)\n183: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/cmdline\", \"The command line invocation of the current program\", pprof.Cmdline)\n184: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/profile\", \"CPU profile\", pprof.Profile)\n185: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/symbol\", \"Symbol looks up the program counters listed in the request\", pprof.Symbol)\n186: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/trace\", \"A trace of execution of the current program.\", pprof.Trace)\n187: \t}\n188: \n189: \tmux.HandleFunc(\"/debug\", s.Debug)\n190: \n191: \tif features.EnableUnsafeAdminEndpoints {\n192: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/force_disconnect\", \"Disconnects a proxy from this Pilot\", s.forceDisconnect)\n193: \t}\n194: \n195: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ecdsz\", \"Status and debug interface for ECDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"ecds\"))\n196: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/edsz\", \"Status and debug interface for EDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"eds\"))\n197: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ndsz\", \"Status and debug interface for NDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"nds\"))\n198: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/adsz\", \"Status and debug interface for ADS\", s.adsz)\n199: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/adsz?push=true\", \"Initiates push of the current state to all connected endpoints\", s.adsz)\n200: \n201: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/syncz\", \"Synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\", s.Syncz)\n202: \n203: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/registryz\", \"Debug support for registry\", s.registryz)\n204: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/endpointz\", \"Obsolete, use endpointShardz\", s.endpointShardz)\n205: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/endpointShardz\", \"Info about the endpoint shards\", s.endpointShardz)\n206: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez\", \"Info about the internal XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n207: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez?sizes=true\", \"Info about the size of the internal XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n208: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez?clear=true\", \"Clear the XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n209: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/configz\", \"Debug support for config\", s.configz)\n210: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/sidecarz\", \"Debug sidecar scope for a proxy\", s.sidecarz)\n211: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/resourcesz\", \"Debug support for watched resources\", s.resourcez)\n212: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/instancesz\", \"Debug support for service instances\", s.instancesz)\n213: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ambientz\", \"Debug support for ambient\", s.ambientz)\n214: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/krtz\", \"Debug support for krt (internal state)\", s.krtz)\n215: \n216: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/authorizationz\", \"Internal authorization policies\", s.authorizationz)\n217: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/telemetryz\", \"Debug Telemetry configuration\", s.telemetryz)\n218: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/config_dump\", \"ConfigDump in the form of the Envoy admin config dump API for passed in proxyID\", s.ConfigDump)\n219: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/push_status\", \"Last PushContext Details\", s.pushStatusHandler)\n220: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pushcontext\", \"Debug support for current push context\", s.pushContextHandler)\n221: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/connections\", \"Info about the connected XDS clients\", s.connectionsHandler)\n222: \n223: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/inject\", \"Active inject template\", s.injectTemplateHandler(webhook))\n224: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/mesh\", \"Active mesh config\", s.meshHandler)\n225: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/clusterz\", \"List remote clusters where istiod reads endpoints\", s.clusterz)\n226: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/networkz\", \"List cross-network gateways\", s.networkz)\n227: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/mcsz\", \"List information about Kubernetes MCS services\", s.mcsz)\n228: \n229: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/list\", \"List all supported debug commands in json\", s.list)\n230: }\n231: \n232: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addDebugHandler(mux *http.ServeMux, internalMux *http.ServeMux,\n233: \tpath string, help string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request),\n234: ) {\n235: \ts.debugHandlers[path] = help\n236: \t// Add handler without auth. This mux is never exposed on an HTTP server and only used internally\n237: \tif internalMux != nil {\n238: \t\tinternalMux.HandleFunc(path, handler)\n239: \t}\n240: \t// Add handler with auth; this is expose on an HTTP server\n241: \tmux.HandleFunc(path, s.allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(http.HandlerFunc(handler)))\n242: }\n243: \n244: func (s *DiscoveryServer) allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(next http.Handler) http.HandlerFunc {\n245: \treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n246: \t\t// Localhost gets unrestricted access (istiod talking to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\n247: \t\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\n248: \t\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\n249: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n250: \t\t\treturn\n251: \t\t}\n252: \t\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\n253: \t\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\n254: \t\tvar ids []string\n255: \t\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\n256: \t\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\n257: \t\t\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\n258: \t\t\t// If one authenticator passes, return\n259: \t\t\tif u != nil && u.Identities != nil && err == nil {\n260: \t\t\t\tids = u.Identities\n261: \t\t\t\tbreak\n262: \t\t\t}\n263: \t\t\tauthFailMsgs = append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\"Authenticator %s: %v\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\n264: \t\t}\n265: \t\tif ids == nil {\n266: \t\t\tistiolog.Errorf(\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\n267: \t\t\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \"authentication failure).\n268: \t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\n269: \t\t\treturn\n270: \t\t}\n271: \t\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\n272: \t\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\n273: \t\t\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\n274: \t\t\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\n275: \t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\", ids, req.URL.Path)\n276: \t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\n277: \t\t\t\treturn\n278: \t\t\t}\n279: \t\t\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\n280: \t\t\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\n281: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\n282: \t\t} else {\n283: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n284: \t\t}\n285: \t}\n286: }\n287: \n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\n289: \tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\n290: \tif err != nil {\n291: \t\treturn false\n292: \t}\n293: \n294: \tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\n295: \treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\n296: }\n297: \n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\n300: \tfor _, id := range identities {\n301: \t\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(id)\n302: \t\tif err != nil {\n303: \t\t\tcontinue\n304: \t\t}\n305: \t\treturn spiffeID.Namespace\n306: \t}\n307: \treturn \"\"\n308: }\n309: \n310: // AuthorizeDebugRequest checks if authenticated identities are authorized to access the requested debug endpoint.\n311: // Note: non-system namespace requests are further verified at connection time to ensure same-namespace proxy access only.\n312: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AuthorizeDebugRequest(identities []string, req *http.Request) bool {\n313: \tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(identities)\n314: \n315: \t// deny if no valid identity found\n316: \tif namespace == \"\" {\n317: \t\treturn false\n318: \t}\n319: \n320: \t// get system namespace (istio-system by default, or mesh root namespace)\n321: \tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\n322: \tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \"\" {\n323: \t\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\n324: \t}\n325: \n326: \t// allow all if identity is from system namespace\n327: \tif namespace == systemNamespace {\n328: \t\treturn true\n329: \t}\n330: \n331: \t// non-system namespace: only allow specific endpoints\n332: \tdebugPath := strings.TrimPrefix(req.URL.Path, \"/debug/\")\n333: \t_, allowed := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugPath]\n334: \treturn allowed\n335: }\n336: \n337: // Syncz dumps the synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\n338: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Syncz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n339: \tnamespace := req.URL.Query().Get(\"namespace\")\n340: \n341: \tsyncz := make([]SyncStatus, 0)\n342: \tfor _, con := range s.SortedClients() {\n343: \t\tnode := con.proxy\n344: \t\tif node != nil && (namespace == \"\" || node.GetNamespace() == namespace) {\n345: \t\t\twrs := node.DeepCloneWatchedResources()\n346: \t\t\tres := make(map[string]ResourceStatus, len(wrs))\n347: \t\t\tfor _, wr := range wrs {\n348: \t\t\t\tres[wr.TypeUrl] = ResourceStatus{\n349: \t\t\t\t\tSent:      wr.NonceSent,\n350: \t\t\t\t\tAcked:     wr.NonceAcked,\n351: \t\t\t\t\tSentTime:  wr.LastSendTime,\n352: \t\t\t\t\tLastError: wr.LastError,\n353: \t\t\t\t}\n354: \t\t\t}\n355: \t\t\tsyncz = append(syncz, SyncStatus{\n356: \t\t\t\tProxyID:              node.ID,\n357: \t\t\t\tProxyType:            node.Type,\n358: \t\t\t\tClusterID:            node.GetClusterID().String(),\n359: \t\t\t\tIstioVersion:         node.GetIstioVersion(),\n360: \t\t\t\tClusterSent:          node.NonceSent(v3.ClusterType),\n361: \t\t\t\tClusterAcked:         node.NonceAcked(v3.ClusterType),\n362: \t\t\t\tListenerSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.ListenerType),\n363: \t\t\t\tListenerAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.ListenerType),\n364: \t\t\t\tRouteSent:            node.NonceSent(v3.RouteType),\n365: \t\t\t\tRouteAcked:           node.NonceAcked(v3.RouteType),\n366: \t\t\t\tEndpointSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.EndpointType),\n367: \t\t\t\tEndpointAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.EndpointType),\n368: \t\t\t\tExtensionConfigSent:  node.NonceSent(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\n369: \t\t\t\tExtensionConfigAcked: node.NonceAcked(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\n370: \t\t\t\tResources:            res,\n371: \t\t\t})\n372: \t\t}\n373: \t}\n374: \twriteJSON(w, syncz, req)\n375: }\n376: \n377: // registryz providees debug support for registry - adding and listing model items.\n378: // Can be combined with the push debug interface to reproduce changes.\n379: func (s *DiscoveryServer) registryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n380: \tall := s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Services()\n381: \twriteJSON(w, all, req)\n382: }\n383: \n384: // Dumps info about the endpoint shards, tracked using the new direct interface.\n385: // Legacy registry provides are synced to the new data structure as well, during\n386: // the full push.\n387: func (s *DiscoveryServer) endpointShardz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n388: \twriteJSON(w, s.Env.EndpointIndex.Shardz(), req)\n389: }\n390: \n391: func (s *DiscoveryServer) cachez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n392: \tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n393: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n394: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Failed to parse request\\n\"))\n395: \t\treturn\n396: \t}\n397: \tif req.Form.Get(\"clear\") != \"\" {\n398: \t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n399: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Cache cleared\\n\"))\n400: \t\treturn\n401: \t}\n402: \tif req.Form.Get(\"sizes\") != \"\" {\n403: \t\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\n404: \t\traw := make(map[string]int, len(snapshot))\n405: \t\ttotalSize := 0\n406: \t\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\n407: \t\t\tif resource == nil {\n408: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n409: \t\t\t}\n410: \t\t\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\n411: \t\t\tsz := len(resource.Resource.GetValue())\n412: \t\t\traw[resourceType] += sz\n413: \t\t\ttotalSize += sz\n414: \t\t}\n415: \t\tres := make(map[string]string, len(raw))\n416: \t\tfor k, v := range raw {\n417: \t\t\tres[k] = util.ByteCount(v)\n418: \t\t}\n419: \t\tres[\"total\"] = util.ByteCount(totalSize)\n420: \t\twriteJSON(w, res, req)\n421: \t\treturn\n422: \t}\n423: \tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\n424: \tresources := make(map[string][]string, len(snapshot)) // Key is typeUrl and value is resource names.\n425: \tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\n426: \t\tif resource == nil {\n427: \t\t\tcontinue\n428: \t\t}\n429: \t\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\n430: \t\tresources[resourceType] = append(resources[resourceType], resource.Name)\n431: \t}\n432: \twriteJSON(w, resources, req)\n433: }\n434: \n435: // kubernetesConfig wraps a config.Config with a custom marshaling method that matches a Kubernetes\n436: // object structure.\n437: type kubernetesConfig struct {\n438: \tconfig.Config\n439: }\n440: \n441: func (k kubernetesConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\n442: \tcfg, err := crd.ConvertConfig(k.Config)\n443: \tif err != nil {\n444: \t\treturn nil, err\n445: \t}\n446: \treturn json.Marshal(cfg)\n447: }\n448: \n449: // Config debugging.\n450: func (s *DiscoveryServer) configz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n451: \tconfigs := make([]kubernetesConfig, 0)\n452: \tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.ConfigStore == nil {\n453: \t\treturn\n454: \t}\n455: \ts.Env.ConfigStore.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\n456: \t\tcfg := s.Env.ConfigStore.List(schema.GroupVersionKind(), \"\")\n457: \t\tfor _, c := range cfg {\n458: \t\t\tconfigs = append(configs, kubernetesConfig{c})\n459: \t\t}\n460: \t\treturn false\n461: \t})\n462: \twriteJSON(w, configs, req)\n463: }\n464: \n465: // SidecarScope debugging\n466: func (s *DiscoveryServer) sidecarz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n467: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n468: \tif con == nil {\n469: \t\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\n470: \t\treturn\n471: \t}\n472: \twriteJSON(w, con.proxy.SidecarScope, req)\n473: }\n474: \n475: // Resource debugging.\n476: func (s *DiscoveryServer) resourcez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n477: \tschemas := make([]config.GroupVersionKind, 0)\n478: \n479: \tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.ConfigStore != nil {\n480: \t\ts.Env.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\n481: \t\t\tschemas = append(schemas, schema.GroupVersionKind())\n482: \t\t\treturn false\n483: \t\t})\n484: \t}\n485: \n486: \twriteJSON(w, schemas, req)\n487: }\n488: \n489: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\n490: type AuthorizationDebug struct {\n491: \tAuthorizationPolicies *model.AuthorizationPolicies `json:\"authorization_policies\"`\n492: }\n493: \n494: // authorizationz dumps the internal authorization policies.\n495: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authorizationz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n496: \tinfo := AuthorizationDebug{\n497: \t\tAuthorizationPolicies: s.globalPushContext().AuthzPolicies,\n498: \t}\n499: \twriteJSON(w, info, req)\n500: }\n501: \n502: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\n503: type TelemetryDebug struct {\n504: \tTelemetries *model.Telemetries `json:\"telemetries\"`\n505: }\n506: \n507: func (s *DiscoveryServer) telemetryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n508: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n509: \tif proxyID != \"\" && con == nil {\n510: \t\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\n511: \t\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\n512: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n513: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\n\"))\n514: \t\treturn\n515: \t}\n516: \tif con == nil {\n517: \t\tinfo := TelemetryDebug{\n518: \t\t\tTelemetries: s.globalPushContext().Telemetry,\n519: \t\t}\n520: \t\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\n521: \t\treturn\n522: \t}\n523: \twriteJSON(w, s.globalPushContext().Telemetry.Debug(con.proxy), req)\n524: }\n525: \n526: // connectionsHandler implements interface for displaying current connections.\n527: // It is mapped to /debug/connections.\n528: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionsHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n529: \tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\n530: \tconnections := s.SortedClients()\n531: \tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\n532: \n533: \tfor _, c := range connections {\n534: \t\tadsClient := AdsClient{\n535: \t\t\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\n536: \t\t\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\n537: \t\t\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\n538: \t\t}\n539: \t\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\n540: \t}\n541: \twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\n542: }\n543: \n544: // adsz implements a status and debug interface for ADS.\n545: // It is mapped to /debug/adsz\n546: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n547: \tif s.handlePushRequest(w, req) {\n548: \t\treturn\n549: \t}\n550: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n551: \tif proxyID != \"\" && con == nil {\n552: \t\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\n553: \t\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\n554: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n555: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\n\"))\n556: \t\treturn\n557: \t}\n558: \tvar connections []*Connection\n559: \tif con != nil {\n560: \t\tconnections = []*Connection{con}\n561: \t} else {\n562: \t\tconnections = s.SortedClients()\n563: \t}\n564: \n565: \tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\n566: \tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\n567: \tfor _, c := range connections {\n568: \t\tadsClient := AdsClient{\n569: \t\t\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\n570: \t\t\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\n571: \t\t\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\n572: \t\t\tLabels:       c.proxy.Labels,\n573: \t\t\tMetadata:     c.proxy.Metadata,\n574: \t\t\tLocality:     c.proxy.Locality,\n575: \t\t\tWatches:      map[string][]string{},\n576: \t\t}\n577: \t\tc.proxy.RLock()\n578: \t\tfor k, wr := range c.proxy.WatchedResources {\n579: \t\t\tadsClient.Watches[k] = wr.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()\n580: \t\t}\n581: \t\tc.proxy.RUnlock()\n582: \t\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\n583: \t}\n584: \twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\n585: }\n586: \n587: func (s *DiscoveryServer) typedConfigDumpHandler(typ string) http.HandlerFunc {\n588: \treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n589: \t\tq := r.URL.Query()\n590: \t\tq.Set(\"types\", typ)\n591: \t\tr.URL.RawQuery = q.Encode()\n592: \t\ts.ConfigDump(w, r)\n593: \t}\n594: }\n595: \n596: // ConfigDump returns information in the form of the Envoy admin API config dump for the specified proxy\n597: // The dump will only contain dynamic listeners/clusters/routes and can be used to compare what an Envoy instance\n598: // should look like according to Pilot vs what it currently does look like.\n599: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigDump(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n600: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n601: \tif con == nil {\n602: \t\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\n603: \t\treturn\n604: \t}\n605: \tif ts := s.getResourceTypes(req); len(ts) != 0 {\n606: \t\tresources := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(con, nil, ts)\n607: \t\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{}\n608: \t\tfor _, resource := range resources {\n609: \t\t\tfor _, rr := range resource {\n610: \t\t\t\tconfigDump.Configs = append(configDump.Configs, rr.Resource)\n611: \t\t\t}\n612: \t\t}\n613: \t\twriteJSON(w, configDump, req)\n614: \t\treturn\n615: \t}\n616: \n617: \tif con.proxy.IsZTunnel() {\n618: \t\tresources := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(con, nil, []string{v3.AddressType})\n619: \t\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{}\n620: \t\tfor _, resource := range resources {\n621: \t\t\tfor _, rr := range resource {\n622: \t\t\t\tconfigDump.Configs = append(configDump.Configs, rr.Resource)\n623: \t\t\t}\n624: \t\t}\n625: \t\twriteJSON(w, configDump, req)\n626: \t\treturn\n627: \t}\n628: \n629: \tincludeEds := req.URL.Query().Has(\"include_eds\")\n630: \tdump, err := s.connectionConfigDump(con, includeEds)\n631: \tif err != nil {\n632: \t\thandleHTTPError(w, err)\n633: \t\treturn\n634: \t}\n635: \twriteJSON(w, dump, req)\n636: }\n637: \n638: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getResourceTypes(req *http.Request) []string {\n639: \tif shortTypes := req.URL.Query().Get(\"types\"); shortTypes != \"\" {\n640: \t\tts := strings.Split(shortTypes, \",\")\n641: \n642: \t\tresourceTypes := sets.New[string]()\n643: \t\tfor _, t := range ts {\n644: \t\t\trType := v3.GetResourceType(t)\n645: \t\t\tresourceTypes.Insert(rType)\n646: \t\t\t// special case for AddressType, include WorkloadType as well\n647: \t\t\t// because they shared same short type name `WDS`\n648: \t\t\tif rType == v3.AddressType {\n649: \t\t\t\tresourceTypes.Insert(v3.WorkloadType)\n650: \t\t\t}\n651: \t\t}\n652: \n653: \t\treturn resourceTypes.UnsortedList()\n654: \t}\n655: \treturn nil\n656: }\n657: \n658: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn *Connection, req *model.PushRequest, ts []string) map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource {\n659: \tdumps := make(map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource)\n660: \tif req == nil {\n661: \t\treq = &model.PushRequest{Push: conn.proxy.LastPushContext, Start: time.Now(), Full: true, Forced: true}\n662: \t}\n663: \n664: \tfor _, resourceType := range ts {\n665: \t\tw := conn.proxy.GetWatchedResource(resourceType)\n666: \t\tif w == nil {\n667: \t\t\t// Not watched, skip\n668: \t\t\tcontinue\n669: \t\t}\n670: \t\tgen := s.findGenerator(resourceType, conn)\n671: \t\tif gen == nil {\n672: \t\t\t// No generator found, skip\n673: \t\t\tcontinue\n674: \t\t}\n675: \t\tif resource, _, err := gen.Generate(conn.proxy, w, req); err == nil {\n676: \t\t\tfor _, rr := range resource {\n677: \t\t\t\tswitch resourceType {\n678: \t\t\t\tcase v3.SecretType:\n679: \t\t\t\t\t// Secrets must be redacted\n680: \t\t\t\t\tsecret := &tls.Secret{}\n681: \t\t\t\t\tif err := rr.Resource.UnmarshalTo(secret); err != nil {\n682: \t\t\t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"failed to unmarshal secret: %v\", err)\n683: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n684: \t\t\t\t\t}\n685: \t\t\t\t\tif secret.GetTlsCertificate() != nil {\n686: \t\t\t\t\t\t// When utilizing XDS caching, the resource object is shared, so modifying this would modify the cached item\n687: \t\t\t\t\t\t// Make a clone\n688: \t\t\t\t\t\trr = protomarshal.Clone(rr)\n689: \t\t\t\t\t\tsecret.GetTlsCertificate().PrivateKey = &core.DataSource{\n690: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tSpecifier: &core.DataSource_InlineBytes{\n691: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tInlineBytes: []byte(\"[redacted]\"),\n692: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n693: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n694: \t\t\t\t\t}\n695: \t\t\t\t\trr.Resource = protoconv.MessageToAny(secret)\n696: \t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n697: \t\t\t\tcase v3.ExtensionConfigurationType:\n698: \t\t\t\t\ttce := &core.TypedExtensionConfig{}\n699: \t\t\t\t\tif err := rr.GetResource().UnmarshalTo(tce); err != nil {\n700: \t\t\t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"failed to unmarshal extension: %v\", err)\n701: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n702: \t\t\t\t\t}\n703: \n704: \t\t\t\t\tswitch tce.TypedConfig.TypeUrl {\n705: \t\t\t\t\tcase xds.WasmHTTPFilterType:\n706: \t\t\t\t\t\tw := &wasm.Wasm{}\n707: \t\t\t\t\t\tif err := tce.TypedConfig.UnmarshalTo(w); err != nil {\n708: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"failed to unmarshal wasm filter: %v\", err)\n709: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n710: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n711: \t\t\t\t\t\t// Redact Wasm secret env variable.\n712: \t\t\t\t\t\tvmenvs := w.GetConfig().GetVmConfig().EnvironmentVariables\n713: \t\t\t\t\t\tif vmenvs != nil {\n714: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tif _, found := vmenvs.KeyValues[model.WasmSecretEnv]; found {\n715: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tvmenvs.KeyValues[model.WasmSecretEnv] = \"<Redacted>\"\n716: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n717: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n718: \t\t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], &discoveryv3.Resource{\n719: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tName:     w.Config.Name,\n720: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(w),\n721: \t\t\t\t\t\t})\n722: \t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n723: \t\t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n724: \t\t\t\t\t}\n725: \t\t\t\tdefault:\n726: \t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n727: \t\t\t\t}\n728: \t\t\t}\n729: \t\t} else {\n730: \t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"generate failed for request resource type (%v): %v\", resourceType, err)\n731: \t\t\tcontinue\n732: \t\t}\n733: \t}\n734: \n735: \treturn dumps\n736: }\n737: \n738: // connectionConfigDump converts the connection internal state into an Envoy Admin API config dump proto\n739: // It is used in debugging to create a consistent object for comparison between Envoy and Pilot outputs\n740: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionConfigDump(conn *Connection, includeEds bool) (*admin.ConfigDump, error) {\n741: \treq := &model.PushRequest{Push: conn.proxy.LastPushContext, Start: time.Now(), Full: true, Forced: true}\n742: \tversion := req.Push.PushVersion\n743: \n744: \tdump := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn, req, []string{\n745: \t\tv3.ClusterType,\n746: \t\tv3.ListenerType,\n747: \t\tv3.RouteType,\n748: \t\tv3.SecretType,\n749: \t\tv3.EndpointType,\n750: \t\tv3.ExtensionConfigurationType,\n751: \t})\n752: \n753: \tdynamicActiveClusters := make([]*admin.ClustersConfigDump_DynamicCluster, 0)\n754: \tfor _, cluster := range dump[v3.ClusterType] {\n755: \t\tdynamicActiveClusters = append(dynamicActiveClusters, &admin.ClustersConfigDump_DynamicCluster{\n756: \t\t\tCluster: cluster.Resource,\n757: \t\t})\n758: \t}\n759: \tclustersAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.ClustersConfigDump{\n760: \t\tVersionInfo:           version,\n761: \t\tDynamicActiveClusters: dynamicActiveClusters,\n762: \t})\n763: \tif err != nil {\n764: \t\treturn nil, err\n765: \t}\n766: \n767: \tdynamicActiveListeners := make([]*admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListener, 0)\n768: \tfor _, listener := range dump[v3.ListenerType] {\n769: \t\tdynamicActiveListeners = append(dynamicActiveListeners, &admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListener{\n770: \t\t\tName: listener.Name,\n771: \t\t\tActiveState: &admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListenerState{\n772: \t\t\t\tListener:    listener.Resource,\n773: \t\t\t\tVersionInfo: version,\n774: \t\t\t},\n775: \t\t})\n776: \t}\n777: \tlistenersAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.ListenersConfigDump{\n778: \t\tVersionInfo:      version,\n779: \t\tDynamicListeners: dynamicActiveListeners,\n780: \t})\n781: \tif err != nil {\n782: \t\treturn nil, err\n783: \t}\n784: \n785: \tdynamicRouteConfig := make([]*admin.RoutesConfigDump_DynamicRouteConfig, 0)\n786: \tfor _, route := range dump[v3.RouteType] {\n787: \t\tdynamicRouteConfig = append(dynamicRouteConfig, &admin.RoutesConfigDump_DynamicRouteConfig{\n788: \t\t\tVersionInfo: version,\n789: \t\t\tRouteConfig: route.Resource,\n790: \t\t})\n791: \t}\n792: \troutesAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.RoutesConfigDump{\n793: \t\tDynamicRouteConfigs: dynamicRouteConfig,\n794: \t})\n795: \tif err != nil {\n796: \t\treturn nil, err\n797: \t}\n798: \n799: \tdynamicSecretsConfig := make([]*admin.SecretsConfigDump_DynamicSecret, 0)\n800: \tfor _, secret := range dump[v3.SecretType] {\n801: \t\tdynamicSecretsConfig = append(dynamicSecretsConfig, &admin.SecretsConfigDump_DynamicSecret{\n802: \t\t\tVersionInfo: version,\n803: \t\t\tSecret:      secret.Resource,\n804: \t\t})\n805: \t}\n806: \tsecretsAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.SecretsConfigDump{\n807: \t\tDynamicActiveSecrets: dynamicSecretsConfig,\n808: \t})\n809: \tif err != nil {\n810: \t\treturn nil, err\n811: \t}\n812: \n813: \textensionsConfig := make([]*admin.EcdsConfigDump_EcdsFilterConfig, 0)\n814: \tfor _, ext := range dump[v3.ExtensionConfigurationType] {\n815: \t\textensionsConfig = append(extensionsConfig, &admin.EcdsConfigDump_EcdsFilterConfig{\n816: \t\t\tVersionInfo: version,\n817: \t\t\tEcdsFilter:  ext.Resource,\n818: \t\t})\n819: \t}\n820: \textensionsAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.EcdsConfigDump{\n821: \t\tEcdsFilters: extensionsConfig,\n822: \t})\n823: \tif err != nil {\n824: \t\treturn nil, err\n825: \t}\n826: \n827: \tvar endpointsAny *anypb.Any\n828: \t// EDS is disabled by default for compatibility with Envoy config_dump interface\n829: \tif includeEds {\n830: \t\tendpointConfig := make([]*admin.EndpointsConfigDump_DynamicEndpointConfig, 0)\n831: \t\tfor _, endpoint := range dump[v3.EndpointType] {\n832: \t\t\tendpointConfig = append(endpointConfig, &admin.EndpointsConfigDump_DynamicEndpointConfig{\n833: \t\t\t\tVersionInfo:    version,\n834: \t\t\t\tEndpointConfig: endpoint.Resource,\n835: \t\t\t})\n836: \t\t}\n837: \t\tendpointsAny, err = protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.EndpointsConfigDump{\n838: \t\t\tDynamicEndpointConfigs: endpointConfig,\n839: \t\t})\n840: \t\tif err != nil {\n841: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n842: \t\t}\n843: \t}\n844: \n845: \tbootstrapAny := protoconv.MessageToAny(&admin.BootstrapConfigDump{})\n846: \tscopedRoutesAny := protoconv.MessageToAny(&admin.ScopedRoutesConfigDump{})\n847: \t// The config dump must have all configs with connections specified in\n848: \t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v2/admin/v2alpha/config_dump.proto\n849: \tconfigs := []*anypb.Any{\n850: \t\tbootstrapAny,\n851: \t\tclustersAny,\n852: \t}\n853: \tif includeEds {\n854: \t\tconfigs = append(configs, endpointsAny)\n855: \t}\n856: \tconfigs = append(configs,\n857: \t\tlistenersAny,\n858: \t\tscopedRoutesAny,\n859: \t\troutesAny,\n860: \t\tsecretsAny,\n861: \t\textensionsAny,\n862: \t)\n863: \tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{\n864: \t\tConfigs: configs,\n865: \t}\n866: \treturn configDump, nil\n867: }\n868: \n869: // injectTemplateHandler dumps the injection template\n870: // Replaces dumping the template at startup.\n871: func (s *DiscoveryServer) injectTemplateHandler(webhook func() map[string]string) func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request) {\n872: \treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n873: \t\t// TODO: we should split the inject template into smaller modules (separate one for dump core, etc),\n874: \t\t// and allow pods to select which patches will be selected. When this happen, this should return\n875: \t\t// all inject templates or take a param to select one.\n876: \t\tif webhook == nil {\n877: \t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n878: \t\t\treturn\n879: \t\t}\n880: \n881: \t\twriteJSON(w, webhook(), req)\n882: \t}\n883: }\n884: \n885: // meshHandler dumps the mesh config\n886: func (s *DiscoveryServer) meshHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n887: \twriteJSON(w, s.Env.Mesh(), req)\n888: }\n889: \n890: // pushStatusHandler dumps the last PushContext\n891: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushStatusHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n892: \tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\n893: \tdefer model.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\n894: \tif model.LastPushStatus == nil {\n895: \t\treturn\n896: \t}\n897: \tout, err := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()\n898: \tif err != nil {\n899: \t\thandleHTTPError(w, err)\n900: \t\treturn\n901: \t}\n902: \tw.Header().Add(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n903: \n904: \t_, _ = w.Write(out)\n905: }\n906: \n907: // PushContextDebug holds debug information for push context.\n908: type PushContextDebug struct {\n909: \tAuthorizationPolicies *model.AuthorizationPolicies\n910: \tNetworkGateways       []model.NetworkGateway\n911: \tUnresolvedGateways    []model.NetworkGateway\n912: }\n913: \n914: // pushContextHandler dumps the current PushContext\n915: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushContextHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n916: \tpush := PushContextDebug{}\n917: \tpc := s.globalPushContext()\n918: \tif pc == nil {\n919: \t\treturn\n920: \t}\n921: \tpush.AuthorizationPolicies = pc.AuthzPolicies\n922: \tif pc.NetworkManager() != nil {\n923: \t\tpush.NetworkGateways = pc.NetworkManager().AllGateways()\n924: \t\tpush.UnresolvedGateways = pc.NetworkManager().Unresolved.AllGateways()\n925: \t}\n926: \n927: \twriteJSON(w, push, req)\n928: }\n929: \n930: // DebugEndpoints lists all the supported debug endpoints.\n931: func (s *DiscoveryServer) DebugEndpoints() []string {\n932: \treturn slices.Sort(maps.Keys(s.debugHandlers))\n933: }\n934: \n935: // Debug lists all the supported debug endpoints.\n936: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Debug(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n937: \ttype debugEndpoint struct {\n938: \t\tName string\n939: \t\tHref string\n940: \t\tHelp string\n941: \t}\n942: \tvar deps []debugEndpoint\n943: \tfor k, v := range s.debugHandlers {\n944: \t\tdeps = append(deps, debugEndpoint{\n945: \t\t\tName: k,\n946: \t\t\tHref: k,\n947: \t\t\tHelp: v,\n948: \t\t})\n949: \t}\n950: \n951: \tsort.Slice(deps, func(i, j int) bool {\n952: \t\treturn deps[i].Name < deps[j].Name\n953: \t})\n954: \n955: \tif err := indexTmpl.Execute(w, deps); err != nil {\n956: \t\tistiolog.Errorf(\"Error in rendering index template %v\", err)\n957: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n958: \t}\n959: }\n960: \n961: // list all the supported debug commands in json.\n962: func (s *DiscoveryServer) list(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n963: \tvar cmdNames []string\n964: \tfor k := range s.debugHandlers {\n965: \t\tkey := strings.Replace(k, \"/debug/\", \"\", -1)\n966: \t\t// exclude current list command\n967: \t\tif key == \"list\" {\n968: \t\t\tcontinue\n969: \t\t}\n970: \t\t// can not support pprof commands\n971: \t\tif strings.Contains(key, \"pprof\") {\n972: \t\t\tcontinue\n973: \t\t}\n974: \t\tcmdNames = append(cmdNames, key)\n975: \t}\n976: \tsort.Strings(cmdNames)\n977: \twriteJSON(w, cmdNames, req)\n978: }\n979: \n980: func (s *DiscoveryServer) forceDisconnect(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n981: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n982: \tif con == nil {\n983: \t\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\n984: \t\treturn\n985: \t}\n986: \tcon.Stop()\n987: \t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n988: }\n989: \n990: func cloneProxy(proxy *model.Proxy) *model.Proxy {\n991: \tif proxy == nil {\n992: \t\treturn nil\n993: \t}\n994: \n995: \tproxy.Lock()\n996: \tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n997: \t// nolint: govet\n998: \tcopied := *proxy\n999: \tout := &copied\n1000: \tout.RWMutex = sync.RWMutex{}\n1001: \t// clone WatchedResources which can be mutated when processing request\n1002: \tout.WatchedResources = make(map[string]*model.WatchedResource, len(proxy.WatchedResources))\n1003: \tfor k, v := range proxy.WatchedResources {\n1004: \t\t// nolint: govet\n1005: \t\tv := *v\n1006: \t\tv.ResourceNames = v.ResourceNames.Copy()\n1007: \t\tout.WatchedResources[k] = &v\n1008: \t}\n1009: \treturn out\n1010: }\n1011: \n1012: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getProxyConnection(proxyID string) *Connection {\n1013: \tfor _, con := range s.Clients() {\n1014: \t\tif strings.Contains(con.ID(), proxyID) {\n1015: \t\t\tout := *con\n1016: \t\t\tout.proxy = cloneProxy(con.proxy)\n1017: \t\t\treturn &out\n1018: \t\t}\n1019: \t}\n1020: \n1021: \treturn nil\n1022: }\n1023: \n1024: func (s *DiscoveryServer) instancesz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1025: \tinstances := map[string][]model.ServiceTarget{}\n1026: \tfor _, con := range s.Clients() {\n1027: \t\tcon.proxy.RLock()\n1028: \t\tif con.proxy != nil {\n1029: \t\t\tinstances[con.proxy.ID] = con.proxy.ServiceTargets\n1030: \t\t}\n1031: \t\tcon.proxy.RUnlock()\n1032: \t}\n1033: \twriteJSON(w, instances, req)\n1034: }\n1035: \n1036: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ambientz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1037: \taddresses, _ := s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(nil)\n1038: \tres := struct {\n1039: \t\tWorkloads []jsonMarshalProto `json:\"workloads\"`\n1040: \t\tServices  []jsonMarshalProto `json:\"services\"`\n1041: \t\tPolicies  []jsonMarshalProto `json:\"policies\"`\n1042: \t}{}\n1043: \t// WDS stores IPs as raw byte form. We want to view them as strings, so convert.\n1044: \t// This doesn't quite work ideally, since json marshal will write as base64, but its better than nothing\n1045: \trewriteAddress := func(b []byte) []byte {\n1046: \t\tip, ok := netip.AddrFromSlice(b)\n1047: \t\tif !ok {\n1048: \t\t\treturn b\n1049: \t\t}\n1050: \t\treturn []byte(ip.String())\n1051: \t}\n1052: \trewriteNetworkAddress := func(b
