*workloadapi.NetworkAddress) *workloadapi.NetworkAddress {\n1053: \t\tnb := protomarshal.Clone(b)\n1054: \t\tnb.Address = rewriteAddress(nb.Address)\n1055: \t\treturn nb\n1056: \t}\n1057: \trewriteGatewayAddress := func(b *workloadapi.GatewayAddress) *workloadapi.GatewayAddress {\n1058: \t\tif b == nil {\n1059: \t\t\treturn nil\n1060: \t\t}\n1061: \t\tnb := protomarshal.Clone(b)\n1062: \t\tswitch t := nb.Destination.(type) {\n1063: \t\tcase *workloadapi.GatewayAddress_Address:\n1064: \t\t\tt.Address = rewriteNetworkAddress(t.Address)\n1065: \t\t}\n1066: \t\treturn nb\n1067: \t}\n1068: \tfor _, original := range addresses {\n1069: \t\taddr := protomarshal.Clone(original.Address)\n1070: \t\tswitch addr := addr.Type.(type) {\n1071: \t\tcase *workloadapi.Address_Workload:\n1072: \t\t\tw := addr.Workload\n1073: \t\t\tw.Addresses = slices.Map(w.Addresses, rewriteAddress)\n1074: \t\t\tw.Waypoint = rewriteGatewayAddress(w.Waypoint)\n1075: \t\t\tw.NetworkGateway = rewriteGatewayAddress(w.NetworkGateway)\n1076: \t\t\tres.Workloads = append(res.Workloads, jsonMarshalProto{w})\n1077: \t\tcase *workloadapi.Address_Service:\n1078: \t\t\ts := addr.Service\n1079: \t\t\ts.Addresses = slices.Map(s.Addresses, rewriteNetworkAddress)\n1080: \t\t\ts.Waypoint = rewriteGatewayAddress(s.Waypoint)\n1081: \t\t\tres.Services = append(res.Services, jsonMarshalProto{s})\n1082: \t\t}\n1083: \t}\n1084: \tfor _, policy := range s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(nil) {\n1085: \t\tres.Policies = append(res.Policies, jsonMarshalProto{policy.Authorization})\n1086: \t}\n1087: \twriteJSON(w, res, req)\n1088: }\n1089: \n1090: func (s *DiscoveryServer) krtz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1091: \twriteJSON(w, s.krtDebugger, req)\n1092: }\n1093: \n1094: func (s *DiscoveryServer) networkz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1095: \tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.NetworkManager == nil {\n1096: \t\treturn\n1097: \t}\n1098: \twriteJSON(w, s.Env.NetworkManager.AllGateways(), req)\n1099: }\n1100: \n1101: func (s *DiscoveryServer) mcsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1102: \tsvcs := sortMCSServices(s.Env.MCSServices())\n1103: \twriteJSON(w, svcs, req)\n1104: }\n1105: \n1106: func sortMCSServices(svcs []model.MCSServiceInfo) []model.MCSServiceInfo {\n1107: \tsort.Slice(svcs, func(i, j int) bool {\n1108: \t\tif strings.Compare(svcs[i].Cluster.String(), svcs[j].Cluster.String()) < 0 {\n1109: \t\t\treturn true\n1110: \t\t}\n1111: \t\tif strings.Compare(svcs[i].Namespace, svcs[j].Namespace) < 0 {\n1112: \t\t\treturn true\n1113: \t\t}\n1114: \t\treturn strings.Compare(svcs[i].Name, svcs[j].Name) < 0\n1115: \t})\n1116: \treturn svcs\n1117: }\n1118: \n1119: func (s *DiscoveryServer) clusterz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1120: \tif s.ListRemoteClusters == nil {\n1121: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n1122: \t\treturn\n1123: \t}\n1124: \twriteJSON(w, s.ListRemoteClusters(), req)\n1125: }\n1126: \n1127: // handlePushRequest handles a ?push=true query param and triggers a push.\n1128: // A boolean response is returned to indicate if the caller should continue\n1129: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handlePushRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) bool {\n1130: \tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n1131: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n1132: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Failed to parse request\\n\"))\n1133: \t\treturn true\n1134: \t}\n1135: \tif req.Form.Get(\"push\") != \"\" {\n1136: \t\tAdsPushAll(s)\n1137: \t\t_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, \"Pushed to %d servers\\n\", s.adsClientCount())\n1138: \t\treturn true\n1139: \t}\n1140: \treturn false\n1141: }\n1142: \n1143: // getDebugConnection fetches the Connection requested by proxyID\n1144: // For non-system namespaces, restricts access to proxies in the caller's namespace only\n1145: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getDebugConnection(req *http.Request) (string, *Connection) {\n1146: \tif proxyID := req.URL.Query().Get(\"proxyID\"); proxyID != \"\" {\n1147: \t\tcon := s.getProxyConnection(proxyID)\n1148: \t\t// Verify namespace if caller is not from system namespace (when auth is enabled)\n1149: \t\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\n1150: \t\t\tcallerNamespace, _ := req.Context().Value(CallerNamespaceKey{}).(string)\n1151: \t\t\tif con != nil && callerNamespace != \"\" {\n1152: \t\t\t\tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\n1153: \t\t\t\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \"\" {\n1154: \t\t\t\t\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\n1155: \t\t\t\t}\n1156: \t\t\t\t// Non-system namespaces can only access proxies in their own namespace\n1157: \t\t\t\tif callerNamespace != systemNamespace && con.proxy.ConfigNamespace != callerNamespace {\n1158: \t\t\t\t\treturn proxyID, nil // Return nil connection to deny access\n1159: \t\t\t\t}\n1160: \t\t\t}\n1161: \t\t}\n1162: \t\treturn proxyID, con\n1163: \t}\n1164: \treturn \"\", nil\n1165: }\n1166: \n1167: func (s *DiscoveryServer) errorHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, proxyID string, con *Connection) {\n1168: \tif proxyID == \"\" {\n1169: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n1170: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"You must provide a proxyID in the query string\\n\"))\n1171: \t\treturn\n1172: \t}\n1173: \tif con == nil {\n1174: \t\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\n1175: \t\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\n1176: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n1177: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\n\"))\n1178: \t\treturn\n1179: \t}\n1180: }\n1181: \n1182: // jsonMarshalProto wraps a proto.Message so it can be marshaled with the standard encoding/json library\n1183: type jsonMarshalProto struct {\n1184: \tproto.Message\n1185: }\n1186: \n1187: func (p jsonMarshalProto) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\n1188: \treturn protomarshal.Marshal(p.Message)\n1189: }\n1190: \n1191: // writeJSON writes a json payload, handling content type, marshaling, and errors\n1192: func writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, obj any, req *http.Request) {\n1193: \tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n1194: \tvar b []byte\n1195: \tvar err error\n1196: \tif req.URL.Query().Has(\"pretty\") {\n1197: \t\tb, err = config.ToPrettyJSON(obj)\n1198: \t} else {\n1199: \t\tb, err = config.ToJSON(obj)\n1200: \t}\n1201: \tif err != nil {\n1202: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1203: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(err.Error()))\n1204: \t\treturn\n1205: \t}\n1206: \t_, err = w.Write(b)\n1207: \tif err != nil {\n1208: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1209: \t}\n1210: }\n1211: \n1212: // handleHTTPError writes an error message to the response\n1213: func handleHTTPError(w http.ResponseWriter, err error) {\n1214: \tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1215: \t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(err.Error()))\n1216: }\n1217: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/debug.go"
  }

activeNamespaceDebuggers = map[string]struct{}{\\n34: \\t\\\"config_dump\\\": {},\\n35: \\t\\\"ndsz\\\":        {},\\n36: \\t\\\"edsz\\\":        {},\\n37: }\\n38: \\n39: // DebugGen is a Generator for istio debug info\\n40: type DebugGen struct {\\n41: \\tServer          *DiscoveryServer\\n42: \\tSystemNamespace string\\n43: \\tDebugMux        *http.ServeMux\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: type ResponseCapture struct {\\n47: \\tbody        *bytes.Buffer\\n48: \\theader      map[string]string\\n49: \\twroteHeader bool\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: func (r ResponseCapture) Header() http.Header {\\n53: \\theader := make(http.Header)\\n54: \\tfor k, v := range r.header {\\n55: \\t\\theader.Set(k, v)\\n56: \\t}\\n57: \\treturn header\\n58: }\\n59: \\n60: func (r ResponseCapture) Write(i []byte) (int, error) {\\n61: \\treturn r.body.Write(i)\\n62: }\\n63: \\n64: func (r ResponseCapture) WriteHeader(statusCode int) {\\n65: \\tr.header[\\\"statusCode\\\"] = strconv.Itoa(statusCode)\\n66: }\\n67: \\n68: func NewResponseCapture() *ResponseCapture {\\n69: \\treturn &ResponseCapture{\\n70: \\t\\theader:      make(map[string]string),\\n71: \\t\\tbody:        new(bytes.Buffer),\\n72: \\t\\twroteHeader: false,\\n73: \\t}\\n74: }\\n75: \\n76: func NewDebugGen(s *DiscoveryServer, systemNamespace string, debugMux *http.ServeMux) *DebugGen {\\n77: \\treturn &DebugGen{\\n78: \\t\\tServer:          s,\\n79: \\t\\tSystemNamespace: systemNamespace,\\n80: \\t\\tDebugMux:        debugMux,\\n81: \\t}\\n82: }\\n83: \\n84: // Generate XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\\n85: func (dg *DebugGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n86: \\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\\n87: \\t\\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\\n88: \\t}\\n89: \\n90: \\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\\n91: \\tif err != nil {\\n92: \\t\\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\\n93: \\t}\\n94: \\n95: \\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\\n96: \\n97: \\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\\n98: \\t\\tName: resourceName,\\n99: \\t\\tResource: &anypb.Any{\\n100: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\\n101: \\t\\t\\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\\n102: \\t\\t},\\n103: \\t}}\\n104: \\treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: // GenerateDeltas XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\\n108: func (dg *DebugGen) GenerateDeltas(\\n109: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n110: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n111: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n112: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n113: \\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\\n114: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\\n115: \\t}\\n116: \\n117: \\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\\n118: \\tif err != nil {\\n119: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\\n120: \\t}\\n121: \\n122: \\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\\n123: \\n124: \\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\\n125: \\t\\tName: resourceName,\\n126: \\t\\tResource: &anypb.Any{\\n127: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\\n128: \\t\\t\\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\\n129: \\t\\t},\\n130: \\t}}\\n131: \\treturn res, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, nil\\n132: }\\n133: \\n134: func validateProxyAuthentication(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource) error {\\n135: \\tif proxy.VerifiedIdentity == nil {\\n136: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"proxy %s is not authorized to receive debug. Ensure you are connecting over TLS port and are authenticated.\\\", proxy.ID)\\n137: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, \\\"authentication required\\\")\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\tif w.ResourceNames == nil || len(w.ResourceNames) != 1 {\\n140: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, \\\"exactly one debug request is required\\\")\\n141: \\t}\\n142: \\treturn nil\\n143: }\\n144: \\n145: func parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, dg *DebugGen) (string, error) {\\n146: \\tresourceName := w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0]\\n147: \\tu, _ := url.Parse(resourceName)\\n148: \\tdebugType := u.Path\\n149: \\tidentity := proxy.VerifiedIdentity\\n150: \\tif identity.Namespace != dg.SystemNamespace {\\n151: \\t\\tif _, ok := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugType]; !ok {\\n152: \\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", status.Errorf(codes.PermissionDenied, \\\"the debug info is not available for current identity: %q\\\", identity)\\n153: \\t\\t}\\n154: \\t}\\n155: \\treturn resourceName, nil\\n156: }\\n157: \\n158: func processDebugRequest(dg *DebugGen, resourceName string) bytes.Buffer {\\n159: \\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\\n160: \\tdebugURL := \\\"/debug/\\\" + resourceName\\n161: \\threq, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, debugURL, nil)\\n162: \\thandler, _ := dg.DebugMux.Handler(hreq)\\n163: \\tresponse := NewResponseCapture()\\n164: \\thandler.ServeHTTP(response, hreq)\\n165: \\tif response.wroteHeader && len(response.header) >= 1 {\\n166: \\t\\theader, _ := json.Marshal(response.header)\\n167: \\t\\tbuffer.Write(header)\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\tbuffer.Write(response.body.Bytes())\\n170: \\treturn buffer\\n171: }\\n172: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/debuggen.go\"}",

\t\"net/url\"\n22: \t\"strconv\"\n23: \n24: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n25: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n26: )\n32: \n33: var activeNamespaceDebuggers = map[string]struct{}{\n34: \t\"config_dump\": {},\n35: \t\"ndsz\":        {},\n36: \t\"edsz\":        {},\n37: }\n38: \n39: // DebugGen is a Generator for istio debug info\n40: type DebugGen struct {\n41: \tServer          *DiscoveryServer\n42: \tSystemNamespace string\n43: \tDebugMux        *http.ServeMux\n44: }\n45: \n46: type ResponseCapture struct {\n47: \tbody        *bytes.Buffer\n48: \theader      map[string]string\n49: \twroteHeader bool\n50: }\n51: \n52: func (r ResponseCapture) Header() http.Header {\n53: \theader := r.body.Write(i)\n62: }\n63: \n64: func (r ResponseCapture) WriteHeader(statusCode int) {\n65: \tr.header[\"statusCode\"] = strconv.Itoa(statusCode)\n66: }\n67: \n68: func NewResponseCapture() *ResponseCapture {\n69: \treturn &ResponseCapture{\n70: \t\theader:      make(map[string]string),\n71: \t\tbody:        new(bytes.Buffer),\n72: \t\twroteHeader: false,\n73: \t}\n74: }\n75: \n76: func NewDebugGen(s *DiscoveryServer, systemNamespace string, debugMux *http.ServeMux) *DebugGen {\n77: \treturn &DebugGen{\n78: \t\tServer:          s,\n79: \t\tSystemNamespace: systemNamespace,\n80: \t\tDebugMux:        debugMux,\n81: \t}\n82: }\n83: \n84: // Generate XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\n85: func (dg *DebugGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n86: \tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n87: \t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n88: \t}\n89: \n90: \tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n91: \tif err != nil {\n92: \t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n93: \t}\n94: \n95: \tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n96: \n97: \tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n98: \t\tName: resourceName,\n99: \t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n100: \t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n101: \t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n102: \t\t},\n103: \t}}\n104: \treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n105: }\n106: \n107: // GenerateDeltas XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\n108: func (dg *DebugGen) GenerateDeltas(\n109: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n110: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n111: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n112: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n113: \tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n114: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n115: \t}\n116: \n117: \tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n118: \tif err != nil {\n119: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n120: \t}\n121: \n122: \tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n123: \n124: \tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n125: \t\tName: resourceName,\n126: \t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n127: \t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n128: \t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n129: \t\t},\n130: \t}}\n131: \treturn res, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, nil\n132: }\n133: \n134: func validateProxyAuthentication(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource) error {\n135: \tif proxy.VerifiedIdentity == nil {\n136: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"proxy %s is not authorized to receive debug. Ensure you are connecting over TLS port and are authenticated.\", proxy.ID)\n137: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, \"authentication required\")\n138: \t}\n139: \tif w.ResourceNames == nil || len(w.ResourceNames) != 1 {\n140: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, \"exactly one debug request is required\")\n141: \t}\n142: \treturn nil\n143: }\n144: \n145: func parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, dg *DebugGen) (string, error) {\n146: \tresourceName := w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0]\n147: \tu, _ := url.Parse(resourceName)\n148: \tdebugType := u.Path\n149: \tidentity := proxy.VerifiedIdentity\n150: \tif identity.Namespace != dg.SystemNamespace {\n151: \t\tif _, ok := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugType]; !ok {\n152: \t\t\treturn \"\", status.Errorf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"the debug info is not available for current identity: %q\", identity)\n153: \t\t}\n154: \t}\n155: \treturn resourceName, nil\n156: }\n157: \n158: func processDebugRequest(dg *DebugGen, resourceName string) bytes.Buffer {\n159: \tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n160: \tdebugURL := \"/debug/\" + resourceName\n161: \threq, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, debugURL, nil)\n162: \thandler, _ := dg.DebugMux.Handler(hreq)\n163: \tresponse := NewResponseCapture()\n164: \thandler.ServeHTTP(response, hreq)\n165: \tif response.wroteHeader && len(response.header) >= 1 {\n166: \t\theader, _ := json.Marshal(response.header)\n167: \t\tbuffer.Write(header)\n168: \t}\n169: \tbuffer.Write(response.body.Bytes())\n170: \treturn buffer\n171: }\n172: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/debuggen.go"

17. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// Copyright Istio Authors\n//\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage xds\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/security\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/spiffe\"\n)\n\n// authenticate authenticates the ADS request using the configured authenticators.\n// Returns the validated principals or an error.\n// If no authenticators are configured, or if the request is on a non-secure\n// stream ( 15010 ) - returns an empty list of principals and no errors.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) authenticate(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {\n\tc, err := security.Authenticate(ctx, s.Authenticators)\n\tif c != nil {\n\t\treturn c.Identities, nil\n\t}\n\treturn nil, err\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) authorize(con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\tif con == nil || con.proxy == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif features.EnableXDSIdentityCheck && identities != nil {\n\t\t// TODO: allow locking down, rejecting unauthenticated requests.\n\t\tid, err := checkConnectionIdentity(con.proxy, identities)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Unauthorized XDS: %v with identity %v: %v\", con.Peer(), identities, err)\n\t\t\treturn status.Newf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"authorization failed: %v\", err).Err()\n\t\t}\n\t\tcon.proxy.VerifiedIdentity = id\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc checkConnectionIdentity(proxy *model.Proxy, identities []string) (*spiffe.Identity, error) {\n\tfor _, rawID := range identities {\n\t\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(rawID)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif proxy.ConfigNamespace != \"\" && spiffeID.Namespace != proxy.ConfigNamespace {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount != \"\" && spiffeID.ServiceAccount != proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn &spiffeID, nil\n\t}\n\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"no identities (%v) matched %v/%v\", identities, proxy.ConfigNamespace, proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount)\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/pilot/pkg/xds/auth.go"
}
Output
{
  "content": "// Copyright Istio Authors\n//\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage xds\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/security\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/spiffe\"\n)\n\n// authenticate authenticates the ADS request using the configured authenticators.\n// Returns the validated principals or an error.\n// If no authenticators are configured, or if the request is on a non-secure\n// stream ( 15010 ) - returns an empty list of principals and no errors.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) authenticate(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {\n\tc, err := security.Authenticate(ctx, s.Authenticators)\n\tif c != nil {\n\t\treturn c.Identities, nil\n\t}\n\treturn nil, err\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) authorize(con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\tif con == nil || con.proxy == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif features.EnableXDSIdentityCheck && identities != nil {\n\t\t// TODO: allow locking down, rejecting unauthenticated requests.\n\t\tid, err := checkConnectionIdentity(con.proxy, identities)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Unauthorized XDS: %v with identity %v: %v\", con.Peer(), identities, err)\n\t\t\treturn status.Newf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"authorization failed: %v\", err).Err()\n\t\t}\n\t\tcon.proxy.VerifiedIdentity = id\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc checkConnectionIdentity(proxy *model.Proxy, identities []string) (*spiffe.Identity, error) {\n\tfor _, rawID := range identities {\n\t\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(rawID)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif proxy.ConfigNamespace != \"\" && spiffeID.Namespace != proxy.ConfigNamespace {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount != \"\" && spiffeID.ServiceAccount != proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn &spiffeID, nil\n\t}\n\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"no identities (%v) matched %v/%v\", identities, proxy.ConfigNamespace, proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount)\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/pilot/pkg/xds/auth.go",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}
18. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// Copyright Istio Authors\n//\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage xds\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\n\tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n\tanypb \"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/anypb\"\n\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n\tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n)\n\nvar activeNamespaceDebuggers = map[string]struct{}{\n\t\"config_dump\": {},\n\t\"ndsz\":        {},\n\t\"edsz\":        {},\n}\n\n// DebugGen is a Generator for istio debug info\ntype DebugGen struct {\n\tServer          *XDSServer\n\tSystemNamespace string\n\tDebugMux        *http.ServeMux\n}\n\ntype ResponseCapture struct {\n\tbody        *bytes.Buffer\n\theader      map[string]string\n\twroteHeader bool\n}\n\nfunc (r ResponseCapture) Header() http.Header {\n\theader := make(http.Header)\n\tfor k, v := range r.header {\n\t\theader.Set(k, v)\n\t}\n\treturn header\n}\n\nfunc (r ResponseCapture) Write(i []byte) (int, error) {\n\treturn r.body.Write(i)\n}\n\nfunc (r ResponseCapture) WriteHeader(statusCode int) {\n\tr.header[\"statusCode\"] = strconv.Itoa(statusCode)\n}\n\nfunc NewResponseCapture() *ResponseCapture {\n\treturn &ResponseCapture{\n\t\theader:      make(map[string]string),\n\t\tbody:        new(bytes.Buffer),\n\t\twroteHeader: false,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc NewDebugGen(s *XDSServer, systemNamespace string, debugMux *http.ServeMux) *DebugGen {\n\treturn &DebugGen{\n\t\tServer:          s,\n\t\tSystemNamespace: systemNamespace,\n\t\tDebugMux:        debugMux,\n\t}\n}\n\n// Generate XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\nfunc (dg *DebugGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n\t}\n\n\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n\t}\n\n\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n\n\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n\t\tName: resourceName,\n\t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n\t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n\t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n\t\t},\n\t}}\n\treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n}\n\n// GenerateDeltas XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\nfunc (dg *DebugGen) GenerateDeltas(\n\tproxy *model.Proxy,\n\treq *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n\t}\n\n\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n\t}\n\n\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n\n\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n\t\tName: resourceName,\n\t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n\t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n\t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n\t\t},\n\t}}\n\treturn res, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, nil\n}\n\nfunc validateProxyAuthentication(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource) error {\n\tif proxy.VerifiedIdentity == nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"proxy %s is not authorized to receive debug. Ensure you are connecting over TLS port and are authenticated.\", proxy.ID)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, \"authentication required\")\n\t}\n\tif w.ResourceNames == nil || len(w.ResourceNames) != 1 {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, \"exactly one debug request is required\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, dg *DebugGen) (string, error) {\n\tresourceName := w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0]\n\tu, _ := url.Parse(resourceName)\n\tdebugType := u.Path\n\tidentity := proxy.VerifiedIdentity\n\tif identity.Namespace != dg.SystemNamespace {\n\t\tif _, ok := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugType]; !ok {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", status.Errorf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"the debug info is not available for current identity: %q\", identity)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn resourceName, nil\n}\n\nfunc processDebugRequest(dg *DebugGen, resourceName string) bytes.Buffer {\n\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n\tdebugURL := \"/debug/\" + resourceName\n\threq, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, debugURL, nil)\n\thandler, _ := dg.DebugMux.Handler(hreq)\n\tresponse := NewResponseCapture()\n\thandler.ServeHTTP(response, hreq)\n\tif response.wroteHeader && len(response.header) >= 1 {\n\t\theader, _ := json.Marshal(response.header)\n\t\tbuffer.Write(header)\n\t}\n\tbuffer.Write(response.body.Bytes())\n\treturn buffer\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/pilot/pkg/xds/debuggen.go"
}

distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n\tanypb (r ResponseCapture) Header() http.Header {\n\theader := make(http.Header)\n\tfor k, v := range r.header {\n\t\theader.Set(k, v)\n\t}\n\treturn header\n}\n\nfunc (r ResponseCapture) Write(i []byte) (int, error) {\n\treturn r.body.Write(i)\n}\n\nfunc (r ResponseCapture) WriteHeader(statusCode int) {\n\tr.header[\"statusCode\"] = strconv.Itoa(statusCode)\n}\n\nfunc NewResponseCapture() *ResponseCapture {\n\treturn &ResponseCapture{\n\t\theader:      make(map[string]string),\n\t\tbody:        new(bytes.Buffer),\n\t\twroteHeader: false,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc NewDebugGen(s *XDSServer, systemNamespace string, debugMux *http.ServeMux) *DebugGen {\n\treturn &DebugGen{\n\t\tServer:          s,\n\t\tSystemNamespace: systemNamespace,\n\t\tDebugMux:        debugMux,\n\t}\n}\n\n// Generate XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\nfunc (dg *DebugGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n\t}\n\n\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n\t}\n\n\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n\n\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n\t\tName: resourceName,\n\t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n\t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n\t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n\t\t},\n\t}}\n\treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n}\n\n// GenerateDeltas XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\nfunc (dg *DebugGen) GenerateDeltas(\n\tproxy *model.Proxy,\n\treq *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n\t}\n\n\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n\t}\n\n\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n\n\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n\t\tName: resourceName,\n\t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n\t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n\t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n\t\t},\n\t}}\n\treturn res, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, nil\n}\n\nfunc validateProxyAuthentication(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource) error {\n\tif proxy.VerifiedIdentity == nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"proxy %s is not authorized to receive debug. Ensure you are connecting over TLS port and are authenticated.\", proxy.ID)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, \"authentication required\")\n\t}\n\tif w.ResourceNames == nil || len(w.ResourceNames) != 1 {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, \"exactly one debug request is required\")\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, dg *DebugGen) (string, error) {\n\tresourceName := w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0]\n\tu, _ := url.Parse(resourceName)\n\tdebugType := u.Path\n\tidentity := proxy.VerifiedIdentity\n\tif identity.Namespace != dg.SystemNamespace {\n\t\tif _, ok := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugType]; !ok {\n\t\t\treturn \"\", status.Errorf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"the debug info is not available for current identity: %q\", identity)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn resourceName, nil\n}\n\nfunc processDebugRequest(dg *DebugGen, resourceName string) bytes.Buffer {\n\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n\tdebugURL := \"/debug/\" + resourceName\n\threq, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, debugURL, nil)\n\thandler, _ := dg.DebugMux.Handler(hreq)\n\tresponse := NewResponseCapture()\n\thandler.ServeHTTP(response, hreq)\n\tif response.wroteHeader && len(response.header) >= 1 {\n\t\theader, _ := json.Marshal(response.header)\n\t\tbuffer.Write(header)\n\t}\n\tbuffer.Write(response.body.Bytes())\n\treturn buffer\n}\n",

\\t\\\"runtime\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n31: \\n32: \\tadmin \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/admin/v3\\\"\\n33: \\tcore \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\\\"\\n34: \\twasm \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/extensions/filters/http/wasm/v3\\\"\\n35: \\ttls \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/extensions/transport_sockets/tls/v3\\\"\\n36: \\tdiscoveryv3 \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/protobuf/proto\\\"\\n38: \\tanypb \\\"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/anypb\\\"\\n39: \\n40: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/kube/crd\\\"\\n41: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n43: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\\\"\\n44: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\\\"\\n45: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n46: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config\\\"\\n47: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/constants\\\"\\n48: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/resource\\\"\\n49: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/xds\\\"\\n50: \\tistiolog \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/log\\\"\\n51: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/maps\\\"\\n52: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/security\\\"\\n53: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\\\"\\n54: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/spiffe\\\"\\n55: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/protomarshal\\\"\\n56: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n57: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/workloadapi\\\"\\n58: )\\n59: \\n60: // CallerNamespaceKey is used to store caller namespace in request context\\n61: type CallerNamespaceKey struct{}\\n62: \\n63: var indexTmpl = template.Must(template.New(\\\"index\\\").Parse(`<html>\\n64: <head>\\n65: <title>Pilot Debug Console</title>\\n66: </head>\\n67: <style>\\n68: #endpoints {\\n69:   font-family: \\\"Trebuchet MS\\\", Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;\\n70:   border-collapse: collapse;\\n71: }\\n72: \\n73: #endpoints td, #endpoints th {\\n74:   border: 1px solid #ddd;\\n75:   padding: 8px;\\n76: }\\n77: \\n78: #endpoints tr:nth-child(even){background-color: #f2f2f2;}\\n79: \\n80: #endpoints tr:hover {background-color: #ddd;}\\n81: \\n82: #endpoints th {\\n83:   padding-top: 12px;\\n84:   padding-bottom: 12px;\\n85:   text-align: left;\\n86:   background-color: black;\\n87:   color: white;\\n88: }\\n89: </style>\\n90: <body>\\n91: <br/>\\n92: <p style = \\\"font-family:Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif;\\\">\\n93: Note: Use <b>pretty</b> in query string (like <b>debug/configz?pretty</b>) to format the output.\\n94: </p>\\n95: <table id=\\\"endpoints\\\">\\n96: <tr><th>Endpoint</th><th>Description</th></tr>\\n97: {{range .}}\\n98: \\t<tr>\\n99: \\t<td><a href='{{.Href}}'>{{.Name}}</a></td><td>{{.Help}}</td>\\n100: \\t</tr>\\n101: {{end}}\\n102: </table>\\n103: <br/>\\n104: </body>\\n105: </html>\\n106: `))\\n107: \\n108: // AdsClient defines the data that is displayed on \\\"/adsz\\\" endpoint.\\n109: type AdsClient struct {\\n110: \\tConnectionID string              `json:\\\"connectionId\\\"`\\n111: \\tConnectedAt  time.Time           `json:\\\"connectedAt\\\"`\\n112: \\tPeerAddress  string              `json:\\\"address\\\"`\\n113: \\tLabels       map[string]string   `json:\\\"labels,omitempty\\\"`\\n114: \\tMetadata     *model.NodeMetadata `json:\\\"metadata,omitempty\\\"`\\n115: \\tLocality     *core.Locality      `json:\\\"locality,omitempty\\\"`\\n116: \\tWatches      map[string][]string `json:\\\"watches,omitempty\\\"`\\n117: }\\n118: \\n119: // AdsClients is collection of AdsClient connected to this Istiod.\\n120: type AdsClients struct {\\n121: \\tTotal     int         `json:\\\"totalClients\\\"`\\n122: \\tConnected []AdsClient `json:\\\"clients,omitempty\\\"`\\n123: }\\n124: \\n125: // SyncStatus is the synchronization status between Pilot and a given Envoy\\n126: type SyncStatus struct {\\n127: \\tClusterID            string                    `json:\\\"cluster_id,omitempty\\\"`\\n128: \\tProxyID              string                    `json:\\\"proxy,omitempty\\\"`\\n129: \\tProxyType            model.NodeType            `json:\\\"proxy_type,omitempty\\\"`\\n130: \\tProxyVersion         string                    `json:\\\"proxy_version,omitempty\\\"`\\n131: \\tIstioVersion         string                    `json:\\\"istio_version,omitempty\\\"`\\n132: \\tClusterSent          string                    `json:\\\"cluster_sent,omitempty\\\"`\\n133: \\tClusterAcked         string                    `json:\\\"cluster_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n134: \\tListenerSent         string                    `json:\\\"listener_sent,omitempty\\\"`\\n135: \\tListenerAcked        string                    `json:\\\"listener_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n136: \\tRouteSent            string                    `json:\\\"route_sent,omitempty\\\"`\\n137: \\tRouteAcked           string                    `json:\\\"route_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n138: \\tEndpointSent         string                    `json:\\\"endpoint_sent,omitempty\\\"`\\n139: \\tEndpointAcked        string                    `json:\\\"endpoint_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n140: \\tExtensionConfigSent  string                    `json:\\\"extensionconfig_sent,omitempty\\\"`\\n141: \\tExtensionConfigAcked string                    `json:\\\"extensionconfig_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n142: \\tResources            map[string]ResourceStatus `json:\\\"resources,omitempty\\\"`\\n143: }\\n144: \\n145: type ResourceStatus struct {\\n146: \\tSent      string    `json:\\\"sent,omitempty\\\"`\\n147: \\tAcked     string    `json:\\\"acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n148: \\tSentTime  time.Time `json:\\\"sentTime,omitempty\\\"`\\n149: \\tLastError string    `json:\\\"lastError,omitempty\\\"`\\n150: }\\n151: \\n152: // SyncedVersions shows what resourceVersion of a given resource has been acked by Envoy.\\n153: type SyncedVersions struct {\\n154: \\tProxyID         string `json:\\\"proxy,omitempty\\\"`\\n155: \\tClusterVersion  string `json:\\\"cluster_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n156: \\tListenerVersion string `json:\\\"listener_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n157: \\tRouteVersion    string `json:\\\"route_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n158: \\tEndpointVersion string `json:\\\"endpoint_acked,omitempty\\\"`\\n159: }\\n160: \\n161: // InitDebug initializes the debug handlers and adds a debug in-memory registry.\\n162: func (s *DiscoveryServer) InitDebug(\\n163: \\tmux *http.ServeMux,\\n164: \\tenableProfiling bool,\\n165: \\tfetchWebhook func() map[string]string,\\n166: ) *http.ServeMux {\\n167: \\tinternalMux := http.NewServeMux()\\n168: \\ts.AddDebugHandlers(mux, internalMux, enableProfiling, fetchWebhook)\\n169: \\treturn internalMux\\n170: }\\n171: \\n172: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AddDebugHandlers(mux, internalMux *http.ServeMux, enableProfiling bool, webhook func() map[string]string) {\\n173: \\t// Debug handlers on HTTP ports are added for backward compatibility.\\n174: \\t// They will be exposed on XDS-over-TLS in future releases.\\n175: \\tif !features.EnableDebugOnHTTP {\\n176: \\t\\treturn\\n177: \\t}\\n178: \\n179: \\tif enableProfiling {\\n180: \\t\\truntime.SetMutexProfileFraction(features.MutexProfileFraction)\\n181: \\t\\truntime.SetBlockProfileRate(features.MutexProfileFraction)\\n182: \\t\\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/pprof/\\\", \\\"Displays pprof index\\\", pprof.Index)\\n183: \\t\\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/pprof/cmdline\\\", \\\"The command line invocation of the current program\\\", pprof.Cmdline)\\n184: \\t\\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/pprof/profile\\\", \\\"CPU profile\\\", pprof.Profile)\\n185: \\t\\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/pprof/symbol\\\", \\\"Symbol looks up the program counters listed in the request\\\", pprof.Symbol)\\n186: \\t\\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/pprof/trace\\\", \\\"A trace of execution of the current program.\\\", pprof.Trace)\\n187: \\t}\\n188: \\n189: \\tmux.HandleFunc(\\\"/debug\\\", s.Debug)\\n190: \\n191: \\tif features.EnableUnsafeAdminEndpoints {\\n192: \\t\\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/force_disconnect\\\", \\\"Disconnects a proxy from this Pilot\\\", s.forceDisconnect)\\n193: \\t}\\n194: \\n195: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/ecdsz\\\", \\\"Status and debug interface for ECDS\\\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\\\"ecds\\\"))\\n196: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/edsz\\\", \\\"Status and debug interface for EDS\\\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\\\"eds\\\"))\\n197: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/ndsz\\\", \\\"Status and debug interface for NDS\\\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\\\"nds\\\"))\\n198: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/adsz\\\", \\\"Status and debug interface for ADS\\\", s.adsz)\\n199: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/adsz?push=true\\\", \\\"Initiates push of the current state to all connected endpoints\\\", s.adsz)\\n200: \\n201: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/syncz\\\", \\\"Synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\\\", s.Syncz)\\n202: \\n203: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/registryz\\\", \\\"Debug support for registry\\\", s.registryz)\\n204: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/endpointz\\\", \\\"Obsolete, use endpointShardz\\\", s.endpointShardz)\\n205: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/endpointShardz\\\", \\\"Info about the endpoint shards\\\", s.endpointShardz)\\n206: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/cachez\\\", \\\"Info about the internal XDS caches\\\", s.cachez)\\n207: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/cachez?sizes=true\\\", \\\"Info about the size of the internal XDS caches\\\", s.cachez)\\n208: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/cachez?clear=true\\\", \\\"Clear the XDS caches\\\", s.cachez)\\n209: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/configz\\\", \\\"Debug support for config\\\", s.configz)\\n210: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/sidecarz\\\", \\\"Debug sidecar scope for a proxy\\\", s.sidecarz)\\n211: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/resourcesz\\\", \\\"Debug support for watched resources\\\", s.resourcez)\\n212: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/instancesz\\\", \\\"Debug support for service instances\\\", s.instancesz)\\n213: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/ambientz\\\", \\\"Debug support for ambient\\\", s.ambientz)\\n214: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/krtz\\\", \\\"Debug support for krt (internal state)\\\", s.krtz)\\n215: \\n216: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/authorizationz\\\", \\\"Internal authorization policies\\\", s.authorizationz)\\n217: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/telemetryz\\\", \\\"Debug Telemetry configuration\\\", s.telemetryz)\\n218: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/config_dump\\\", \\\"ConfigDump in the form of the Envoy admin config dump API for passed in proxyID\\\", s.ConfigDump)\\n219: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/push_status\\\", \\\"Last PushContext Details\\\", s.pushStatusHandler)\\n220: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/pushcontext\\\", \\\"Debug support for current push context\\\", s.pushContextHandler)\\n221: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/connections\\\", \\\"Info about the connected XDS clients\\\", s.connectionsHandler)\\n222: \\n223: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/inject\\\", \\\"Active inject template\\\", s.injectTemplateHandler(webhook))\\n224: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/mesh\\\", \\\"Active mesh config\\\", s.meshHandler)\\n225: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/clusterz\\\", \\\"List remote clusters where istiod reads endpoints\\\", s.clusterz)\\n226: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/networkz\\\", \\\"List cross-network gateways\\\", s.networkz)\\n227: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/mcsz\\\", \\\"List information about Kubernetes MCS services\\\", s.mcsz)\\n228: \\n229: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/list\\\", \\\"List all supported debug commands in json\\\", s.list)\\n230: }\\n231: \\n232: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addDebugHandler(mux *http.ServeMux, internalMux *http.ServeMux,\\n233: \\tpath string, help string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request),\\n234: ) {\\n235: \\ts.debugHandlers[path] = help\\n236: \\t// Add handler without auth. This mux is never exposed on an HTTP server and only used internally\\n237: \\tif internalMux != nil {\\n238: \\t\\tinternalMux.HandleFunc(path, handler)\\n239: \\t}\\n240: \\t// Add handler with auth; this is expose on an HTTP server\\n241: \\tmux.HandleFunc(path, s.allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(http.HandlerFunc(handler)))\\n242: }\\n243: \\n244: func (s *DiscoveryServer) allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(next http.Handler) http.HandlerFunc {\\n245: \\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n246: \\t\\t// Localhost gets unrestricted access (istiod talking to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\\n247: \\t\\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\\n248: \\t\\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\\n249: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n250: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n251: \\t\\t}\\n252: \\t\\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\\n253: \\t\\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\\n254: \\t\\tvar ids []string\\n255: \\t\\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\\n256: \\t\\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\\n257: \\t\\t\\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\\n258: \\t\\t\\t// If one authenticator passes, return\\n259: \\t\\t\\tif u != nil && u.Identities != nil && err == nil {\\n260: \\t\\t\\t\\tids = u.Identities\\n261: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n262: \\t\\t\\t}\\n263: \\t\\t\\tauthFailMsgs = append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Authenticator %s: %v\\\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\\n264: \\t\\t}\\n265: \\t\\tif ids == nil {\\n266: \\t\\t\\tistiolog.Errorf(\\\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\\\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\\n267: \\t\\t\\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \\\"authentication failure).\\n268: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\\n269: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n270: \\t\\t}\\n271: \\t\\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\\n272: \\t\\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\\n274: \\t\\t\\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\\n275: \\t\\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\\\", ids, req.URL.Path)\\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n278: \\t\\t\\t}\\n279: \\t\\t\\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\\n280: \\t\\t\\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\\n281: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\\n282: \\t\\t} else {\\n283: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n284: \\t\\t}\\n285: \\t}\\n286: }\\n287: \\n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\\n289: \\tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\\n290: \\tif err != nil {\\n291: \\t\\treturn false\\n292: \\t}\\n293: \\n294: \\tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\\n295: \\treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\\n296: }\\n297: \\n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\\n300: \\tfor _, id := range identities {\\n301: \\t\\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(id)\\n302: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n304: \\t\\t}\\n305: \\t\\treturn spiffeID.Namespace\\n306: \\t}\\n307: \\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n308: }\\n309: \\n310: // AuthorizeDebugRequest checks if authenticated identities are authorized to access the requested debug endpoint.\\n311: // Note: non-system namespace requests are further verified at connection time to ensure same-namespace proxy access only.\\n312: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AuthorizeDebugRequest(identities []string, req *http.Request) bool {\\n313: \\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(identities)\\n314: \\n315: \\t// deny if no valid identity found\\n316: \\tif namespace == \\\"\\\" {\\n317: \\t\\treturn false\\n318: \\t}\\n319: \\n320: \\t// get system namespace (istio-system by default, or mesh root namespace)\\n321: \\tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\\n322: \\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \\\"\\\" {\\n323: \\t\\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\\n324: \\t}\\n325: \\n326: \\t// allow all if identity is from system namespace\\n327: \\tif namespace == systemNamespace {\\n328: \\t\\treturn true\\n329: \\t}\\n330: \\n331: \\t// non-system namespace: only allow specific endpoints\\n332: \\tdebugPath := strings.TrimPrefix(req.URL.Path, \\\"/debug/\\\")\\n333: \\t_, allowed := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugPath]\\n334: \\treturn allowed\\n335: }\\n336: \\n337: // Syncz dumps the synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\\n338: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Syncz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n339: \\tnamespace := req.URL.Query().Get(\\\"namespace\\\")\\n340: \\n341: \\tsyncz := make([]SyncStatus, 0)\\n342: \\tfor _, con := range s.SortedClients() {\\n343: \\t\\tnode := con.proxy\\n344: \\t\\tif node != nil && (namespace == \\\"\\\" || node.GetNamespace() == namespace) {\\n345: \\t\\t\\twrs := node.DeepCloneWatchedResources()\\n346: \\t\\t\\tres := make(map[string]ResourceStatus, len(wrs))\\n347: \\t\\t\\tfor _, wr := range wrs {\\n348: \\t\\t\\t\\tres[wr.TypeUrl] = ResourceStatus{\\n349: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSent:      wr.NonceSent,\\n350: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAcked:     wr.NonceAcked,\\n351: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSentTime:  wr.LastSendTime,\\n352: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tLastError: wr.LastError,\\n353: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n354: \\t\\t\\t}\\n355: \\t\\t\\tsyncz = append(syncz, SyncStatus{\\n356: \\t\\t\\t\\tProxyID:              node.ID,\\n357: \\t\\t\\t\\tProxyType:            node.Type,\\n358: \\t\\t\\t\\tClusterID:            node.GetClusterID().String(),\\n359: \\t\\t\\t\\tIstioVersion:         node.GetIstioVersion(),\\n360: \\t\\t\\t\\tClusterSent:          node.NonceSent(v3.ClusterType),\\n361: \\t\\t\\t\\tClusterAcked:         node.NonceAcked(v3.ClusterType),\\n362: \\t\\t\\t\\tListenerSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.ListenerType),\\n363: \\t\\t\\t\\tListenerAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.ListenerType),\\n364: \\t\\t\\t\\tRouteSent:            node.NonceSent(v3.RouteType),\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\tRouteAcked:           node.NonceAcked(v3.RouteType),\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\tEndpointSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.EndpointType),\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\tEndpointAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.EndpointType),\\n368: \\t\\t\\t\\tExtensionConfigSent:  node.NonceSent(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\\n369: \\t\\t\\t\\tExtensionConfigAcked: node.NonceAcked(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\\n370: \\t\\t\\t\\tResources:            res,\\n371: \\t\\t\\t})\\n372: \\t\\t}\\n373: \\t}\\n374: \\twriteJSON(w, syncz, req)\\n375: }\\n376: \\n377: // registryz providees debug support for registry - adding and listing model items.\\n378: // Can be combined with the push debug interface to reproduce changes.\\n379: func (s *DiscoveryServer) registryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n380: \\tall := s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Services()\\n381: \\twriteJSON(w, all, req)\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: // Dumps info about the endpoint shards, tracked using the new direct interface.\\n385: // Legacy registry provides are synced to the new data structure as well, during\\n386: // the full push.\\n387: func (s *DiscoveryServer) endpointShardz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n388: \\twriteJSON(w, s.Env.EndpointIndex.Shardz(), req)\\n389: }\\n390: \\n391: func (s *DiscoveryServer) cachez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n392: \\tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\\n393: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\\n394: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Failed to parse request\\\\n\\\"))\\n395: \\t\\treturn\\n396: \\t}\\n397: \\tif req.Form.Get(\\\"clear\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n398: \\t\\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\\n399: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Cache cleared\\\\n\\\"))\\n400: \\t\\treturn\\n401: \\t}\\n402: \\tif req.Form.Get(\\\"sizes\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n403: \\t\\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\\n404: \\t\\traw := make(map[string]int, len(snapshot))\\n405: \\t\\ttotalSize := 0\\n406: \\t\\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\\n407: \\t\\t\\tif resource == nil {\\n408: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n409: \\t\\t\\t}\\n410: \\t\\t\\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\\n411: \\t\\t\\tsz := len(resource.Resource.GetValue())\\n412: \\t\\t\\traw[resourceType] += sz\\n413: \\t\\t\\ttotalSize += sz\\n414: \\t\\t}\\n415: \\t\\tres := make(map[string]string, len(raw))\\n416: \\t\\tfor k, v := range raw {\\n417: \\t\\t\\tres[k] = util.ByteCount(v)\\n418: \\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\tres[\\\"total\\\"] = util.ByteCount(totalSize)\\n420: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, res, req)\\n421: \\t\\treturn\\n422: \\t}\\n423: \\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\\n424: \\tresources := make(map[string][]string, len(snapshot)) // Key is typeUrl and value is resource names.\\n425: \\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\\n426: \\t\\tif resource == nil {\\n427: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n428: \\t\\t}\\n429: \\t\\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\\n430: \\t\\tresources[resourceType] = append(resources[resourceType], resource.Name)\\n431: \\t}\\n432: \\twriteJSON(w, resources, req)\\n433: }\\n434: \\n435: // kubernetesConfig wraps a config.Config with a custom marshaling method that matches a Kubernetes\\n436: // object structure.\\n437: type kubernetesConfig struct {\\n438: \\tconfig.Config\\n439: }\\n440: \\n441: func (k kubernetesConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\\n442: \\tcfg, err := crd.ConvertConfig(k.Config)\\n443: \\tif err != nil {\\n444: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n445: \\t}\\n446: \\treturn json.Marshal(cfg)\\n447: }\\n448: \\n449: // Config debugging.\\n450: func (s *DiscoveryServer) configz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n451: \\tconfigs := make([]kubernetesConfig, 0)\\n452: \\tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.ConfigStore == nil {\\n453: \\t\\treturn\\n454: \\t}\\n455: \\ts.Env.ConfigStore.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\\n456: \\t\\tcfg := s.Env.ConfigStore.List(schema.GroupVersionKind(), \\\"\\\")\\n457: \\t\\tfor _, c := range cfg {\\n458: \\t\\t\\tconfigs = append(configs, kubernetesConfig{c})\\n459: \\t\\t}\\n460: \\t\\treturn false\\n461: \\t})\\n462: \\twriteJSON(w, configs, req)\\n463: }\\n464: \\n465: // SidecarScope debugging\\n466: func (s *DiscoveryServer) sidecarz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n467: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n468: \\tif con == nil {\\n469: \\t\\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\\n470: \\t\\treturn\\n471: \\t}\\n472: \\twriteJSON(w, con.proxy.SidecarScope, req)\\n473: }\\n474: \\n475: // Resource debugging.\\n476: func (s *DiscoveryServer) resourcez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n477: \\tschemas := make([]config.GroupVersionKind, 0)\\n478: \\n479: \\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.ConfigStore != nil {\\n480: \\t\\ts.Env.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\\n481: \\t\\t\\tschemas = append(schemas, schema.GroupVersionKind())\\n482: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n483: \\t\\t})\\n484: \\t}\\n485: \\n486: \\twriteJSON(w, schemas, req)\\n487: }\\n488: \\n489: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\\n490: type AuthorizationDebug struct {\\n491: \\tAuthorizationPolicies *model.AuthorizationPolicies `json:\\\"authorization_policies\\\"`\\n492: }\\n493: \\n494: // authorizationz dumps the internal authorization policies.\\n495: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authorizationz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n496: \\tinfo := AuthorizationDebug{\\n497: \\t\\tAuthorizationPolicies: s.globalPushContext().AuthzPolicies,\\n498: \\t}\\n499: \\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\\n500: }\\n501: \\n502: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\\n503: type TelemetryDebug struct {\\n504: \\tTelemetries *model.Telemetries `json:\\\"telemetries\\\"`\\n505: }\\n506: \\n507: func (s *DiscoveryServer) telemetryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n508: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n509: \\tif proxyID != \\\"\\\" && con == nil {\\n510: \\t\\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\\n511: \\t\\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\\n512: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n513: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\\\n\\\"))\\n514: \\t\\treturn\\n515: \\t}\\n516: \\tif con == nil {\\n517: \\t\\tinfo := TelemetryDebug{\\n518: \\t\\t\\tTelemetries: s.globalPushContext().Telemetry,\\n519: \\t\\t}\\n520: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\\n521: \\t\\treturn\\n522: \\t}\\n523: \\twriteJSON(w, s.globalPushContext().Telemetry.Debug(con.proxy), req)\\n524: }\\n525: \\n526: // connectionsHandler implements interface for displaying current connections.\\n527: // It is mapped to /debug/connections.\\n528: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionsHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n529: \\tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\\n530: \\tconnections := s.SortedClients()\\n531: \\tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\\n532: \\n533: \\tfor _, c := range connections {\\n534: \\t\\tadsClient := AdsClient{\\n535: \\t\\t\\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\\n536: \\t\\t\\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\\n537: \\t\\t\\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\\n538: \\t\\t}\\n539: \\t\\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\\n540: \\t}\\n541: \\twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\\n542: }\\n543: \\n544: // adsz implements a status and debug interface for ADS.\\n545: // It is mapped to /debug/adsz\\n546: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n547: \\tif s.handlePushRequest(w, req) {\\n548: \\t\\treturn\\n549: \\t}\\n550: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n551: \\tif proxyID != \\\"\\\" && con == nil {\\n552: \\t\\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\\n553: \\t\\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\\n554: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n555: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\\\n\\\"))\\n556: \\t\\treturn\\n557: \\t}\\n558: \\tvar connections []*Connection\\n559: \\tif con != nil {\\n560: \\t\\tconnections = []*Connection{con}\\n561: \\t} else {\\n562: \\t\\tconnections = s.SortedClients()\\n563: \\t}\\n564: \\n565: \\tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\\n566: \\tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\\n567: \\tfor _, c := range connections {\\n568: \\t\\tadsClient := AdsClient{\\n569: \\t\\t\\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\\n570: \\t\\t\\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\\n571: \\t\\t\\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\\n572: \\t\\t\\tLabels:       c.proxy.Labels,\\n573: \\t\\t\\tMetadata:     c.proxy.Metadata,\\n574: \\t\\t\\tLocality:     c.proxy.Locality,\\n575: \\t\\t\\tWatches:      map[string][]string{},\\n576: \\t\\t}\\n577: \\t\\tc.proxy.RLock()\\n578: \\t\\tfor k, wr := range c.proxy.WatchedResources {\\n579: \\t\\t\\tadsClient.Watches[k] = wr.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()\\n580: \\t\\t}\\n581: \\t\\tc.proxy.RUnlock()\\n582: \\t\\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\\n583: \\t}\\n584: \\twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\\n585: }\\n586: \\n587: func (s *DiscoveryServer) typedConfigDumpHandler(typ string) http.HandlerFunc {\\n588: \\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n589: \\t\\tq := r.URL.Query()\\n590: \\t\\tq.Set(\\\"types\\\", typ)\\n591: \\t\\tr.URL.RawQuery = q.Encode()\\n592: \\t\\ts.ConfigDump(w, r)\\n593: \\t}\\n594: }\\n595: \\n596: // ConfigDump returns information in the form of the Envoy admin API config dump for the specified proxy\\n597: // The dump will only contain dynamic listeners/clusters/routes and can be used to compare what an Envoy instance\\n598: // should look like according to Pilot vs what it currently does look like.\\n599: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigDump(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n600: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n601: \\tif con == nil {\\n602: \\t\\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\\n603: \\t\\treturn\\n604: \\t}\\n605: \\tif ts := s.getResourceTypes(req); len(ts) != 0 {\\n606: \\t\\tresources := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(con, nil, ts)\\n607: \\t\\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{}\\n608: \\t\\tfor _, resource := range resources {\\n609: \\t\\t\\tfor _, rr := range resource {\\n610: \\t\\t\\t\\tconfigDump.Configs = append(configDump.Configs, rr.Resource)\\n611: \\t\\t\\t}\\n612: \\t\\t}\\n613: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, configDump, req)\\n614: \\t\\treturn\\n615: \\t}\\n616: \\n617: \\tif con.proxy.IsZTunnel() {\\n618: \\t\\tresources := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(con, nil, []string{v3.AddressType})\\n619: \\t\\tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{}\\n620: \\t\\tfor _, resource := range resources {\\n621: \\t\\t\\tfor _, rr := range resource {\\n622: \\t\\t\\t\\tconfigDump.Configs = append(configDump.Configs, rr.Resource)\\n623: \\t\\t\\t}\\n624: \\t\\t}\\n625: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, configDump, req)\\n626: \\t\\treturn\\n627: \\t}\\n628: \\n629: \\tincludeEds := req.URL.Query().Has(\\\"include_eds\\\")\\n630: \\tdump, err := s.connectionConfigDump(con, includeEds)\\n631: \\tif err != nil {\\n632: \\t\\thandleHTTPError(w, err)\\n633: \\t\\treturn\\n634: \\t}\\n635: \\twriteJSON(w, dump, req)\\n636: }\\n637: \\n638: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getResourceTypes(req *http.Request) []string {\\n639: \\tif shortTypes := req.URL.Query().Get(\\\"types\\\"); shortTypes != \\\"\\\" {\\n640: \\t\\tts := strings.Split(shortTypes, \\\",\\\")\\n641: \\n642: \\t\\tresourceTypes := sets.New[string]()\\n643: \\t\\tfor _, t := range ts {\\n644: \\t\\t\\trType := v3.GetResourceType(t)\\n645: \\t\\t\\tresourceTypes.Insert(rType)\\n646: \\t\\t\\t// special case for AddressType, include WorkloadType as well\\n647: \\t\\t\\t// because they shared same short type name `WDS`\\n648: \\t\\t\\tif rType == v3.AddressType {\\n649: \\t\\t\\t\\tresourceTypes.Insert(v3.WorkloadType)\\n650: \\t\\t\\t}\\n651: \\t\\t}\\n652: \\n653: \\t\\treturn resourceTypes.UnsortedList()\\n654: \\t}\\n655: \\treturn nil\\n656: }\\n657: \\n658: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn *Connection, req *model.PushRequest, ts []string) map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource {\\n659: \\tdumps := make(map[string][]*discoveryv3.Resource)\\n660: \\tif req == nil {\\n661: \\t\\treq = &model.PushRequest{Push: conn.proxy.LastPushContext, Start: time.Now(), Full: true, Forced: true}\\n662: \\t}\\n663: \\n664: \\tfor _, resourceType :="
}

\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n28: \\n29: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n30: \\tistiogrpc \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\\\"\\n33: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n34: \\tistiolog \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/log\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\\\"\\n36: \\t_ \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/protomarshal\\\" // Ensure we get the more efficient vtproto gRPC encoder\\n37: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\\\"\\n39: )\\n40: \\n41: var deltaLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\\\"delta\\\", \\\"delta xds debugging\\\")\\n42: \\n43: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamDeltas(stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) error {\\n44: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n45: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n46: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n47: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n48: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n49: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n50: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n51: \\t}\\n52: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n53: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n54: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n55: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n56: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n57: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n58: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n59: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n60: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n61: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n62: \\t}\\n63: \\n64: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n65: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n66: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n67: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n68: \\t}\\n69: \\n70: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n71: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n72: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n73: \\t}\\n74: \\n75: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n76: \\tif err != nil {\\n77: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\tif ids != nil {\\n80: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n81: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/delta.go%3FL1-80\"}",

    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"errors\"\n19: \t\"fmt\"\n20: \t\"strconv\"\n21: \t\"strings\"\n22: \t\"time\"\n23: \n24: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n25: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n26: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\"\n27: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n28: \n29: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n30: \tistiogrpc \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc\"\n31: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n32: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n33: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n34: \tistiolog \"istio.io/istio/pkg/log\"\n35: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\"\n36: \t_ \"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/protomarshal\" // Ensure we get the more efficient vtproto gRPC encoder\n37: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n38: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n39: )\n40: \n41: var deltaLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"delta\", \"delta xds debugging\")\n42: \n43: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamDeltas(stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) error {\n44: \tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n45: \t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n46: \t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n47: \t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n48: \t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n49: \t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n50: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n51: \t}\n52: \t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n53: \t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n54: \t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n55: \t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n56: \t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n57: \t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n58: \t// ip tables update latencies.\n59: \t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n60: \tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n61: \t\treturn errors.New(\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n62: \t}\n63: \n64: \tctx := stream.Context()\n65: \tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n66: \tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n67: \t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n68: \t}\n69: \n70: \tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n71: \t\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n72: \t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n73: \t}\n74: \n75: \tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n76: \tif err != nil {\n77: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n78: \t}\n79: \tif ids != nil {\n80: \t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n81: ",

  }

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n19: \\n20: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n21: \\n22: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/endpoints\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n28: )\\n29: \\n30: // SvcUpdate is a callback from service discovery when service info changes.\\n31: func (s *DiscoveryServer) SvcUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, event model.Event) {\\n32: \\t// When a service deleted, we should cleanup the endpoint shards and also remove keys from EndpointIndex to\\n33: \\t// prevent memory leaks.\\n34: \\tif event == model.EventDelete {\\n35: \\t\\tinboundServiceDeletes.Increment()\\n36: \\t\\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteServiceShard(shard, hostname, namespace, false)\\n37: \\t} else {\\n38: \\t\\tinboundServiceUpdates.Increment()\\n39: \\t}\\n40: }\\n41: \\n42: // EDSUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\\n43: // This is the main method implementing EDS.\\n44: // It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\\n45: // the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\\n46: // on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\\n47: func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\\n48: \\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\\n49: ) {\\n50: \\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\\n51: \\t// Update the endpoint shards\\n52: \\tpushType := s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, true)\\n53: \\tif pushType == model.IncrementalPush || pushType == model.FullPush {\\n54: \\t\\t// Trigger a push\\n55: \\t\\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\\n56: \\t\\t\\tFull:           pushType == model.FullPush,\\n57: \\t\\t\\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: serviceName, Namespace: namespace}),\\n58: \\t\\t\\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.EndpointUpdate),\\n59: \\t\\t})\\n60: \\t}\\n61: }\\n62: \\n63: // EDSCacheUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\\n64: // This is the main method implementing EDS.\\n65: // It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\\n66: // the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\\n67: // on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\\n68: //\\n69: // Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push\\n70: func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSCacheUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\\n71: \\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\\n72: ) {\\n73: \\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\\n74: \\t// Update the endpoint shards\\n75: \\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, false)\\n76: }\\n77: \\n78: func (s *DiscoveryServer) RemoveShard(shardKey model.ShardKey) {\\n79: \\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteShard(shardKey)\\n80: }\\n81: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/eds.go%3FL1-80\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"fmt\"\n19: \n20: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n21: \n22: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n23: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n24: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\"\n25: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/endpoints\"\n26: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n27: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n28: )\n29: \n30: // SvcUpdate is a callback from service discovery when service info changes.\n31: func (s *DiscoveryServer) SvcUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, event model.Event) {\n32: \t// When a service deleted, we should cleanup the endpoint shards and also remove keys from EndpointIndex to\n33: \t// prevent memory leaks.\n34: \tif event == model.EventDelete {\n35: \t\tinboundServiceDeletes.Increment()\n36: \t\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteServiceShard(shard, hostname, namespace, false)\n37: \t} else {\n38: \t\tinboundServiceUpdates.Increment()\n39: \t}\n40: }\n41: \n42: // EDSUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\n43: // This is the main method implementing EDS.\n44: // It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\n45: // the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n46: // on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\n47: func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n48: \tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n49: ) {\n50: \tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n51: \t// Update the endpoint shards\n52: \tpushType := s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, true)\n53: \tif pushType == model.IncrementalPush || pushType == model.FullPush {\n54: \t\t// Trigger a push\n55: \t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\n56: \t\t\tFull:           pushType == model.FullPush,\n57: \t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: serviceName, Namespace: namespace}),\n58: \t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.EndpointUpdate),\n59: \t\t})\n60: \t}\n61: }\n62: \n63: // EDSCacheUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\n64: // This is the main method implementing EDS.\n65: // It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\n66: // the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n67: // on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\n68: //\n69: // Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push\n70: func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSCacheUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n71: \tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n72: ) {\n73: \tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n74: \t// Update the endpoint shards\n75: \ts.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, false)\n76: }\n77: \n78: func (s *DiscoveryServer) RemoveShard(shardKey model.ShardKey) {\n79: \ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteShard(shardKey)\n80: }\n81: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/eds.go%3FL1-80"
  }
}
