  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n19: \\n20: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\\\"\\n22: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n25: )\\n26: \\n27: type WorkloadGenerator struct {\\n28: \\tServer *DiscoveryServer\\n29: }\\n30: \\n31: var (\\n32: \\t_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadGenerator{}\\n33: \\t_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadGenerator{}\\n34: )\\n35: \\n36: // GenerateDeltas computes Workload resources. This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,\\n37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\\n38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\\n39: //\\n40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.\\n41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.\\n42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).\\n43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\\n44: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n45: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n46: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n48: \\taddresses := req.AddressesUpdated\\n49: \\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\\n50: \\tif !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {\\n51: \\t\\t// Nothing changed...\\n52: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\\n53: \\t}\\n54: \\n55: \\tif !w.Wildcard {\\n56: \\t\\treturn e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)\\n57: \\t}\\n58: \\n59: \\treqAddresses := addresses\\n60: \\tif isReq {\\n61: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/workload.go%3FL1-60\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n19: \n20: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n21: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\"\n22: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n23: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n24: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n25: )\n26: \n27: type WorkloadGenerator struct {\n28: \tServer *DiscoveryServer\n29: }\n30: \n31: var (\n32: \t_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadGenerator{}\n33: \t_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadGenerator{}\n34: )\n35: \n36: // GenerateDeltas computes Workload resources. This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,\n37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\n38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\n39: //\n40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.\n41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.\n42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).\n43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\n44: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n45: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n46: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n48: \taddresses := req.AddressesUpdated\n49: \tisReq := req.IsRequest()\n50: \tif !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {\n51: \t\t// Nothing changed...\n52: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n53: \t}\n54: \n55: \tif !w.Wildcard {\n56: \t\treturn e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)\n57: \t}\n58: \n59: \treqAddresses := addresses\n60: \tif isReq {\n61: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/workload.go%3FL1-60"
  }

  "description": "Create Python helper script for replacements"
}

"     1\u2192// Copyright Istio Authors\n     2\u2192//\n     3\u2192// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     4\u2192// you may not use this file except in compliance either express or implied.\n    12\u2192// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    13\u2192// limitations under the License.\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192package xds\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192import (\n    18\u2192\t\"context\"\n    19\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    20\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    21\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    22\u2192\t\"sync\"\n    23\u2192\t\"time\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n    26\u2192\t\"github.com/google/uuid\"\n    27\u2192\t\"go.uber.org/atomic\"\n    28\u2192\t\"golang.org/x/time/rate\"\n    29\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\"\n    32\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n    33\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n    34\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/core/envoyfilter\"\n    35\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\"\n    36\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n    37\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube/krt\"\n    38\u2192\tistiolog \"istio.io/istio/pkg/log\"\n    39\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/maps\"\n    40\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/security\"\n    41\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\"\n    42\u2192)\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192var periodicRefreshMetrics = 10 * time.Second\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192type DebounceOptions struct {\n    47\u2192\t// DebounceAfter is the delay added to events to wait\n    48\u2192\t// after a registry/config event for debouncing.\n    49\u2192\t// This will delay the push by at least this interval, plus\n    50\u2192\t// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is\n    51\u2192\t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\n    52\u2192\t// delaying until things settle.\n    53\u2192\tDebounceAfter time.Duration\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192\t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\n    56\u2192\t// while debouncing. Defaults to 10 seconds. If events keep\n    57\u2192\t// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.\n    58\u2192\tdebounceMax time.Duration\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192\t// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.\n    61\u2192\tenableEDSDebounce bool\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192// XDSServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs\n    65\u2192type XDSServer struct {\n    66\u2192\t// Env is the model environment.\n    67\u2192\tEnv *model.Environment\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192\t// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.\n    70\u2192\t// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection\n    71\u2192\t// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a\n    72\u2192\t// different generator for a type.\n    73\u2192\t// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.\n    74\u2192\tGenerators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192\t// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. Individual generators\n    77\u2192\t// may also choose to not send any updates.\n    78\u2192\tProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192\t// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.\n    81\u2192\tconcurrentPushLimit chan struct{}\n    82\u2192\t// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\n    83\u2192\tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\n    86\u2192\tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\n    87\u2192\t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\n    88\u2192\t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\n    89\u2192\t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\n    90\u2192\t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\n    91\u2192\t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\n    92\u2192\tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\n    95\u2192\t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\n    96\u2192\tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192\t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\n    99\u2192\tpushQueue *PushQueue\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192\t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\n   102\u2192\tdebugHandlers map[string]string\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192\t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\n   105\u2192\tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\n   106\u2192\tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192\t// Authenticators for XDS requests. Should be same/subset of the CA authenticators.\n   109\u2192\tAuthenticators []security.Authenticator\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192\tWorkloadEntryController *autoregistration.Controller\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192\t// serverReady indicates caches have been synced up and server is ready to process requests.\n   114\u2192\tserverReady atomic.Bool\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192\tDebounceOptions DebounceOptions\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\t// Cache for XDS resources\n   119\u2192\tCache model.XdsCache\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192\t// JwtKeyResolver holds a reference to the JWT key resolver instance.\n   122\u2192\tJwtKeyResolver *model.JwksResolver\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192\t// ListRemoteClusters collects debug information about other clusters this istiod reads from.\n   125\u2192\tListRemoteClusters func() []cluster.DebugInfo\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\t// ClusterAliases are alias names for cluster. When a proxy connects with a cluster ID\n   128\u2192\t// and if it has a different alias we should use that a cluster ID for proxy.\n   129\u2192\tClusterAliases map[cluster.ID]cluster.ID\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192\t// pushVersion stores the numeric push version. This should be accessed via NextVersion()\n   132\u2192\tpushVersion atomic.Uint64\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192\t// DiscoveryStartTime is the time since the binary started\n   135\u2192\tDiscoveryStartTime time.Time\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192\tkrtDebugger *krt.DebugHandler\n   138\u2192}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192// NewXDSServer creates XDSServer that sources data from Pilot's internal mesh data structures\n   141\u2192func NewXDSServer(env *model.Environment, clusterAliases map[string]string, debugger *krt.DebugHandler) *XDSServer {\n   142\u2192\tout := &XDSServer{\n   143\u2192\t\tEnv:                 env,\n   144\u2192\t\tGenerators:          map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator{},\n   145\u2192\t\tProxyNeedsPush:      DefaultProxyNeedsPush,\n   146\u2192\t\tconcurrentPushLimit: make(chan struct{}, features.PushThrottle),\n   147\u2192\t\tRequestRateLimit:    rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(features.RequestLimit), 1),\n   148\u2192\t\tInboundUpdates:      atomic.NewInt64(0),\n   149\u2192\t\tCommittedUpdates:    atomic.NewInt64(0),\n   150\u2192\t\tpushChannel:         make(chan *model.PushRequest, 10),\n   151\u2192\t\tpushQueue:           NewPushQueue(),\n   152\u2192\t\tdebugHandlers:       map[string]string{},\n   153\u2192\t\tadsClients:          map[string]*Connection{},\n   154\u2192\t\tkrtDebugger:         debugger,\n   155\u2192\t\tDebounceOptions: DebounceOptions{\n   156\u2192\t\t\tDebounceAfter:     features.DebounceAfter,\n   157\u2192\t\t\tdebounceMax:       features.DebounceMax,\n   158\u2192\t\t\tenableEDSDebounce: features.EnableEDSDebounce,\n   159\u2192\t\t},\n   160\u2192\t\tCache:              env.Cache,\n   161\u2192\t\tDiscoveryStartTime: processStartTime,\n   162\u2192\t}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192\tout.ClusterAliases = make(map[cluster.ID]cluster.ID)\n   165\u2192\tfor alias := range clusterAliases {\n   166\u2192\t\tout.ClusterAliases[cluster.ID(alias)] = cluster.ID(clusterAliases[alias])\n   167\u2192\t}\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\tout.initJwksResolver()\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192\treturn out\n   172\u2192}\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192// initJwkResolver initializes the JWT key resolver to be used.\n   175\u2192func (s *XDSServer) initJwksResolver() {\n   176\u2192\tif s.JwtKeyResolver != nil {\n   177\u2192\t\ts.closeJwksResolver()\n   178\u2192\t}\n   179\u2192\ts.JwtKeyResolver = model.NewJwksResolver(\n   180\u2192\t\tmodel.JwtPubKeyEvictionDuration, model.JwtPubKeyRefreshInterval,\n   181\u2192\t\tmodel.JwtPubKeyRefreshIntervalOnFailure, model.JwtPubKeyRetryInterval)\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192\t// Flush cached discovery responses when detecting jwt public key change.\n   184\u2192\ts.JwtKeyResolver.PushFunc = func() {\n   185\u2192\t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Reason: model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger), Forced: true})\n   186\u2192\t}\n   187\u2192}\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192// closeJwksResolver shuts down the JWT key resolver used.\n   190\u2192func (s *XDSServer) closeJwksResolver() {\n   191\u2192\tif s.JwtKeyResolver != nil {\n   192\u2192\t\ts.JwtKeyResolver.Close()\n   193\u2192\t}\n   194\u2192}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192// Register adds the ADS handler to the grpc server\n   197\u2192func (s *XDSServer) Register(rpcs *grpc.Server) {\n   198\u2192\t// Register v3 server\n   199\u2192\tdiscovery.RegisterAggregatedDiscoveryServiceServer(rpcs, s)\n   200\u2192}\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192var processStartTime = time.Now()\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192// CachesSynced is called when caches have been synced so that server can accept connections.\n   205\u2192func (s *XDSServer) CachesSynced() {\n   206\u2192\tlog.Infof(\"All caches have been synced up in %v, marking server ready\", time.Since(s.DiscoveryStartTime))\n   207\u2192\ts.serverReady.Store(true)\n   208\u2192}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192func (s *XDSServer) IsServerReady() bool {\n   211\u2192\treturn s.serverReady.Load()\n   212\u2192}\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192func (s *XDSServer) Start(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   215\u2192\tgo s.WorkloadEntryController.Run(stopCh)\n   216\u2192\tgo s.handleUpdates(stopCh)\n   217\u2192\tgo s.periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh)\n   218\u2192\tgo s.sendPushes(stopCh)\n   219\u2192\tgo s.Cache.Run(stopCh)\n   220\u2192}\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192// Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)\n   223\u2192func (s *XDSServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   224\u2192\tticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)\n   225\u2192\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n   226\u2192\tfor {\n   227\u2192\t\tselect {\n   228\u2192\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n   229\u2192\t\t\tpush := s.globalPushContext()\n   230\u2192\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\n   231\u2192\t\t\tif model.LastPushStatus != push {\n   232\u2192\t\t\t\tmodel.LastPushStatus = push\n   233\u2192\t\t\t\tpush.UpdateMetrics()\n   234\u2192\t\t\t\tout, _ := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()\n   235\u2192\t\t\t\tif string(out) != \"{}\" {\n   236\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push Status: %s\", string(out))\n   237\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   238\u2192\t\t\t}\n   239\u2192\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\n   240\u2192\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n   241\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   242\u2192\t\t}\n   243\u2192\t}\n   244\u2192}\n   245\u2192\n   246\u2192// dropCacheForRequest clears the cache in response to a push request\n   247\u2192func (s *XDSServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {\n   248\u2192\t// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache\n   249\u2192\tif req.Forced {\n   250\u2192\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n   251\u2192\t} else {\n   252\u2192\t\t// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs\n   253\u2192\t\ts.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)\n   254\u2192\t}\n   255\u2192}\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192// Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.\n   258\u2192func (s *XDSServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\n   259\u2192\tif !req.Full {\n   260\u2192\t\treq.Push = s.globalPushContext()\n   261\u2192\t\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n   262\u2192\t\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n   263\u2192\t\treturn\n   264\u2192\t}\n   265\u2192\t// Reset the status during the push.\n   266\u2192\toldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()\n   267\u2192\tif oldPushContext != nil {\n   268\u2192\t\toldPushContext.OnConfigChange()\n   269\u2192\t\t// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.\n   270\u2192\t\tenvoyfilter.RecordMetrics()\n   271\u2192\t}\n   272\u2192\t// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is\n   273\u2192\t// saved.\n   274\u2192\tt0 := time.Now()\n   275\u2192\tversionLocal := s.NextVersion()\n   276\u2192\tpush := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)\n   277\u2192\tinitContextTime := time.Since(t0)\n   278\u2192\tlog.Debugf(\"InitContext %v for push took %s\", versionLocal, initContextTime)\n   279\u2192\tpushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192\treq.Push = push\n   282\u2192\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n   283\u2192}\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192func nonce(noncePrefix string) string {\n   286\u2192\treturn noncePrefix + uuid.New().String()\n   287\u2192}\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192// Returns the global push context. This should be used with caution; generally the proxy-specific\n   290\u2192// PushContext should be used to get the current state in the context of a single proxy. This should\n   291\u2192// only be used for \"global\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\n   292\u2192func (s *XDSServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\n   293\u2192\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\n   294\u2192}\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192var fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"fullpush\", \"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\")\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192// ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\n   299\u2192func (s *XDSServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\n   300\u2192\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n   301\u2192\t\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\n   302\u2192\t\t\tpanic(\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\")\n   303\u2192\t\t}\n   304\u2192\t}\n   305\u2192\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\n   306\u2192\t\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address\n   307\u2192\t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n   308\u2192\t\t// the cache.\n   309\u2192\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n   310\u2192\t}\n   311\u2192\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n   312\u2192\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n   313\u2192\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n   314\u2192\t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n   315\u2192\t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n   316\u2192\t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n   317\u2192\t}\n   318\u2192\ts.pushChannel <- req\n   319\u2192}\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n   322\u2192// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n   323\u2192// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n   324\u2192// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n   325\u2192// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\n   326\u2192func (s *XDSServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   327\u2192\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n   328\u2192}\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\n   331\u2192func debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n   332\u2192\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n   333\u2192\tvar startDebounce time.Time\n   334\u2192\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192\tpushCounter := 0\n   337\u2192\tdebouncedEvents := 0\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192\t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\n   340\u2192\tvar req *model.PushRequest\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192\tfree := true\n   343\u2192\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\n   346\u2192\t\tpushFn(req)\n   347\u2192\t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n   348\u2192\t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n   349\u2192\t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n   350\u2192\t}\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192\tpushWorker := func() {\n   353\u2192\t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n   354\u2192\t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n   355\u2192\t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n   356\u2192\t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\n   357\u2192\t\t\tif req != nil {\n   358\u2192\t\t\t\tpushCounter++\n   359\u2192\t\t\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n   360\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n   361\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\n   362\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n   363\u2192\t\t\t\t} else {\n   364\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n   365\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\n   366\u2192\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n   367\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   368\u2192\t\t\t\tfree = false\n   369\u2192\t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n   370\u2192\t\t\t\treq = nil\n   371\u2192\t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n   372\u2192\t\t\t}\n   373\u2192\t\t} else {\n   374\u2192\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\n   375\u2192\t\t}\n   376\u2192\t}\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192\tfor {\n   379\u2192\t\tselect {\n   380\u2192\t\tcase <-freeCh:\n   381\u2192\t\t\tfree = true\n   382\u2192\t\t\tpushWorker()\n   383\u2192\t\tcase r := <-ch:\n   384\u2192\t\t\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\n   385\u2192\t\t\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\n   386\u2192\t\t\t\tr.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)\n   387\u2192\t\t\t}\n   388\u2192\t\t\tif !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {\n   389\u2192\t\t\t\t// trigger push now, just for EDS\n   390\u2192\t\t\t\tgo func(req *model.PushRequest) {\n   391\u2192\t\t\t\t\tpushFn(req)\n   392\u2192\t\t\t\t\tupdateSent.Inc()\n   393\u2192\t\t\t\t}(r)\n   394\u2192\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   395\u2192\t\t\t}\n   396\u2192\n   397\u2192\t\t\tlastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()\n   398\u2192\t\t\tif debouncedEvents == 0 {\n   399\u2192\t\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)\n   400\u2192\t\t\t\tstartDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime\n   401\u2192\t\t\t}\n   402\u2192\t\t\tdebouncedEvents++\n   403\u2192\n   404\u2192\t\t\treq = req.Merge(r)\n   405\u2192\t\tcase <-timeChan:\n   406\u2192\t\t\tif free {\n   407\u2192\t\t\t\tpushWorker()\n   408\u2192\t\t\t}\n   409\u2192\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n   410\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   411\u2192\t\t}\n   412\u2192\t}\n   413\u2192}\n   414\u2192\n   415\u2192func configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n   416\u2192\tconfigs := \"\"\n   417\u2192\tfor key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n   418\u2192\t\tconfigs += key.String()\n   419\u2192\t\tbreak\n   420\u2192\t}\n   421\u2192\tif len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {\n   422\u2192\t\tmore := \" and \" + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + \" more configs\"\n   423\u2192\t\tconfigs += more\n   424\u2192\t}\n   425\u2192\treturn configs\n   426\u2192}\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192func reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n   429\u2192\tvar (\n   430\u2192\t\treason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason\n   431\u2192\t\treason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int\n   432\u2192\t)\n   433\u2192\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\n   434\u2192\t\tif idx == 0 {\n   435\u2192\t\t\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\n   436\u2192\t\t} else if idx == 1 {\n   437\u2192\t\t\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\n   438\u2192\t\t} else {\n   439\u2192\t\t\tbreak\n   440\u2192\t\t}\n   441\u2192\t\tidx++\n   442\u2192\t}\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192\tswitch len(req.Reason) {\n   445\u2192\tcase 0:\n   446\u2192\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n   447\u2192\tcase 1:\n   448\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt)\n   449\u2192\tcase 2:\n   450\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\n   451\u2192\tdefault:\n   452\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\n   453\u2192\t\t\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\n   454\u2192\t}\n   455\u2192}\n   456\u2192\n   457\u2192func doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\n   458\u2192\tfor {\n   459\u2192\t\tselect {\n   460\u2192\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n   461\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   462\u2192\t\tdefault:\n   463\u2192\t\t\t// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.\n   464\u2192\t\t\t// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently\n   465\u2192\t\t\tsemaphore <- struct{}{}\n   466\u2192\n   467\u2192\t\t\t// Get the next proxy to push. This will block if there are no updates required.\n   468\u2192\t\t\tclient, push, shuttingdown := queue.Dequeue()\n   469\u2192\t\t\tif shuttingdown {\n   470\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n   471\u2192\t\t\t}\n   472\u2192\t\t\trecordPushTriggers(push.Reason)\n   473\u2192\t\t\t// Signals that a push is done by reading from the semaphore, allowing another send on it.\n   474\u2192\t\t\tdoneFunc := func() {\n   475\u2192\t\t\t\tqueue.MarkDone(client)\n   476\u2192\t\t\t\t<-semaphore\n   477\u2192\t\t\t}\n   478\u2192\n   479\u2192\t\t\tproxiesQueueTime.Record(time.Since(push.Start).Seconds())\n   480\u2192\t\t\tvar closed <-chan struct{}\n   481\u2192\t\t\tif client.deltaStream != nil {\n   482\u2192\t\t\t\tclosed = client.deltaStream.Context().Done()\n   483\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   484\u2192\t\t\t\tclosed = client.StreamDone()\n   485\u2192\t\t\t}\n   486\u2192\t\t\tgo func() {\n   487\u2192\t\t\t\tpushEv := &Event{\n   488\u2192\t\t\t\t\tpushRequest: push,\n   489\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdone:        doneFunc,\n   490\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   491\u2192\n   492\u2192\t\t\t\tselect {\n   493\u2192\t\t\t\tcase client.PushCh() <- pushEv:\n   494\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn\n   495\u2192\t\t\t\tcase <-closed: // grpc stream was closed\n   496\u2192\t\t\t\t\tdoneFunc()\n   497\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Client closed connection %v\", client.ID())\n   498\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   499\u2192\t\t\t}()\n   500\u2192\t\t}\n   501\u2192\t}\n   502\u2192}\n   503\u2192\n   504\u2192// initPushContext creates a global push context and stores it on the environment. Note: while this\n   505\u2192// method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;\n   506\u2192// if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in\n   507\u2192// reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.\n   508\u2192func (s *XDSServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {\n   509\u2192\tpush := model.NewPushContext()\n   510\u2192\tpush.PushVersion = version\n   511\u2192\tpush.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver\n   512\u2192\tpush.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)\n   513\u2192\n   514\u2192\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n   515\u2192\ts.Env.SetPushContext(push)\n   516\u2192\n   517\u2192\treturn push\n   518\u2192}\n   519\u2192\n   520\u2192func (s *XDSServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n   521\u2192\tdoSendPushes(stopCh, s.concurrentPushLimit, s.pushQueue)\n   522\u2192}\n   523\u2192\n   524\u2192// Shutdown shuts down XDSServer components.\n   525\u2192func (s *XDSServer) Shutdown() {\n   526\u2192\ts.closeJwksResolver()\n   527\u2192\ts.pushQueue.ShutDown()\n   528\u2192}\n   529\u2192\n   530\u2192// Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,\n   531\u2192// but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\n   532\u2192// This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\n   533\u2192func (s *XDSServer) Clients() []*Connection {\n   534\u2192\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n   535\u2192\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n   536\u2192\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\n   537\u2192\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\n   538\u2192\t\tselect {\n   539\u2192\t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n   540\u2192\t\tdefault:\n   541\u2192\t\t\t// Initialization not complete, skip\n   542\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   543\u2192\t\t}\n   544\u2192\t\tclients = append(clients, con)\n   545\u2192\t}\n   546\u2192\treturn clients\n   547\u2192}\n   548\u2192\n   549\u2192// SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\n   550\u2192// Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\n   551\u2192func (s *XDSServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\n   552\u2192\tclients := s.Clients()\n   553\u2192\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\n   554\u2192\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\n   555\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n   556\u2192\t\t}\n   557\u2192\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\n   558\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n   559\u2192\t\t}\n   560\u2192\t\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\n   561\u2192\t})\n   562\u2192\treturn clients\n   563\u2192}\n   564\u2192\n   565\u2192// AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\n   566\u2192// Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\n   567\u2192func (s *XDSServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\n   568\u2192\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n   569\u2192\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n   570\u2192\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\n   571\u2192}\n   572\u2192\n   573\u2192func (s *XDSServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\n   574\u2192\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\n   575\u2192\t\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\n   576\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   577\u2192\t}\n   578\u2192\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another\n   579\u2192\t// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.\n   580\u2192\twait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)\n   581\u2192\tdefer cancel()\n   582\u2192\treturn s.RequestRateLimit.Wait(wait)\n   583\u2192}\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192func (s *XDSServer) NextVersion() string {\n   586\u2192\treturn time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339) + \"/\" + strconv.FormatUint(s.pushVersion.Inc(), 10)\n   587\u2192}\n   588\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
38. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pilot/pkg/xds/auth.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright Istio Authors\n     2\u2192//\n     3\u2192// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     4\u2192// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     5\u2192// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     6\u2192//\n     7\u2192//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     8\u2192//\n     9\u2192// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    10\u2192// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    11\u2192// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    12\u2192// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    13\u2192// limitations under the License.\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192package xds\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192import (\n    18\u2192\t\"context\"\n    19\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n    22\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n    25\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n    26\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/security\"\n    27\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/spiffe\"\n    28\u2192)\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192// authenticate authenticates the ADS request using the configured authenticators.\n    31\u2192// Returns the validated principals or an error.\n    32\u2192// If no authenticators are configured, or if the request is on a non-secure\n    33\u2192// stream ( 15010 ) - returns an empty list of principals and no errors.\n    34\u2192func (s *XDSServer) authenticate(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {\n    35\u2192\tc, err := security.Authenticate(ctx, s.Authenticators)\n    36\u2192\tif c != nil {\n    37\u2192\t\treturn c.Identities, nil\n    38\u2192\t}\n    39\u2192\treturn nil, err\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192func (s *XDSServer) authorize(con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n    43\u2192\tif con == nil || con.proxy == nil {\n    44\u2192\t\treturn nil\n    45\u2192\t}\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192\tif features.EnableXDSIdentityCheck && identities != nil {\n    48\u2192\t\t// TODO: allow locking down, rejecting unauthenticated requests.\n    49\u2192\t\tid, err := checkConnectionIdentity(con.proxy, identities)\n    50\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    51\u2192\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Unauthorized XDS: %v with identity %v: %v\", con.Peer(), identities, err)\n    52\u2192\t\t\treturn status.Newf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"authorization failed: %v\", err).Err()\n    53\u2192\t\t}\n    54\u2192\t\tcon.proxy.VerifiedIdentity = id\n    55\u2192\t}\n    56\u2192\treturn nil\n    57\u2192}\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192func checkConnectionIdentity(proxy *model.Proxy, identities []string) (*spiffe.Identity, error) {\n    60\u2192\tfor _, rawID := range identities {\n    61\u2192\t\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(rawID)\n    62\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n    63\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    64\u2192\t\t}\n    65\u2192\t\tif proxy.ConfigNamespace != \"\" && spiffeID.Namespace != proxy.ConfigNamespace {\n    66\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    67\u2192\t\t}\n    68\u2192\t\tif proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount != \"\" && spiffeID.ServiceAccount != proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount {\n    69\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n    70\u2192\t\t}\n    71\u2192\t\treturn &spiffeID, nil\n    72\u2192\t}\n    73\u2192\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"no identities (%v) matched %v/%v\", identities, proxy.ConfigNamespace, proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount)\n    74\u2192}\n    75\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
39. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pilot/pkg/xds/debuggen.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192// Copyright Istio Authors\n     2\u2192//\n     3\u2192// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n     4\u2192// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n     5\u2192// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     6\u2192//\n     7\u2192//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n     8\u2192//\n     9\u2192// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    10\u2192// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    11\u2192// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    12\u2192// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    13\u2192// limitations under the License.\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192package xds\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192import (\n    18\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n    19\u2192\t\"encoding/json\"\n    20\u2192\t\"net/http\"\n    21\u2192\t\"net/url\"\n    22\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n    25\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n    26\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n    27\u2192\tanypb \"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/anypb\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n    30\u2192\tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n    31\u2192)\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192var activeNamespaceDebuggers = map[string]struct{}{\n    34\u2192\t\"config_dump\": {},\n    35\u2192\t\"ndsz\":        {},\n    36\u2192\t\"edsz\":        {},\n    37\u2192}\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192// DebugGen is a Generator for istio debug info\n    40\u2192type DebugGen struct {\n    41\u2192\tServer          *XDSServer\n    42\u2192\tSystemNamespace string\n    43\u2192\tDebugMux        *http.ServeMux\n    44\u2192}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192type ResponseCapture struct {\n    47\u2192\tbody        *bytes.Buffer\n    48\u2192\theader      map[string]string\n    49\u2192\twroteHeader bool\n    50\u2192}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192func (r ResponseCapture) Header() http.Header {\n    53\u2192\theader := make(http.Header)\n    54\u2192\tfor k, v := range r.header {\n    55\u2192\t\theader.Set(k, v)\n    56\u2192\t}\n    57\u2192\treturn header\n    58\u2192}\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192func (r ResponseCapture) Write(i []byte) (int, error) {\n    61\u2192\treturn r.body.Write(i)\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192func (r ResponseCapture) WriteHeader(statusCode int) {\n    65\u2192\tr.header[\"statusCode\"] = strconv.Itoa(statusCode)\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192func NewResponseCapture() *ResponseCapture {\n    69\u2192\treturn &ResponseCapture{\n    70\u2192\t\theader:      make(map[string]string),\n    71\u2192\t\tbody:        new(bytes.Buffer),\n    72\u2192\t\twroteHeader: false,\n    73\u2192\t}\n    74\u2192}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192func NewDebugGen(s *XDSServer, systemNamespace string, debugMux *http.ServeMux) *DebugGen {\n    77\u2192\treturn &DebugGen{\n    78\u2192\t\tServer:          s,\n    79\u2192\t\tSystemNamespace: systemNamespace,\n    80\u2192\t\tDebugMux:        debugMux,\n    81\u2192\t}\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192// Generate XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\n    85\u2192func (dg *DebugGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n    86\u2192\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n    87\u2192\t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n    88\u2192\t}\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n    91\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    92\u2192\t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n    93\u2192\t}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n    98\u2192\t\tName: resourceName,\n    99\u2192\t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n   100\u2192\t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n   101\u2192\t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n   102\u2192\t\t},\n   103\u2192\t}}\n   104\u2192\treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n   105\u2192}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192// GenerateDeltas XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\n   108\u2192func (dg *DebugGen) GenerateDeltas(\n   109\u2192\tproxy *model.Proxy,\n   110\u2192\treq *model.PushRequest,\n   111\u2192\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n   112\u2192) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n   113\u2192\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n   114\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n   115\u2192\t}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n   118\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   119\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n   120\u2192\t}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n   125\u2192\t\tName: resourceName,\n   126\u2192\t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n   127\u2192\t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n   128\u2192\t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n   129\u2192\t\t},\n   130\u2192\t}}\n   131\u2192\treturn res, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, nil\n   132\u2192}\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192func validateProxyAuthentication(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource) error {\n   135\u2192\tif proxy.VerifiedIdentity == nil {\n   136\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"proxy %s is not authorized to receive debug. Ensure you are connecting over TLS port and are authenticated.\", proxy.ID)\n   137\u2192\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, \"authentication required\")\n   138\u2192\t}\n   139\u2192\tif w.ResourceNames == nil || len(w.ResourceNames) != 1 {\n   140\u2192\t\treturn status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, \"exactly one debug request is required\")\n   141\u2192\t}\n   142\u2192\treturn nil\n   143\u2192}\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192func parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, dg *DebugGen) (string, error) {\n   146\u2192\tresourceName := w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0]\n   147\u2192\tu, _ := url.Parse(resourceName)\n   148\u2192\tdebugType := u.Path\n   149\u2192\tidentity := proxy.VerifiedIdentity\n   150\u2192\tif identity.Namespace != dg.SystemNamespace {\n   151\u2192\t\tif _, ok := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugType]; !ok {\n   152\u2192\t\t\treturn \"\", status.Errorf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"the debug info is not available for current identity: %q\", identity)\n   153\u2192\t\t}\n   154\u2192\t}\n   155\u2192\treturn resourceName, nil\n   156\u2192}\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192func processDebugRequest(dg *DebugGen, resourceName string) bytes.Buffer {\n   159\u2192\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n   160\u2192\tdebugURL := \"/debug/\" + resourceName\n   161\u2192\threq, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, debugURL, nil)\n   162\u2192\thandler, _ := dg.DebugMux.Handler(hreq)\n   163\u2192\tresponse := NewResponseCapture()\n   164\u2192\thandler.ServeHTTP(response, hreq)\n   165\u2192\tif response.wroteHeader && len(response.header) >= 1 {\n   166\u2192\t\theader, _ := json.Marshal(response.header)\n   167\u2192\t\tbuffer.Write(header)\n   168\u2192\t}\n   169\u2192\tbuffer.Write(response.body.Bytes())\n   170\u2192\treturn buffer\n   171\u2192}\n   172\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

}
Output
"pilot/pkg/xds/auth.go:34:func (s *XDSServer) authenticate(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {\npilot/pkg/xds/auth.go:42:func (s *XDSServer) authorize(con *Connection, identities []string) error {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:175:func (s *XDSServer) initJwksResolver() {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:190:func (s *XDSServer) closeJwksResolver() {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:197:func (s *XDSServer) Register(rpcs *grpc.Server) {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:205:func (s *XDSServer) CachesSynced() {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:210:func (s *XDSServer) IsServerReady() bool {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:214:func (s *XDSServer) Start(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:223:func (s *XDSServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:247:func (s *XDSServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:258:func (s *XDSServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:292:func (s *XDSServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:299:func (s *XDSServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:326:func (s *XDSServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:508:func (s *XDSServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:520:func (s *XDSServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:525:func (s *XDSServer) Shutdown() {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:533:func (s *XDSServer) Clients() []*Connection {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:551:func (s *XDSServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:567:func (s *XDSServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:573:func (s *XDSServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\npilot/pkg/xds/discovery.go:585:func (s *XDSServer) NextVersion() string {"

44. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "grep -rn \"type XDSServer\" /workspace/pilot/pkg/ 2>/dev/null",
  "description": "Search for XDSServer type definition"
}

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"sync/atomic\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\tcore \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\\\"\\n25: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n26: \\tuatomic \\\"go.uber.org/atomic\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n30: \\n31: \\t\\\"istio.io/api/label\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n34: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\\\"\\n36: \\tlabelutil \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/label\\\"\\n37: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\\\"\\n41: \\tpm \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/model\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n43: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\\\"\\n44: )\\n45: \\n46: var (\\n47: \\tlog = xds.Log\\n48: \\n49: \\t// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.\\n50: \\tconnectionNumber = int64(0)\\n51: )\\n52: \\n53: // Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.\\n54: var firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\\n55: \\n56: var knativeEnv = env.Register(\\\"K_REVISION\\\", \\\"\\\",\\n57: \\t\\\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\\\").Get()\\n58: \\n59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\\n60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\\n61: \\n62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\\n63: type DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\\n64: \\n65: // DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\\n66: type DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\\n67: \\n68: // DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\\n69: type DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\\n70: \\n71: type Connection struct {\\n72: \\txds.Connection\\n73: \\n74: \\t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\\n75: \\t// This is included in internal events.\\n76: \\tnode *core.Node\\n77: \\n78: \\t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\\n79: \\tproxy *model.Proxy\\n80: \\n81: \\t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\\n82: \\tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\\n83: \\n84: \\tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\\n85: \\n86: \\ts   *DiscoveryServer\\n87: \\tids []string\\n88: }\\n89: \\n90: func (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\\n91: \\treturn &conn.Connection\\n92: }\\n93: \\n94: func (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\\n95: \\treturn conn.proxy\\n96: }\\n97: \\n98: // Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\\n99: type Event struct {\\n100: \\t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\\n101: \\tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\\n102: \\n103: \\t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\\n104: \\tdone func()\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\\n108: \\treturn &Connection{\\n109: \\t\\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\\n110: \\t}\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\\n114: \\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {\\n118: \\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\\n119: }\\n120: \\n121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\\n122: \\treturn conn.proxy\\n123: }\\n124: \\n125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\\n126: \\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\\n127: }\\n128: \\n129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\\n130: \\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\\n131: \\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\\n132: \\tpushEv.done()\\n133: \\treturn err\\n134: }\\n135: \\n136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n140: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n141: \\tlog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \\\", stype,\\n142: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\\n143: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n144: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\\n145: \\t\\treturn nil\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\n148: \\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\\n149: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n150: \\t\\treturn s.pushXds(con,\\n151: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\\n152: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\\n156: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n161: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n162: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n163: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n164: \\n165: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n166: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n167: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n168: \\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n169: \\t\\tDelta:  delta,\\n170: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\n173: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\\n174: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n175: \\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\\n176: \\t// computation.\\n177: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n178: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n179: \\t}\\n180: \\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n185: \\treturn s.Stream(stream)\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n189: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n190: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n191: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n192: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n193: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n194: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n195: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n196: \\t}\\n197: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n198: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n199: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n200: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n201: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n202: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n203: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n204: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n205: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n206: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n207: \\t}\\n208: \\n209: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n210: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n211: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n212: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n213: \\t}\\n214: \\n215: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n216: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n217: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n218: \\t}\\n219: \\n220: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n221: \\tif err != nil {\\n222: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\tif ids != nil {\\n225: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n226: \\t} else {\\n227: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\\\", peerAddr)\\n228: \\t}\\n229: \\n230: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n231: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n232: \\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n233: \\tcon.ids = ids\\n234: \\tcon.s = s\\n235: \\treturn xds.Stream(con)\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\\n239: // to the tracking map.\\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\\n241: \\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\\n242: \\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\\n243: \\tif err != nil {\\n244: \\t\\treturn err\\n245: \\t}\\n246: \\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\\n247: \\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\\n248: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\\n249: \\t}\\n250: \\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\\n251: \\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\\n252: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\\n253: \\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\\n254: \\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\\n255: \\tcon.node = node\\n256: \\tcon.proxy = proxy\\n257: \\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\\n258: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\\\")\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\n261: \\t// Authorize xds clients\\n262: \\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\\n263: \\t\\treturn err\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\\n267: \\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\\n268: \\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\\n269: \\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\\n270: \\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\\n271: \\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\\n272: \\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\\n273: \\t// proxy\\n274: \\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\\n275: \\n276: \\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\\n277: \\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\\n278: \\t\\ts.closeConnection(con)\\n279: \\t\\treturn err\\n280: \\t}\\n281: \\treturn nil\\n282: }\\n283: \\n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\\n285: \\tif con.ID() == \\\"\\\" {\\n286: \\t\\treturn\\n287: \\t}\\n288: \\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\\n289: \\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\\n290: }\\n291: \\n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\\n293: \\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\\n294: \\treturn node + \\\"-\\\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\\n295: }\\n296: \\n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\\n301: \\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\\n302: \\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\\n306: \\tif err != nil {\\n307: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\\n310: \\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\\n311: \\tproxy.XdsNode = node\\n312: \\treturn proxy, nil\\n313: }\\n314: \\n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\\n318: \\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\\n319: \\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\\n320: \\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\\n321: \\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\\n322: \\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\\n323: \\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\\n324: \\t\\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\\n325: \\t\\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\\n326: \\t\\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\\n327: \\t\\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\\n328: \\t\\t\\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\\n329: \\t\\t\\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\\n330: \\t\\t\\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\\n331: \\t\\t}\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\\n334: \\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\\n335: \\t\\tproxy.Labels,\\n336: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\\n337: \\t\\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\\n338: \\t\\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\\n339: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\\n340: \\t)\\n341: }\\n342: \\n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\\n344: \\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\\n345: \\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\\n346: \\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\\n347: \\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\\n348: \\t\\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\\n349: \\t\\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\\n350: \\t\\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\\n351: \\t\\tif localityLabel != \\\"\\\" {\\n352: \\t\\t\\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\\n353: \\t\\t}\\n354: \\t\\treturn nil\\n355: \\t}\\n356: \\treturn &core.Locality{\\n357: \\t\\tRegion:  region,\\n358: \\t\\tZone:    zone,\\n359: \\t\\tSubZone: subzone,\\n360: \\t}\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\\n366: \\tproxy := con.proxy\\n367: \\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\\n368: \\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\\n369: \\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\\n370: \\t\\treturn err\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\\n373: \\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\\n374: \\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\\n375: \\n376: \\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\\n377: \\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\\n378: \\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \\\"\\\" {\\n379: \\t\\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\treturn nil\\n383: }\\n384: \\n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\\n386: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n387: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n388: \\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\\n389: \\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\\n390: \\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\\n391: \\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\\n392: \\t\\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\\n393: \\t\\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\\n394: \\t\\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\\n395: \\t\\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\\n396: \\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\t// only recompute workload labels when\\n400: \\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\\n401: \\t// 2. proxy update\\n402: \\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\\n403: \\tif recomputeLabels {\\n404: \\t\\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\\n405: \\t\\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\\n406: \\t}\\n407: \\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\\n408: \\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\\n409: \\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\\n410: \\t// applicable to this proxy\\n411: \\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\\n412: \\tif request == nil {\\n413: \\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n414: \\t} else {\\n415: \\t\\tpush = request.Push\\n416: \\t\\tif request.Forced {\\n417: \\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n418: \\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\\n420: \\t\\t\\tswitch conf.Kind {\\n421: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\\n422: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n423: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Gateway:\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n425: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Ingress:\\n426: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n427: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n428: \\t\\t\\t}\\n429: \\t\\t\\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\\n430: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n431: \\t\\t\\t}\\n432: \\t\\t}\\n433: \\t}\\n434: \\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\\n435: \\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\\n436: \\t\\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\\n437: \\t}\\n438: \\t// only compute gateways for \\\"router\\\" type proxy.\\n439: \\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\\n440: \\t\\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\\n441: \\t}\\n442: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\\n443: \\tif request != nil {\\n444: \\t\\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\\n445: \\t}\\n446: }\\n447: \\n448: // handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\\n449: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\\n450: \\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\\n451: \\t\\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\\n452: \\t\\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\\n453: \\t\\tif !event.Healthy {\\n454: \\t\\t\\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\\n455: \\t\\t}\\n456: \\t\\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\\n457: \\t}\\n458: }\\n459: \\n460: // DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\\n461: // Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\\n462: // This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\\n463: // pattern.\\n464: //\\n465: // The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\\n466: // list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\\n467: func (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\\n468: \\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\\n472: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\\n473: \\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\\n474: \\n475: \\tif pushRequest.Full {\\n476: \\t\\t// Update Proxy with current information.\\n477: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n478: \\t}\\n479: \\n480: \\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n481: \\tif !needsPush {\\n482: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\\\", con.ID())\\n483: \\t\\treturn nil\\n484: \\t}\\n485: \\n486: \\t// Send pushes to all generators\\n487: \\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\\n488: \\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\\n489: \\tfor _, w := range wrl {\\n490: \\t\\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\\n491: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n492: \\t\\t}\\n493: \\t}\\n494: \\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\\n495: \\treturn nil\\n496: }\\n497: \\n498: // PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\\n499: // order after the types listed here\\n500: var PushOrder = []string{\\n501: \\tv3.ClusterType,\\n502: \\tv3.EndpointType,\\n503: \\tv3.ListenerType,\\n504: \\tv3.RouteType,\\n505: \\tv3.SecretType,\\n506: \\tv3.AddressType,\\n507: \\tv3.WorkloadType,\\n508: \\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\\n509: }\\n510: \\n511: // KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\\n512: var KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\\n513: \\n514: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {\\n515: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n516: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n517: \\treturn len(s.adsClients)\\n518: }\\n519: \\n520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\\n521: \\tvar connection *Connection\\n522: \\n523: \\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\\n524: \\t\\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\\n525: \\t\\t\\tconnection = v\\n526: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n527: \\t\\t}\\n528: \\t}\\n529: \\n530: \\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\\n531: \\tif connection == nil {\\n532: \\t\\treturn\\n533: \\t}\\n534: \\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n535: \\t\\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\\n536: \\t\\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\\n537: \\t\\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\\\", currentlyPending)\\n538: \\t\\t}\\n539: \\t}\\n540: \\n541: \\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\\n542: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n543: \\t\\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\\n544: \\t\\tStart:  time.Now(),\\n545: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\\n546: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n547: \\t})\\n548: }\\n549: \\n550: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\\n551: // Mainly used in Debug interface.\\n552: func AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {\\n553: \\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\\n554: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n555: \\t\\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\\n556: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\\n557: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n558: \\t})\\n559: }\\n560: \\n561: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\\n562: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n563: \\tif !req.Full {\\n564: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\\\",\\n565: \\t\\t\\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\\n566: \\t} else {\\n567: \\t\\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\\n568: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\\\",\\n569: \\t\\t\\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\\n570: \\t\\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\\n571: \\n572: \\t\\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\\n573: \\t\\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\\n574: \\t\\t\\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\\n575: \\t\\t}\\n576: \\t}\\n577: \\n578: \\ts.StartPush(req)\\n579: }\\n580: \\n581: // Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\\n582: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n583: \\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\\n584: \\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n585: \\t\\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\\n586: \\t\\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\\n587: \\t\\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\\\", currentlyPending)\\n588: \\t\\t}\\n589: \\t}\\n590: \\treq.Start = time.Now()\\n591: \\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\\n592: \\t\\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\\n593: \\t}\\n594: }\\n595: \\n596: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\\n597: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\\n598: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\\n599: \\ts.adsClients[conID] = con\\n600: \\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\\n601: }\\n602: \\n603: func (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {\\n604: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\\n605: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\\n606: \\n607: \\tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\\n608: \\t\\tlog.Errorf(\\\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\\\", conID)\\n609: \\t\\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\\n610: \\t} else {\\n611: \\t\\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\\n612: \\t\\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\\n613: \\t}\\n614: }\\n615: \\n616: func (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\\n617: \\treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\\n618: }\\n619: \\n620: // watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\\n621: // watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\\n622: func (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\\n623: \\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\\n624: \\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\\n625: \\t// first add all known types, in order\\n626: \\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\\n627: \\t\\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\\n628: \\t\\t\\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\\n629: \\t\\t}\\n630: \\t}\\n631: \\t// Then add any undeclared types\\n632: \\tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\\n633: \\t\\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\\n634: \\t\\t\\tordered = append(ordered, res)\\n635: \\t\\t}\\n636: \\t}\\n637: \\treturn ordered\\n638: }\\n639: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/ads.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"fmt\"\n19: \t\"strconv\"\n20: \t\"strings\"\n21: \t\"sync/atomic\"\n22: \t\"time\"\n23: \n24: \tcore \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\"\n25: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n26: \tuatomic \"go.uber.org/atomic\"\n27: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n28: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\"\n29: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n30: \n31: \t\"istio.io/api/label\"\n32: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\"\n33: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n34: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n35: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n36: \tlabelutil \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/label\"\n37: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n38: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\"\n39: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n40: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\"\n41: \tpm \"istio.io/istio/pkg/model\"\n42: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n43: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n44: )\n45: \n46: var (\n47: \tlog = xds.Log\n48: \n49: \t// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.\n50: \tconnectionNumber = int64(0)\n51: )\n52: \n53: // Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.\n54: var firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\n55: \n56: var knativeEnv = env.Register(\"K_REVISION\", \"\",\n57: \t\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\").Get()\n58: \n59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\n60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\n61: \n62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\n63: type DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\n64: \n65: // DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\n66: type DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\n67: \n68: // DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\n69: type DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\n70: \n71: type Connection struct {\n72: \txds.Connection\n73: \n74: \t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\n75: \t// This is included in internal events.\n76: \tnode *core.Node\n77: \n78: \t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\n79: \tproxy *model.Proxy\n80: \n81: \t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS.
