Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\n82: \tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\n83: \n84: \tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\n85: \n86: \ts   *DiscoveryServer\n87: \tids []string\n88: }\n89: \n90: func (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\n91: \treturn &conn.Connection\n92: }\n93: \n94: func (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\n95: \treturn conn.proxy\n96: }\n97: \n98: // Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\n99: type Event struct {\n100: \t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\n101: \tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\n102: \n103: \t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n104: \tdone func()\n105: }\n106: \n107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n108: \treturn &Connection{\n109: \t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n110: \t}\n111: }\n112: \n113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n114: \treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n115: }\n116: \n117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {\n118: \tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n119: }\n120: \n121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n122: \treturn conn.proxy\n123: }\n124: \n125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n126: \treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n127: }\n128: \n129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n130: \tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n131: \terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n132: \tpushEv.done()\n133: \treturn err\n134: }\n135: \n136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n140: \tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n141: \tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n142: \t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n143: \tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n144: \t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n145: \t\treturn nil\n146: \t}\n147: \n148: \t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n149: \tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n150: \t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n151: \t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n152: \t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n153: \t}\n154: \n155: \tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n156: \tif !shouldRespond {\n157: \t\treturn nil\n158: \t}\n159: \n160: \trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n161: \t\tFull:   true,\n162: \t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n163: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n164: \n165: \t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n166: \t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n167: \t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n168: \t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n169: \t\tDelta:  delta,\n170: \t\tForced: true,\n171: \t}\n172: \n173: \t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n174: \t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n175: \t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n176: \t// computation.\n177: \tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n178: \t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n179: \t}\n180: \treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n181: }\n182: \n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n185: \treturn s.Stream(stream)\n186: }\n187: \n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n189: \tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n190: \t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n191: \t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n192: \t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n193: \t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n194: \t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n195: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n196: \t}\n197: \t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n198: \t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n199: \t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n200: \t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n201: \t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n202: \t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n203: \t// ip tables update latencies.\n204: \t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n205: \tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n206: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n207: \t}\n208: \n209: \tctx := stream.Context()\n210: \tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n211: \tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n212: \t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n213: \t}\n214: \n215: \tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n216: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n217: \t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n218: \t}\n219: \n220: \tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n221: \tif err != nil {\n222: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n223: \t}\n224: \tif ids != nil {\n225: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n226: \t} else {\n227: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n228: \t}\n229: \n230: \t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n231: \ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n232: \tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n233: \tcon.ids = ids\n234: \tcon.s = s\n235: \treturn xds.Stream(con)\n236: }\n237: \n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n239: // to the tracking map.\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n241: \t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n242: \tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n243: \tif err != nil {\n244: \t\treturn err\n245: \t}\n246: \t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n247: \tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n248: \t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n249: \t}\n250: \t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n251: \t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n252: \tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n253: \t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n254: \tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n255: \tcon.node = node\n256: \tcon.proxy = proxy\n257: \tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n258: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n259: \t}\n260: \n261: \t// Authorize xds clients\n262: \tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n263: \t\treturn err\n264: \t}\n265: \n266: \t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n267: \t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n268: \t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n269: \t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n270: \t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n271: \ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n272: \t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n273: \t// proxy\n274: \tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n275: \n276: \t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n277: \tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n278: \t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n279: \t\treturn err\n280: \t}\n281: \treturn nil\n282: }\n283: \n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n285: \tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n286: \t\treturn\n287: \t}\n288: \ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n289: \ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n290: }\n291: \n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\n293: \tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n294: \treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n295: }\n296: \n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n301: \tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n302: \tif err != nil {\n303: \t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n304: \t}\n305: \tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n306: \tif err != nil {\n307: \t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n308: \t}\n309: \t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n310: \tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n311: \tproxy.XdsNode = node\n312: \treturn proxy, nil\n313: }\n314: \n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n318: \t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n319: \t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n320: \t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n321: \t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n322: \tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n323: \tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n324: \t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n325: \t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n326: \t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n327: \t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n328: \t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n329: \t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n330: \t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n331: \t\t}\n332: \t}\n333: \t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n334: \tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n335: \t\tproxy.Labels,\n336: \t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n337: \t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n338: \t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n339: \t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n340: \t)\n341: }\n342: \n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n344: \tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n345: \tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n346: \tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n347: \tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n348: \t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n349: \t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n350: \t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n351: \t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n352: \t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n353: \t\t}\n354: \t\treturn nil\n355: \t}\n356: \treturn &core.Locality{\n357: \t\tRegion:  region,\n358: \t\tZone:    zone,\n359: \t\tSubZone: subzone,\n360: \t}\n361: }\n362: \n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n366: \tproxy := con.proxy\n367: \t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n368: \t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n369: \tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n370: \t\treturn err\n371: \t}\n372: \ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n373: \t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n374: \tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n375: \n376: \tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n377: \t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n378: \tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n379: \t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n380: \t}\n381: \n382: \treturn nil\n383: }\n384: \n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n386: \tproxy.Lock()\n387: \tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n388: \tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n389: \t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n390: \t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n391: \tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n392: \t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n393: \t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n394: \t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n395: \t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n396: \t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n397: \t}\n398: \n399: \t// only recompute workload labels when\n400: \t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n401: \t// 2. proxy update\n402: \trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n403: \tif recomputeLabels {\n404: \t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n405: \t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n406: \t}\n407: \t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n408: \t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n409: \t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n410: \t// applicable to this proxy\n411: \tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n412: \tif request == nil {\n413: \t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n414: \t} else {\n415: \t\tpush = request.Push\n416: \t\tif request.Forced {\n417: \t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n418: \t\t}\n419: \t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n420: \t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n421: \t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n422: \t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n423: \t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n424: \t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n425: \t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n426: \t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n427: \t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n428: \t\t\t}\n429: \t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n430: \t\t\t\tbreak\n431: \t\t\t}\n432: \t\t}\n433: \t}\n434: \t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n435: \tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n436: \t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n437: \t}\n438: \t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n439: \tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n440: \t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n441: \t}\n442: \tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n443: \tif request != nil {\n444: \t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n445: \t}\n446: }\n447: \n448: // handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\n449: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n450: \tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n451: \t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n452: \t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n453: \t\tif !event.Healthy {\n454: \t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n455: \t\t}\n456: \t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n457: \t}\n458: }\n459: \n460: // DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n461: // Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n462: // This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n463: // pattern.\n464: //\n465: // The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n466: // list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\n467: func (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n468: \treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n469: }\n470: \n471: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\n472: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n473: \tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n474: \n475: \tif pushRequest.Full {\n476: \t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n477: \t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n478: \t}\n479: \n480: \tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n481: \tif !needsPush {\n482: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n483: \t\treturn nil\n484: \t}\n485: \n486: \t// Send pushes to all generators\n487: \t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n488: \twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n489: \tfor _, w := range wrl {\n490: \t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n491: \t\t\treturn err\n492: \t\t}\n493: \t}\n494: \tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n495: \treturn nil\n496: }\n497: \n498: // PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n499: // order after the types listed here\n500: var PushOrder = []string{\n501: \tv3.ClusterType,\n502: \tv3.EndpointType,\n503: \tv3.ListenerType,\n504: \tv3.RouteType,\n505: \tv3.SecretType,\n506: \tv3.AddressType,\n507: \tv3.WorkloadType,\n508: \tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n509: }\n510: \n511: // KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\n512: var KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\n513: \n514: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {\n515: \ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n516: \tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n517: \treturn len(s.adsClients)\n518: }\n519: \n520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n521: \tvar connection *Connection\n522: \n523: \tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n524: \t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n525: \t\t\tconnection = v\n526: \t\t\tbreak\n527: \t\t}\n528: \t}\n529: \n530: \t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n531: \tif connection == nil {\n532: \t\treturn\n533: \t}\n534: \tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n535: \t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n536: \t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n537: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n538: \t\t}\n539: \t}\n540: \n541: \ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n542: \t\tFull:   true,\n543: \t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n544: \t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n545: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n546: \t\tForced: true,\n547: \t})\n548: }\n549: \n550: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n551: // Mainly used in Debug interface.\n552: func AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {\n553: \ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n554: \t\tFull:   true,\n555: \t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n556: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n557: \t\tForced: true,\n558: \t})\n559: }\n560: \n561: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\n562: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n563: \tif !req.Full {\n564: \t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n565: \t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n566: \t} else {\n567: \t\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\n568: \t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n569: \t\t\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n570: \t\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\n571: \n572: \t\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\n573: \t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n574: \t\t\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n575: \t\t}\n576: \t}\n577: \n578: \ts.StartPush(req)\n579: }\n580: \n581: // Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\n582: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n583: \t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n584: \tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n585: \t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n586: \t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n587: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n588: \t\t}\n589: \t}\n590: \treq.Start = time.Now()\n591: \tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n592: \t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n593: \t}\n594: }\n595: \n596: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\n597: \ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n598: \tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n599: \ts.adsClients[conID] = con\n600: \trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\n601: }\n602: \n603: func (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {\n604: \ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n605: \tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n606: \n607: \tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\n608: \t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\", conID)\n609: \t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n610: \t} else {\n611: \t\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\n612: \t\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\n613: \t}\n614: }\n615: \n616: func (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\n617: \treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\n618: }\n619: \n620: // watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\n621: // watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\n622: func (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n623: \tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n624: \tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n625: \t// first add all known types, in order\n626: \tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n627: \t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n628: \t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n629: \t\t}\n630: \t}\n631: \t// Then add any undeclared types\n632: \tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\n633: \t\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\n634: \t\t\tordered = append(ordered, res)\n635: \t\t}\n636: \t}\n637: \treturn ordered\n638: }\n639: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/ads.go"
  }
}
50. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/process_files.py << 'EOF'\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\nimport re\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\ndef replace_content(content):\n    \"\"\"Apply all necessary replacements.\"\"\"\n    # Replace type and function names\n    content = re.sub(r'\\bDiscoveryServer\\b', 'XDSServer', content)\n    content = re.sub(r'\\bNewDiscoveryServer\\b', 'NewXDSServer', content)\n    return content\n\n# Read from stdin, apply replacements, write to stdout\ncontent = sys.stdin.read()\ncontent = replace_content(content)\nsys.stdout.write(content)\nEOF\n\nchmod +x /tmp/process_files.py\npython3 /tmp/process_files.py < /dev/null\necho \"Script ready\"\n",
  "description": "Create Python helper for replacements"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Script ready"
}
51. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "\n# Let me create ads.go with replacements using a heredoc pipe\ncat <<'ADS_FILE' > /workspace/pilot/pkg/xds/ads.go\n// Copyright Istio Authors\n//\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage xds\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync/atomic\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tcore \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\"\n\tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n\tuatomic \"go.uber.org/atomic\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n\n\t\"istio.io/api/label\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n\tlabelutil \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/label\"\n\tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\"\n\tpm \"istio.io/istio/pkg/model\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tlog = xds.Log\n\n\t// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.\n\tconnectionNumber = int64(0)\n)\n\n// Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.\nvar firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\n\nvar knativeEnv = env.Register(\"K_REVISION\", \"\",\n\t\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\").Get()\n\n// DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\ntype DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\n\n// DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\ntype DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\n\n// DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\ntype DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\n\n// DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\ntype DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\n\ntype Connection struct {\n\txds.Connection\n\n\t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\n\t// This is included in internal events.\n\tnode *core.Node\n\n\t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\n\tproxy *model.Proxy\n\n\t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\n\tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\n\n\tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\n\n\ts   *XDSServer\n\tids []string\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\n\treturn &conn.Connection\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\n// Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\ntype Event struct {\n\t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\n\tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\n\n\t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n\tdone func()\n}\n\nfunc newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n\treturn &Connection{\n\t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Close() {\n\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n\tpushEv.done()\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta:  delta,\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n\t// computation.\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n}\n\n// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\treturn s.Stream(stream)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n\t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\tcon.ids = ids\n\tcon.s = s\n\treturn xds.Stream(con)\n}\n\n// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n// to the tracking map.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n\t}\n\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n\tcon.node = node\n\tcon.proxy = proxy\n\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Authorize xds clients\n\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n\t// proxy\n\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n\n\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n\t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n\tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n}\n\nfunc connectionID(node string) string {\n\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n\treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n}\n\n// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n// fully initialize the proxy.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n\tproxy.XdsNode = node\n\treturn proxy, nil\n}\n\n// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\nfunc setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n\t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n\t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n\t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n\t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n\t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n\t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n\t\tproxy.Labels,\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n\t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n\t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n\t)\n}\n\nfunc localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n\t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n\t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n\t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n\t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &core.Locality{\n\t\tRegion:  region,\n\t\tZone:    zone,\n\t\tSubZone: subzone,\n\t}\n}\n\n// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n// initProxyMetadata.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n\tproxy := con.proxy\n\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n\n\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n\t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n\tproxy.Lock()\n\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n\t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n\t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n\t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n\t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t}\n\n\t// only recompute workload labels when\n\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n\t// 2. proxy update\n\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n\tif recomputeLabels {\n\t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n\t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n\t}\n\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n\t// applicable to this proxy\n\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n\tif request == nil {\n\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t} else {\n\t\tpush = request.Push\n\t\tif request.Forced {\n\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n\t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n\t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n\t}\n\t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n\t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n\t}\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n\tif request != nil {\n\t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n\t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n\t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n\t\tif !event.Healthy {\n\t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n\t}\n}\n\n// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n// pattern.\n//\n// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n// order after the types listed here\nvar PushOrder = []string{\n\tv3.ClusterType,\n\tv3.EndpointType,\n\tv3.ListenerType,\n\tv3.RouteType,\n\tv3.SecretType,\n\tv3.AddressType,\n\tv3.WorkloadType,\n\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n}\n\n// KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\nvar KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) adsClientCount() int {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn len(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n\tvar connection *Connection\n\n\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n\t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n\t\t\tconnection = v\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n\tif connection == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n// Mainly used in Debug interface.\nfunc AdsPushAll(s *XDSServer) {\n\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t} else {\n\t\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\n\n\t\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\n\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.StartPush(req)\n}\n\n// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treq.Start = time.Now()\n\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n\t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\ts.adsClients[conID] = con\n\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) removeCon(conID string) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\n\t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\", conID)\n\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t} else {\n\t\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\n\t\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\n\treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\n}\n\n// watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\n// watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\nfunc (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n\t// first add all known types, in order\n\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n\t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Then add any undeclared types\n\tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\n\t\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn ordered\n}\nADS_FILE\n\necho \"Created ads.go\"\n",
  "description": "Create ads.go with replacements"
}

{
  "command": "\n# Create a script to fetch files from Sourcegraph and apply sed replacements\ncat > /tmp/fetch_and_process.sh << 'SCRIPT'\n#!/bin/bash\n\nprocess_file() {\n    local file_path=$1\n    local file_name=$(basename \"$file_path\")\n    local dir_path=\"/workspace/$(dirname \"$file_path\")\"\n    \n    mkdir -p \"$dir_path\"\n    # Placeholder - files will be created next\n    echo \"Would process: $file_path\"\n}\n\n# List of files to process\nfiles=(\n    \"pilot/pkg/xds/delta.go\"\n    \"pilot/pkg/xds/deltatest.go\"\n    \"pilot/pkg/xds/eds.go\"\n    \"pilot/pkg/xds/xdsgen.go\"\n    \"pilot/pkg/xds/workload.go\"\n    \"pilot/pkg/xds/statusgen.go\"\n    \"pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go\"\n    \"pilot/test/xds/fake.go\"\n)\n\nfor file in \"${files[@]}\"; do\n    process_file \"$file\"\ndone\nSCRIPT\n\nchmod +x /tmp/fetch_and_process.sh\n/tmp/fetch_and_process.sh\n",
  "description": "Create file processing script"
}

}
