Output
"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n19: \\n20: \\tadmin \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/admin/v3\\\"\\n21: \\tcore \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\\\"\\n22: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n23: \\tstatus \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/status/v3\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb\\\"\\n25: \\n26: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\\\"\\n28: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\\\"\\n30: )\\n31: \\n32: const (\\n33: \\tTypeDebugPrefix = v3.DebugType + \\\"/\\\"\\n34: \\n35: \\t// TypeDebugSyncronization requests Envoy CSDS for proxy sync status\\n36: \\tTypeDebugSyncronization = v3.DebugType + \\\"/syncz\\\"\\n37: \\n38: \\t// TypeDebugConfigDump requests Envoy configuration for a proxy without creating one\\n39: \\tTypeDebugConfigDump = v3.DebugType + \\\"/config_dump\\\"\\n40: \\n41: \\t// TODO: TypeURLReady - readiness events for endpoints, agent can propagate\\n42: )\\n43: \\n44: // StatusGen is a Generator for XDS status: connections, syncz, configdump\\n45: type StatusGen struct {\\n46: \\tServer *DiscoveryServer\\n47: \\n48: \\t// TODO: track last N Nacks and connection events, with 'version' based on timestamp.\\n49: \\t// On new connect, use version to send recent events since last update.\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: func NewStatusGen(s *DiscoveryServer) *StatusGen {\\n53: \\treturn &StatusGen{\\n54: \\t\\tServer: s,\\n55: \\t}\\n56: }\\n57: \\n58: // Generate XDS responses about internal events:\\n59: // - connection status\\n60: // - NACKs\\n61: // We can also expose ACKS.\\n62: func (sg *StatusGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n63: \\treturn sg.handleInternalRequest(proxy, w, req)\\n64: }\\n65: \\n66: // Generate delta XDS responses about internal events:\\n67: // - connection status\\n68: // - NACKs\\n69: // We can also expose ACKS.\\n70: func (sg *StatusGen) GenerateDeltas(\\n71: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n72: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n73: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n74: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n75: \\tres, detail, err := sg.handleInternalRequest(proxy, w, req)\\n76: \\treturn res, nil, detail, true, err\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: func (sg *StatusGen) handleInternalRequest(_ *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, _ *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n80: \\tres := model.Resources{}\\n81: \\n82: \\tswitch w.TypeUrl {\\n83: \\tcase TypeDebugSyncronization:\\n84: \\t\\tres = sg.debugSyncz()\\n85: \\tcase TypeDebugConfigDump:\\n86: \\t\\tif len(w.ResourceNames) == 0 || len(w.ResourceNames) > 1 {\\n87: \\t\\t\\t// Malformed request from client\\n88: \\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"%s with %d ResourceNames\\\", TypeDebugConfigDump, len(w.ResourceNames))\\n89: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n90: \\t\\t}\\n91: \\t\\tvar err error\\n92: \\t\\tdumpRes, err := sg.debugConfigDump(w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0])\\n93: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n94: \\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"%s failed: %v\\\", TypeDebugConfigDump, err)\\n95: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n96: \\t\\t}\\n97: \\t\\tres = dumpRes\\n98: \\t}\\n99: \\treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\\n100: }\\n101: \\n102: // isSidecar ad-hoc method to see if connection represents a sidecar\\n103: func isProxy(con *Connection) bool {\\n104: \\treturn con != nil &&\\n105: \\t\\tcon.proxy != nil &&\\n106: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Metadata != nil &&\\n107: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Metadata.ProxyConfig != nil\\n108: }\\n109: \\n110: func isZtunnel(con *Connection) bool {\\n111: \\treturn con != nil &&\\n112: \\t\\tcon.proxy != nil &&\\n113: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Metadata != nil &&\\n114: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Type == model.Ztunnel\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: func (sg *StatusGen) debugSyncz() model.Resources {\\n118: \\tres := model.Resources{}\\n119: \\n120: \\tfor _, con := range sg.Server.Clients() {\\n121: \\t\\tcon.proxy.RLock()\\n122: \\t\\t// Skip \\\"nodes\\\" without metadata (they are probably istioctl queries!)\\n123: \\t\\tif isProxy(con) || isZtunnel(con) {\\n124: \\t\\t\\txdsConfigs := make([]*status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig, 0)\\n125: \\t\\t\\twrs := con.proxy.DeepCloneWatchedResources()\\n126: \\t\\t\\tfor _, wr := range wrs {\\n127: \\t\\t\\t\\tpxc := &status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig{}\\n128: \\t\\t\\t\\tpxc.ConfigStatus = debugSyncStatus(wr)\\n129: \\t\\t\\t\\tpxc.LastUpdated = timestamppb.New(wr.LastSendTime)\\n130: \\t\\t\\t\\tpxc.TypeUrl = wr.TypeUrl\\n131: \\t\\t\\t\\tif wr.LastError != \\\"\\\" {\\n132: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpxc.ErrorState = &admin.UpdateFailureState{\\n133: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tLastUpdateAttempt: timestamppb.New(wr.LastSendTime),\\n134: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetails:           wr.LastError,\\n135: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n136: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\t\\t\\t\\txdsConfigs = append(xdsConfigs, pxc)\\n139: \\t\\t\\t}\\n140: \\t\\t\\tslices.SortBy(xdsConfigs, func(a *status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig) string {\\n141: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn a.TypeUrl\\n142: \\t\\t\\t})\\n143: \\t\\t\\tclientConfig := &status.ClientConfig{\\n144: \\t\\t\\t\\tNode: &core.Node{\\n145: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tId: con.proxy.ID,\\n146: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tMetadata: model.NodeMetadata{\\n147: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tClusterID:    con.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\\n148: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNamespace:    con.proxy.Metadata.Namespace,\\n149: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tIstioVersion: con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion,\\n150: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}.ToStruct(),\\n151: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n152: \\t\\t\\t\\tGenericXdsConfigs: xdsConfigs,\\n153: \\t\\t\\t}\\n154: \\t\\t\\tres = append(res, &discovery.Resource{\\n155: \\t\\t\\t\\tName:     clientConfig.Node.Id,\\n156: \\t\\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(clientConfig),\\n157: \\t\\t\\t})\\n158: \\t\\t}\\n159: \\t\\tcon.proxy.RUnlock()\\n160: \\t}\\n161: \\n162: \\treturn res\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: func debugSyncStatus(wr model.WatchedResource) status.ConfigStatus {\\n166: \\tif wr.LastError != \\\"\\\" {\\n167: \\t\\treturn status.ConfigStatus_ERROR\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\tif wr.NonceSent == \\\"\\\" {\\n170: \\t\\treturn status.ConfigStatus_NOT_SENT\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\tif wr.NonceAcked == wr.NonceSent {\\n173: \\t\\treturn status.ConfigStatus_SYNCED\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\treturn status.ConfigStatus_STALE\\n176: }\\n177: \\n178: func (sg *StatusGen) debugConfigDump(proxyID string) (model.Resources, error) {\\n179: \\tconn := sg.Server.getProxyConnection(proxyID)\\n180: \\tif conn == nil {\\n181: \\t\\t// This is \\\"like\\\" a 404.  The error is the client's.  However, this endpoint\\n182: \\t\\t// only tracks a single \\\"shard\\\" of connections.  The client may try another instance.\\n183: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"config dump could not find connection for proxyID %q\\\", proxyID)\\n184: \\t}\\n185: \\n186: \\tdump, err := sg.Server.connectionConfigDump(conn, false)\\n187: \\tif err != nil {\\n188: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n189: \\t}\\n190: \\n191: \\treturn model.AnyToUnnamedResources(dump.Configs), nil\\n192: }\\n193: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/statusgen.go\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the \\t\\\"net\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n24: \\n25: \\tendpoint \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/endpoint/v3\\\"\\n26: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/test/bufconn\\\"\\n30: \\tauthorizationv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/authorization/v1\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/fake\\\"\\n34: \\tk8stesting \\\"k8s.io/client-go/testing\\\"\\n35: \\n36: \\tmeshconfig \\\"istio.io/api/mesh/v1alpha1\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/bootstrap\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway\\\"\\n40: \\tingress \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/kube/ingress\\\"\\n41: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/memory\\\"\\n42: \\tkubesecrets \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/credentials/kube\\\"\\n43: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n44: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n45: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/core\\\"\\n46: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry\\\"\\n47: \\tkube \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/kube/controller\\\"\\n48: \\tmemregistry \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/memory\\\"\\n49: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/xdsfake\\\"\\n50: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds\\\"\\n51: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/endpoints\\\"\\n52: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n53: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/test/xdstest\\\"\\n54: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/adsc\\\"\\n55: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\\\"\\n56: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config\\\"\\n57: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/constants\\\"\\n58: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/mesh\\\"\\n59: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/mesh/meshwatcher\\\"\\n60: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/collections\\\"\\n61: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/gvk\\\"\\n62: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/gvr\\\"\\n63: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n64: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/keepalive\\\"\\n65: \\tkubelib \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube\\\"\\n66: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube/krt\\\"\\n67: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube/multicluster\\\"\\n68: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/test\\\"\\n69: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/test/util/retry\\\"\\n70: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n71: )\\n72: \\n73: type FakeOptions struct {\\n74: \\t// If provided, sets the name of the \\\"default\\\" or local cluster to the similaed pilots. (Defaults to opts.DefaultClusterName)\\n75: \\tDefaultClusterName cluster.ID\\n76: \\t// If provided, the minor version will be overridden for calls to GetKubernetesVersion to 1.minor\\n77: \\tKubernetesVersion string\\n78: \\t// If provided, a service registry with the name of each map key will be created with the given objects.\\n79: \\tKubernetesObjectsByCluster map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object\\n80: \\t// If provided, these objects will be used directly for the default cluster (\\\"Kubernetes\\\" or DefaultClusterName)\\n81: \\tKubernetesObjects []runtime.Object\\n82: \\t// If provided, a service registry with the name of each map key will be created with the given objects.\\n83: \\tKubernetesObjectStringByCluster map[cluster.ID]string\\n84: \\t// If provided, the yaml string will be parsed and used as objects for the default cluster (\\\"Kubernetes\\\" or DefaultClusterName)\\n85: \\tKubernetesObjectString string\\n86: \\t// If provided, these configs will be used directly\\n87: \\tConfigs []config.Config\\n88: \\t// If provided, the yaml string will be parsed and used as configs\\n89: \\tConfigString string\\n90: \\t// If provided, the ConfigString will be treated as a go template, with this as input params\\n91: \\tConfigTemplateInput any\\n92: \\t// If provided, this mesh config will be used\\n93: \\tMeshConfig      *meshconfig.MeshConfig\\n94: \\tNetworksWatcher mesh.NetworksWatcher\\n95: \\n96: \\t// Callback to modify the kube client before it is started\\n97: \\tKubeClientModifier func(c kubelib.Client)\\n98: \\n99: \\t// Override the default kube client constructor\\n100: \\tKubeClientBuilder func(objects ...runtime.Object) kubelib.Client\\n101: \\n102: \\t// ListenerBuilder, if specified, allows making the server use the given\\n103: \\t// listener instead of a buffered conn.\\n104: \\tListenerBuilder func() (net.Listener, error)\\n105: \\n106: \\t// Time to debounce\\n107: \\t// By default, set to 0s to speed up tests\\n108: \\tDebounceTime time.Duration\\n109: \\n110: \\t// EnableFakeXDSUpdater will use a XDSUpdater that can be used to watch events\\n111: \\tEnableFakeXDSUpdater       bool\\n112: \\tDisableSecretAuthorization bool\\n113: \\tServices                   []*model.Service\\n114: \\tGateways                   []model.NetworkGateway\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: type FakeDiscoveryServer struct {\\n118: \\t*core.ConfigGenTest\\n119: \\tt              test.Failer\\n120: \\tDiscovery      *xds.DiscoveryServer\\n121: \\tDiscoveryDebug *http.ServeMux\\n122: \\tListener       net.Listener\\n123: \\tBufListener    *bufconn.Listener\\n124: \\tkubeClient     kubelib.Client\\n125: \\tKubeRegistry   *kube.FakeController\\n126: \\tXdsUpdater     model.XDSUpdater\\n127: \\tMemRegistry    *memregistry.ServiceDiscovery\\n128: }\\n129: \\n130: func NewFakeDiscoveryServer(t test.Failer, opts FakeOptions) *FakeDiscoveryServer {\\n131: \\tm := opts.MeshConfig\\n132: \\tif m == nil {\\n133: \\t\\tm = mesh.DefaultMeshConfig()\\n134: \\t}\\n135: \\n136: \\t// Init with a dummy environment, since we have a circular dependency with the env creation.\\n137: \\ts := xds.NewDiscoveryServer(model.NewEnvironment(), map[string]string{}, krt.GlobalDebugHandler)\\n138: \\t// Disable debounce to reduce test times\\n139: \\ts.DebounceOptions.DebounceAfter = opts.DebounceTime\\n140: \\t// Setup time to Now instead of process start to make logs not misleading\\n141: \\ts.DiscoveryStartTime = time.Now()\\n142: \\tt.Cleanup(s.Shutdown)\\n143: \\n144: \\tserviceHandler := func(_, curr *model.Service, _ model.Event) {\\n145: \\t\\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\\n146: \\t\\t\\tFull:           true,\\n147: \\t\\t\\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: string(curr.Hostname), Namespace: curr.Attributes.Namespace}),\\n148: \\t\\t\\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ServiceUpdate),\\n149: \\t\\t}\\n150: \\t\\ts.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\\n151: \\t}\\n152: \\n153: \\tif opts.DefaultClusterName == \\\"\\\" {\\n154: \\t\\topts.DefaultClusterName = constants.DefaultClusterName\\n155: \\t}\\n156: \\tk8sObjects := getKubernetesObjects(t, opts)\\n157: \\tvar defaultKubeClient kubelib.Client\\n158: \\tvar defaultKubeController *kube.FakeController\\n159: \\tvar registries []serviceregistry.Instance\\n160: \\tif opts.NetworksWatcher != nil {\\n161: \\t\\topts.NetworksWatcher.AddNetworksHandler(func() {\\n162: \\t\\t\\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\\n163: \\t\\t\\t\\tFull:   true,\\n164: \\t\\t\\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.NetworksTrigger),\\n165: \\t\\t\\t\\tForced: true,\\n166: \\t\\t\\t})\\n167: \\t\\t})\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\tvar xdsUpdater model.XDSUpdater = s\\n170: \\tif opts.EnableFakeXDSUpdater {\\n171: \\t\\txdsUpdater = xdsfake.NewWithDelegate(s)\\n172: \\t}\\n173: \\tmc := multicluster.NewFakeController()\\n174: \\tcreds := kubesecrets.NewMulticluster(opts.DefaultClusterName, mc)\\n175: \\n176: \\tconfigController := memory.NewController(memory.MakeSkipValidation(collections.PilotGatewayAPI()))\\n177: \\tclientBuilder := opts.KubeClientBuilder\\n178: \\tif clientBuilder == nil {\\n179: \\t\\tclientBuilder = func(objects ...runtime.Object) kubelib.Client {\\n180: \\t\\t\\treturn kubelib.NewFakeClientWithVersion(opts.KubernetesVersion, objects...)\\n181: \\t\\t}\\n182: \\t}\\n183: \\tfor k8sCluster, objs := range k8sObjects {\\n184: \\t\\tclient := clientBuilder(objs...)\\n185: \\t\\tif opts.KubeClientModifier != nil {\\n186: \\t\\t\\topts.KubeClientModifier(client)\\n187: \\t\\t}\\n188: \\t\\tk8s, _ := kube.NewFakeControllerWithOptions(t, kube.FakeControllerOptions{\\n189: \\t\\t\\tServiceHandler:  serviceHandler,\\n190: \\t\\t\\tClient:          client,\\n191: \\t\\t\\tClusterID:       k8sCluster,\\n192: \\t\\t\\tDomainSuffix:    \\\"cluster.local\\\",\\n193: \\t\\t\\tXDSUpdater:      xdsUpdater,\\n194: \\t\\t\\tNetworksWatcher: opts.NetworksWatcher,\\n195: \\t\\t\\tSkipRun:         true,\\n196: \\t\\t\\tConfigCluster:   k8sCluster == opts.DefaultClusterName,\\n197: \\t\\t\\tMeshWatcher:     meshwatcher.NewTestWatcher(m),\\n198: \\t\\t\\tCRDs: []schema.GroupVersionResource{\\n199: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Install all CRDs used (mostly in Ambient)\\n200: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.AuthorizationPolicy,\\n201: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.PeerAuthentication,\\n202: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.WorkloadEntry,\\n203: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.GatewayClass,\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.KubernetesGateway,\\n205: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.HTTPRoute,\\n206: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.GRPCRoute,\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.TCPRoute,\\n208: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.TLSRoute,\\n209: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.ReferenceGrant,\\n210: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.ServiceEntry,\\n211: \\t\\t\\t},\\n212: \\t\\t})\\n213: \\t\\tstop := test.NewStop(t)\\n214: \\t\\t// start default client informers after creating ingress/secret controllers\\n215: \\t\\tif defaultKubeClient == nil || k8sCluster == opts.DefaultClusterName {\\n216: \\t\\t\\tdefaultKubeClient = client\\n217: \\t\\t\\tif opts.DisableSecretAuthorization {\\n218: \\t\\t\\t\\tDisableAuthorizationForSecret(defaultKubeClient.Kube().(*fake.Clientset))\\n219: \\t\\t\\t}\\n220: \\t\\t\\tdefaultKubeController = k8s\\n221: \\t\\t} else {\\n222: \\t\\t\\tclient.RunAndWait(stop)\\n223: \\t\\t}\\n224: \\t\\tregistries = append(registries, k8s)\\n225: \\t\\tmc.Add(k8sCluster, client, stop)\\n226: \\t}\\n227: \\n228: \\tstop := test.NewStop(t)\\n229: \\tingr := ingress.NewController(defaultKubeClient, meshwatcher.NewTestWatcher(m), kube.Options{\\n230: \\t\\tDomainSuffix: \\\"cluster.local\\\",\\n231: \\t}, xdsUpdater)\\n232: \\tdefaultKubeClient.RunAndWait(stop)\\n233: \\n234: \\tvar gwc *gateway.Controller\\n235: \\tcg := core.NewConfigGenTest(t, core.TestOptions{\\n236: \\t\\tConfigs:             opts.Configs,\\n237: \\t\\tConfigString:        opts.ConfigString,\\n238: \\t\\tConfigTemplateInput: opts.ConfigTemplateInput,\\n239: \\t\\tConfigController:    configController,\\n240: \\t\\tMeshConfig:          m,\\n241: \\t\\tXDSUpdater:          xdsUpdater,\\n242: \\t\\tNetworksWatcher:     opts.NetworksWatcher,\\n243: \\t\\tServiceRegistries:   registries,\\n244: \\t\\tConfigStoreCaches:   []model.ConfigStoreController{ingr},\\n245: \\t\\tCreateConfigStore: func(c model.ConfigStoreController) model.ConfigStoreController {\\n246: \\t\\t\\tg := gateway.NewController(defaultKubeClient, func(class schema.GroupVersionResource, stop <-chan struct{}) bool {\\n247: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n248: \\t\\t\\t}, kube.Options{\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\tDomainSuffix: \\\"cluster.local\\\",\\n250: \\t\\t\\t}, xdsUpdater)\\n251: \\t\\t\\tgwc = g\\n252: \\t\\t\\treturn gwc\\n253: \\t\\t},\\n254: \\t\\tSkipRun:   true,\\n255: \\t\\tClusterID: opts.DefaultClusterName,\\n256: \\t\\tServices:  opts.Services,\\n257: \\t\\tGateways:  opts.Gateways,\\n258: \\t})\\n259: \\tcg.Registry.AppendServiceHandler(serviceHandler)\\n260: \\ts.Env = cg.Env()\\n261: \\ts.Env.GatewayAPIController = gwc\\n262: \\tif err := s.Env.InitNetworksManager(s); err != nil {\\n263: \\t\\tt.Fatal(err)\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\tbootstrap.InitGenerators(s, core.NewConfigGenerator(s.Cache), \\\"istio-system\\\", \\\"\\\", nil)\\n267: \\ts.Generators[v3.SecretType] = xds.NewSecretGen(creds, s.Cache, opts.DefaultClusterName, nil)\\n268: \\ts.Generators[v3.ExtensionConfigurationType].(*xds.EcdsGenerator).SetCredController(creds)\\n269: \\n270: \\tdebugMux := s.InitDebug(http.NewServeMux(), false, func() map[string]string {\\n271: \\t\\treturn nil\\n272: \\t})\\n273: \\n274: \\tmemRegistry := cg.MemRegistry\\n275: \\tmemRegistry.XdsUpdater = s\\n276: \\n277: \\t// Setup config handlers\\n278: \\t// TODO code re-use from server.go\\n279: \\tconfigHandler := func(_, curr config.Config, event model.Event) {\\n280: \\t\\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\\n281: \\t\\t\\tFull:           true,\\n282: \\t\\t\\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: gvk.MustToKind(curr.GroupVersionKind), Name: curr.Name, Namespace: curr.Namespace}),\\n283: \\t\\t\\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ConfigUpdate),\\n284: \\t\\t}\\n285: \\t\\ts.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\\n286: \\t}\\n287: \\tschemas := collections.Pilot.All()\\n288: \\tif features.EnableGatewayAPI {\\n289: \\t\\tschemas = collections.PilotGatewayAPI().All()\\n290: \\t}\\n291: \\tfor _, schema := range schemas {\\n292: \\t\\t// This resource type was handled in external/servicediscovery.go, no need to rehandle here.\\n293: \\t\\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.ServiceEntry {\\n294: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n295: \\t\\t}\\n296: \\t\\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.WorkloadEntry {\\n297: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n298: \\t\\t}\\n299: \\n300: \\t\\tcg.Store().RegisterEventHandler(schema.GroupVersionKind(), configHandler)\\n301: \\t}\\n302: \\tfor _, registry := range registries {\\n303: \\t\\tk8s, ok := registry.(*kube.FakeController)\\n304: \\t\\t// this closely matches what we do in serviceregistry/kube/controller/multicluster.go\\n305: \\t\\tif !ok || k8s.Cluster() != cg.ServiceEntryRegistry.Cluster() {\\n306: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n307: \\t\\t}\\n308: \\t\\tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.AppendWorkloadHandler(k8s.WorkloadInstanceHandler)\\n309: \\t\\tk8s.AppendWorkloadHandler(cg.ServiceEntryRegistry.WorkloadInstanceHandler)\\n310: \\t}\\n311: \\ts.WorkloadEntryController = autoregistration.NewController(cg.Store(), \\\"test\\\", keepalive.Infinity)\\n312: \\n313: \\tvar listener net.Listener\\n314: \\tif opts.ListenerBuilder != nil {\\n315: \\t\\tvar err error\\n316: \\t\\tif listener, err = opts.ListenerBuilder(); err != nil {\\n317: \\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(err)\\n318: \\t\\t}\\n319: \\t} else {\\n320: \\t\\t// Start in memory gRPC listener\\n321: \\t\\tbuffer := 1024 * 1024\\n322: \\t\\tlistener = bufconn.Listen(buffer)\\n323: \\t}\\n324: \\n325: \\tgrpcServer := grpc.NewServer()\\n326: \\ts.Register(grpcServer)\\n327: \\tgo func() {\\n328: \\t\\tif err := grpcServer.Serve(listener); err != nil && !(err == grpc.ErrServerStopped || err.Error() == \\\"closed\\\") {\\n329: \\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(err)\\n330: \\t\\t}\\n331: \\t}()\\n332: \\tt.Cleanup(func() {\\n333: \\t\\tgrpcServer.Stop()\\n334: \\t\\t_ = listener.Close()\\n335: \\t})\\n336: \\t// Start the discovery server\\n337: \\ts.Start(stop)\\n338: \\tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.XdsUpdater = s\\n339: \\t// Now that handlers are added, get everything started\\n340: \\tcg.Run()\\n341: \\tkubelib.WaitForCacheSync(\\\"fake\\\", stop,\\n342: \\t\\tcg.Registry.HasSynced,\\n343: \\t\\tcg.Store().HasSynced)\\n344: \\tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.ResyncEDS()\\n345: \\n346: \\t// Send an update. This ensures that even if there are no configs provided, the push context is\\n347: \\t// initialized.\\n348: \\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Forced: true})\\n349: \\n350: \\t// Wait until initial updates are committed\\n351: \\tc := s.InboundUpdates.Load()\\n352: \\tretry.UntilOrFail(t, func() bool {\\n353: \\t\\treturn s.CommittedUpdates.Load() >= c\\n354: \\t}, retry.Delay(time.Millisecond))\\n355: \\n356: \\t// Mark ourselves ready\\n357: \\ts.CachesSynced()\\n358: \\n359: \\tbufListener, _ := listener.(*bufconn.Listener)\\n360: \\tfake := &FakeDiscoveryServer{\\n361: \\t\\tt:              t,\\n362: \\t\\tDiscovery:      s,\\n363: \\t\\tDiscoveryDebug: debugMux,\\n364: \\t\\tListener:       listener,\\n365: \\t\\tBufListener:    bufListener,\\n366: \\t\\tConfigGenTest:  cg,\\n367: \\t\\tkubeClient:     defaultKubeClient,\\n368: \\t\\tKubeRegistry:   defaultKubeController,\\n369: \\t\\tXdsUpdater:     xdsUpdater,\\n370: \\t\\tMemRegistry:    memRegistry,\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\n373: \\treturn fake\\n374: }\\n375: \\n376: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) KubeClient() kubelib.Client {\\n377: \\treturn f.kubeClient\\n378: }\\n379: \\n380: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) PushContext() *model.PushContext {\\n381: \\treturn f.Env().PushContext()\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: // ConnectADS starts an ADS connection to the server. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\\n385: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectADS() *xds.AdsTest {\\n386: \\tconn, err := grpc.Dial(\\\"buffcon\\\",\\n387: \\t\\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\\n388: \\t\\tgrpc.WithBlock(),\\n389: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\\n390: \\t\\t\\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\\n391: \\t\\t}))\\n392: \\tif err != nil {\\n393: \\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"failed to connect: %v\\\", err)\\n394: \\t}\\n395: \\treturn xds.NewAdsTest(f.t, conn)\\n396: }\\n397: \\n398: // ConnectDeltaADS starts a Delta ADS connection to the server. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\\n399: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectDeltaADS() *xds.DeltaAdsTest {\\n400: \\tconn, err := grpc.Dial(\\\"buffcon\\\",\\n401: \\t\\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\\n402: \\t\\tgrpc.WithBlock(),\\n403: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\\n404: \\t\\t\\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\\n405: \\t\\t}))\\n406: \\tif err != nil {\\n407: \\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"failed to connect: %v\\\", err)\\n408: \\t}\\n409: \\treturn xds.NewDeltaAdsTest(f.t, conn)\\n410: }\\n411: \\n412: func APIWatches() []string {\\n413: \\twatches := []string{gvk.MeshConfig.String()}\\n414: \\tfor _, sch := range collections.Pilot.All() {\\n415: \\t\\twatches = append(watches, sch.GroupVersionKind().String())\\n416: \\t}\\n417: \\treturn watches\\n418: }\\n419: \\n420: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectUnstarted(p *model.Proxy, watch []string) *adsc.ADSC {\\n421: \\tf.t.Helper()\\n422: \\tp = f.SetupProxy(p)\\n423: \\tinitialWatch := []*discovery.DiscoveryRequest{}\\n424: \\tfor _, typeURL := range watch {\\n425: \\t\\tinitialWatch = append(initialWatch, &discovery.DiscoveryRequest{TypeUrl: typeURL})\\n426: \\t}\\n427: \\topts := []grpc.DialOption{grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials())}\\n428: \\tif f.BufListener != nil {\\n429: \\t\\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\\n430: \\t\\t\\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\\n431: \\t\\t}))\\n432: \\t}\\n433: \\tadscConn, err := adsc.New(f.Listener.Addr().String(), &adsc.ADSConfig{\\n434: \\t\\tConfig: adsc.Config{\\n435: \\t\\t\\tIP:        p.IPAddresses[0],\\n436: \\t\\t\\tNodeType:  p.Type,\\n437: \\t\\t\\tMeta:      p.Metadata.ToStruct(),\\n438: \\t\\t\\tLocality:  p.Locality,\\n439: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: p.ConfigNamespace,\\n440: \\t\\t\\tGrpcOpts:  opts,\\n441: \\t\\t},\\n442: \\t\\tInitialDiscoveryRequests: initialWatch,\\n443: \\t})\\n444: \\tif err != nil {\\n445: \\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"Error connecting: %v\\\", err)\\n446: \\t}\\n447: \\tf.t.Cleanup(func() {\\n448: \\t\\tadscConn.Close()\\n449: \\t})\\n450: \\treturn adscConn\\n451: }\\n452: \\n453: // Connect starts an ADS connection to the server using adsc. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\\n454: // watch can be configured to determine the resources to watch initially, and wait can be configured to determine what\\n455: // resources we should initially wait for.\\n456: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) Connect(p *model.Proxy, watch []string, wait []string) *adsc.ADSC {\\n457: \\tf.t.Helper()\\n458: \\tif watch == nil {\\n459: \\t\\twatch = []string{v3.ClusterType}\\n460: \\t}\\n461: \\tadscConn := f.ConnectUnstarted(p, watch)\\n462: \\tif err := adscConn.Run(); err != nil {\\n463: \\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"ADSC: failed running: %v\\\", err)\\n464: \\t}\\n465: \\tif len(wait) > 0 {\\n466: \\t\\t_, err := adscConn.Wait(10*time.Second, wait...)\\n467: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n468: \\t\\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"Error getting initial for %v config: %v\\\", wait, err)\\n469: \\t\\t}\\n470: \\t}\\n471: \\treturn adscConn\\n472: }\\n473: \\n474: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) Endpoints(p *model.Proxy) []*endpoint.ClusterLoadAssignment {\\n475: \\tloadAssignments := make([]*endpoint.ClusterLoadAssignment, 0)\\n476: \\tfor _, c := range xdstest.ExtractEdsClusterNames(f.Clusters(p)) {\\n477: \\t\\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(c, p, f.PushContext())\\n478: \\t\\tloadAssignments = append(loadAssignments, builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(f.Discovery.Env.EndpointIndex))\\n479: \\t}\\n480: \\treturn loadAssignments\\n481: }\\n482: \\n483: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) T() test.Failer {\\n484: \\treturn f.t\\n485: }\\n486: \\n487: // EnsureSynced checks that all ConfigUpdates sent have been established\\n488: // This does NOT ensure that the change has been sent to all proxies; only that PushContext is updated\\n489: // Typically, if trying to ensure changes are sent, its better to wait for the push event.\\n490: \\n491: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) EnsureSynced(t test.Failer) {\\n492: \\tc := f.Discovery.InboundUpdates.Load()\\n493: \\tretry.UntilOrFail(t, func() bool {\\n494: \\t\\treturn f.Discovery.CommittedUpdates.Load() >= c\\n495: \\t}, retry.Delay(time.Millisecond))\\n496: }\\n497: \\n498: func getKubernetesObjects(t test.Failer, opts FakeOptions) map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object {\\n499: \\tobjects := map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object{}\\n500: \\n501: \\tif len(opts.KubernetesObjects) > 0 {\\n502: \\t\\tobjects[opts.DefaultClusterName] = append(objects[opts.DefaultClusterName], opts.KubernetesObjects...)\\n503: \\t}\\n504: \\tif len(opts.KubernetesObjectString) > 0 {\\n505: \\t\\tparsed, err := kubernetesObjectsFromString(opts.KubernetesObjectString)\\n506: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n507: \\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed parsing KubernetesObjectString: %v\\\", err)\\n508: \\t\\t}\\n509: \\t\\tobjects[opts.DefaultClusterName] = append(objects[opts.DefaultClusterName], parsed...)\\n510: \\t}\\n511: \\tfor k8sCluster, objectStr := range opts.KubernetesObjectStringByCluster {\\n512: \\t\\tparsed, err := kubernetesObjectsFromString(objectStr)\\n513: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n514: \\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed parsing KubernetesObjectStringByCluster for %s: %v\\\", k8sCluster, err)\\n515: \\t\\t}\\n516: \\t\\tobjects[k8sCluster] = append(objects[k8sCluster], parsed...)\\n517: \\t}\\n518: \\tfor k8sCluster, clusterObjs := range opts.KubernetesObjectsByCluster {\\n519: \\t\\tobjects[k8sCluster] = append(objects[k8sCluster], clusterObjs...)\\n520: \\t}\\n521: \\n522: \\tif len(objects) == 0 {\\n523: \\t\\treturn map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object{opts.DefaultClusterName: {}}\\n524: \\t}\\n525: \\n526: \\treturn objects\\n527: }\\n528: \\n529: func kubernetesObjectsFromString(s string) ([]runtime.Object, error) {\\n530: \\tvar objects []runtime.Object\\n531: \\tdecode := kubelib.IstioCodec.UniversalDeserializer().Decode\\n532: \\tobjectStrs := strings.Split(s, \\\"\\\\n---\\\\n\\\")\\n533: \\tfor _, s := range objectStrs {\\n534: \\t\\tif len(strings.TrimSpace(s)) == 0 {\\n535: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n536: \\t\\t}\\n537: \\t\\to, _, err := decode([]byte(s), nil, nil)\\n538: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n539: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed deserializing kubernetes object: %v (%v)\\\", err, s)\\n540: \\t\\t}\\n541: \\t\\tobjects = append(objects, o)\\n542: \\t}\\n543: \\treturn objects, nil\\n544: }\\n545: \\n546: // DisableAuthorizationForSecret makes the authorization check always pass. Should be used only for tests.\\n547: func DisableAuthorizationForSecret(fake *fake.Clientset) {\\n548: \\tfake.Fake.PrependReactor(\\\"create\\\", \\\"subjectaccessreviews\\\", func(action k8stesting.Action) (bool, runtime.Object, error) {\\n549: \\t\\treturn true, &authorizationv1.SubjectAccessReview{\\n550: \\t\\t\\tStatus: authorizationv1.SubjectAccessReviewStatus{\\n551: \\t\\t\\t\\tAllowed: true,\\n552: \\t\\t\\t},\\n553: \\t\\t}, nil\\n554: \\t})\\n555: }\\n556: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/test/xds/fake.go\"}"

the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package bootstrap\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"crypto/tls\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"crypto/x509\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"net\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"net/netip\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"os\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"sync/atomic\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n31: \\n32: \\t\\\"github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify\\\"\\n33: \\tgrpcprom \\\"github.com/grpc-ecosystem/go-grpc-prometheus\\\"\\n34: \\t\\\"golang.org/x/net/http2\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/reflection\\\"\\n38: \\tcorev1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/rest\\\"\\n40: \\n41: \\tmeshconfig \\\"istio.io/api/mesh/v1alpha1\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"istio.io/api/security/v1beta1\\\"\\n43: \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/credentials/kube\\\"\\n46: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n47: \\tistiogrpc \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc\\\"\\n48:
